### Shijin13 — 2012-10-01T15:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swedish-chef-serves-food-on-the-edge-of-rotting.4030/post-55558>

Agree that Noakes in on the right track.

as to why are people choosing junk carbs over cheap meat sources - how much is related to programming in the form of commercials etc, being pushed by the major food mfrs? and how much is related to the fact - that those same individuals - haven't seen the meat prepared b/c their grandparents prepared it, and never taught their children how to make it (b/c they were boys and didn't have girls etc - a lot of what's passed down generationally regarding foods is passed down grandmother - mother - daughter, I think only recently have you seen that branch out to include sons)

look at the US. if you're on Food stamps - the foods you can buy are limited mostly to "Junk" healthy whole grains, rice, pasta, & beans, only recently have veggies/fruits been included, and you don't get a whole lot of meat options, that are reasonably priced. also most people who use food stamps don't have vehicles to get to the nearest grocery stores and end up using convenience stores such as 7-11 - which just offers crap.

could the same thing be occurring in S.Africa - my cousin who was in Lesotho for close to 2yrs, said good quality foods were not available. And my other cousin who's been in Kenya for the past 6mths working w/the CDC and UNICEF studying AIDS prevention - has said meat is very limited, and there's very few people who eat the traditional foods any more. most of the stuff in the stores - are US made products.

those of us who eat out side the box - will be better off in the future should a food shortage occur - b/c we're not opposed to eating those things considered not fit for consumption, or its considered inhumane (horse meat) either way, those of us who eat outside the box, are better off in the future.

### Michael — 2012-10-01T16:13:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swedish-chef-serves-food-on-the-edge-of-rotting.4030/post-55560>

All that sounds very likely.

I guess if you uproot and move to a city for work there may at the time be little other than "mealies". After a couple of generations if there are these kidneys available for pennies - possibly because the whites no longer will buy them - well, by then you've forgotten they were ever in the diet. You eat what you eat out of habit.

Foreign aid is another interesting one. Joel Salatin seems to think that the current purpose of much of it is to undermine local food-producers. Not an unintended side-effect but a purpose. Again, this would be big business having the ear of government in the US and Europe - or dropping something in the pockets of people in government.

### SeaHorse — 2012-10-01T16:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/swedish-chef-serves-food-on-the-edge-of-rotting.4030/post-55564>

> > **Jack Kruse;56704 wrote:** soon you will find out why

I read the article you posted "How to stop following the Herd"...interesting in light of this discussion about food norms. I feel there are maybe two elements to my tastes changing from cooked to raw...one comes from watching, listening and reading on the forum. I'm seeing pictures, hearing stories, reading Inger's posts (and many other pioneers) and all of this is patterning and normalizing something that wasn't part of my thinking before. So repetition and modelling both are changing my brain. And then there are the physiological changes that have happened due to changes in how I've been living....so my own brain is now CAPABLE of making different choices and is starting to crave different nutrition.

The "Herd" article makes the point that decisions people make can come from "public" or "private" information. The first human to break open a lobster on the rocks was pursuing a private experiment...once they had done that, there was public information about what happens when you bash a lobster. Other factors start to play a part in someone's decision making (following, modelling,etc). Suddenly cracking open lobsters is a movement and you've got a social cascade.

Flash forward to today. We are living in a world where many of the social movements and norms are profoundly damaging to us as individuals and as a species. We have to get back to our N=1 experiments and the Forum is providing the "public" information to help us in our private explorations to health....we've got to get back to the lobsters, each of us on our own.

### x_danny_x — 2012-09-30T21:56:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-55466>

My Eczema left me with some scars at my feet and lower leg.

my hand and face got affected but they seem to cleared. does anyone know any remedies or anything that can help me get a handle at this. these meds for eczema is not cheap and laser treatment was mentioned by my dermotologist to get rid of them which is expensive. laser treatment though as well doesnt get rid of eczema permantly.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-09-30T22:39:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-55473>

Selenium and several of the other Brain specific nutrients are likely off........read BG 5 again, use some coconut oil on your skin and consider some magnesium oil in the skin too.

### x_danny_x — 2012-09-30T22:48:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-55476>

will check out Selenium and avoid it.

### BJK77 — 2012-09-30T23:27:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-55480>

> **x\_danny\_x;56510 wrote:** been using coconut oil and magnesium oil. will read BG 5 again for info that i missed.
>
>
>
> will check out Selenium and avoid it.

Mg and coconut oil worked well on DH's eczema as did increasing seafood. I don't think you want to avoid selenium. I think Dr K would say getting selenium and other brain specific nutrients from the epi-paleo diet is what you need to focus on. As I said, increasing seafood has made a huge difference for DH with his eczema/psoriasis issues. I didn't have skin problems per se, BUT my skin has definitely improved on the epi-paleo diet.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-01T00:02:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-55481>

Selenium underpins IBD especially crohn's This is why Crohn's highest incidence and prevalence are found in NZ and Aussie because the continent is devoid of Se in the ground. They need seafood to get it.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-09T00:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-56982>

Hi x\_danny\_x,

My dad had the worst case of eczema I have even seen in my life. He looked like a boiled lobster. Over 90% of his body was covered with the eczema. He tried everything. Spent a fortune on creams. It just made it worst. In the end, he did some sort of light treatment. It took only 2-3 minutes per treatment and he did about 15 treatments. It completely went away. He has a tiny spot on one hand. I don't know if you have tried this type of treatment but it worked for my dad.

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-09T01:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-56989>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-56996>

> **x\_danny\_x;58291 wrote:** is that laser therapy you are talking about caroline?

### x_danny_x — 2012-10-10T03:50:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-57233>

How did he get this treatment? Did he buy some light treating equipment for this? If so, where can I buy such a device?

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-10-12T03:09:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-57682>

No, it was done by doctor's prescription. I just talked my dad and he said it was UVB treatment. The doctors thought he had eczema for years. Finally they did this treatment for psoriasis. (By the way, his "psoriasis" looked like "eczema".) The treatment worked great. Of course, it might not work for you:

[http://www.psoriasis.org/about-psoriasis/treatments/phototherapy](http://http://www.psoriasis.org/about-psoriasis/treatments/phototherapy)

One other treatment I have found that works great for skin problems that will not heal over the long term is shea butter. (My husband had a skin irritation on the bottom of one foot that would not heal. Likely a chemical sensitivity to epoxy resin. He'd spend a fortune on prescription cortico-steriod creams. Finally, he tried my shea butter and the wound finally healed up.) Shea is very thick and soothing for sore skin. Here is where I get mine:

[http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/butter/shea.php](http://http://www.mountainroseherbs.com/learn/butter/shea.php)

### Gracelind — 2012-10-12T06:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-57702>

I can second that Mountain Rose Herbs is a wonderful online source for herbs, essential oils, teas, and healing oils and butters.

There are some testimonials at Chagrin Valley Soap that folks with eczema found healing with some of the different hand-made soaps. A search for eczema on [this page](http://http://www.chagrinvalleysoapandsalve.com/ingredients/baseoils.aspx) will highlight specific oils and other ingredients that have been found to improve eczema.

You can buy sample sizes to try. The whole bars last a very long time if you just wet your hands to soap up and don't keep the bar in the shower. No financial interest, just nearly 10 years of using Ida's soaps and salves.

### fitness@home — 2012-10-12T12:49:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-57721>

I really love the Chagrin Valley soaps and shampoo bars. Don't have to deal with eczema but like Gracelind said, go read the customer testimonials on the website. Great customer service...And I love that you can order sample sizes of the soaps to try.

My favorite shampoo bar is Summer Sunshine - need to place an order soon! Also use their Whipped Squalane Face & Eye Mousse at bedtime (just added one pump of Twist DHEA cream to it as my own little experiment).

### indigogirl — 2012-10-12T16:28:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-eczema.4035/post-57745>

I have had psoriasis on my elbow on and off ( flares, calms down) for years. I've used a myriad of prescription cortisone creams to treat the flares. I've just started treating it with topical iodine and it is drying out and is not itchy. I've only been doing this for 2 days, but so far so good. I was using the Lugols, but it stains my skin until I absorb it. I am now using clear iodine.

I haven't read this whole thread so someone might have mentioned this: Dr Kruse told me about a year ago (I think in response to my question on one of his blogs) to take NAC and 1000 mg vitamin C 3X a day for psoriasis...that's what he told ME, not sure if its general info, but it was not in a consult so he didn't have my labs, background, etc.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-01T03:01:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-hudson-valley-ny.4041/post-55512>

Hi

I have been lurking for a bit. I read and re-read trying to grasp as much as possible. I have hormonal and weight issues. I could gain weight if I didn't eat for a week.... It's heart breaking to try and try and see no results.

I have had a long history on trying to solve digestive issues. My conclusion is a wheat/gluten issue then finding out corn is out as well. I took out all grains. I'm ok with that.

The gyno told me I am peri-menopausal at 42. Ok one more thing to add to the pile of crap to deal with.

I just got my cold fat burner that I will use tonight. I am eating as much seafood as possible. I'm trying to get my supplements lined up.

I feel like this is the right path for me.

One funny thing.... Last November I had to go to survival school in the military. For a week we go into the woods 50 miles from the Canadian border in Washington. I froze my ass off. Since that time I can NOT get cold. It's starting to get cooler here. I'm still in t-shirts shorts and flip flops while everyone else is bundled up.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-07T11:36:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-beginning-to-the-road-to-optimal.4044/post-69738>

Have you made your resolutions urgent yet? Things may come to those who wait...but only the things left by those who hustle. The opposite of complacency, begins with an urge; your inner drive, where determination and desire serves as your catalyst for change. The most important things in life are only accomplished if we have a sense of urgency in place. Don't wait any longer. Be found working for the new you and and creating your new masterpiece. Inspiration exists, but it has to find you working towards it.
When you understand your purpose; you want to be in that place, wherever that place is, to do what you are called to do. But it is not just about you; it is about the people that are waiting to receive the gift of your calling in order for their lives to be changed.

When I think of urgency I think of timing. Time is our most valuable asset because it is not promised. If you are called to do something and you let the circumstances of life, fears, lack of faith will be there to help you, passiveness and self-doubt and lack of confidence take over; then someone else willing to do it will get it done. In terms of your own health........no one is better than you to help yourself unless that fire, that inner urgency is lit right now. Go get a match and light the pilot light now!

# Thread: Anxiety Now! and Optimal Living
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2012-10-01T17:44:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55581>

I have been suffering from chronic migraines with dizziness since May 2011. In July 2011 my father passed after being hospitalized for 2 months, my mother was placed in a memory care facility for dementia shortly thereafter and we had some bad real estate investments force us into bankruptcy early this year. That said, my marriage is good, my job is good, finances are recovering and I love where I live because I get 300+ days of sunshine.

OK, now that all THAT is over I figured I would start to recover. Anxiety has taken hold and it is no doubt affecting my health. I have anti-depressants in hand (my PCP has been trying to get me to take something for a year now), but I am trying desperately to control this myself and get back to feeling 'normal'. It will not go away. I shake, I vibrate, my mind spins and spins, my chest is tight and my stomach is always in knots.

I do guided meditation and breathing. I cannot exercise right now because I get dizzy (presumably from the migraine issue). I was exercising (fast walking, weight lifting) up until about 2 months ago when the dizziness was just too much to bear. It was making me MORE nervous.

I am in process of getting my labs for my consult. Bloodwork is done (my cholesterol is through the roof, but it's always been high, and apparently my hormones are in the toilet), waiting for OK from insurance for GI and ASI. That should come in a couple of days. But in the meantime, I am seriously considering starting some small dose of anti-anxiety meds. I am sure the stress I am feeling is doing damage to my system. I am in a vicious circle and am not sure how much longer I can take sitting here shaking before I really crack.

I am wondering if trying to get this anxiety under control medically would affect my ability to control my anxiety through this optimal living program. My husband and I have started to follow the Paleo Diet to get started.

Just FYI - I have had panic attacks and an anxiety disorder all my life. I have good period and bad periods. This is one of the longest bad periods I have had. I moved to AZ in November of 2009 and believe it or not, had no anxiety issues at all until May of 2011. You would have thought that a cross-country move would have sunk me - but I was great!

Thoughts and opinions?

Karen from Arizona

### ealachan — 2012-10-01T18:14:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55587>

I had major depression and anxiety issues starting in 2005. I tried several different medications, none of which made me feel any better, really...they just put me in a different state of Not Good, if you see what I mean. A combination of therapy, learning how to meditate, and changing my diet all helped me a lot, as did starting to supplement vitamin D3 and fish oil. I'm off the fish oil now because I eat actual FISH now, but those mega-doses of D3 and fish oil I started taking in the middle of last summer, in combination with cutting carbs out almost completely, really helped snap me out of my anxiety cycle. Last winter was the first winter in 6 years that I went completely unmedicated for depression, and the first winter in probably 10 years where I didn't have any panic attacks at all.

CT has also helped my depression and anxiety, I believe. When you're getting into a really cold tub, shower or lake, it's kind of impossible to be sad / angry / upset, I think. You may not exactly be happy, but you become so focused on "OMG COLD!!" that it kind of blocks everything else out. At least, that's what it often does for me. Other times it just makes me downright giddy. I stand in the shower with the water turned all the way cold and just giggle to myself. It's fairly easy to see how absurd life really is in a cold shower, I think.

### shah78 — 2012-10-01T18:25:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55589>

The migraine issue and the anxiety issue are just two symptoms of the same problem. they both "stem" from a misaligned axis and atlas bone as well as misaligned cranial bones. I get the anxiety-depression-headache syndrom frequently after lifting weights incorrectly ,( or falling, or extended neck yoga poses). I get some neuromuscular therapy on my neck/cranium and usually within minutes(sometimes hours,or atleast by the next morning so I can go back into the gym to hurt myself again !) I feel no anxiety. Let me repeat, the migraines and the anxiety are THE SAME ISSUE. This can be fixed by a well trained, but run of the mill certified Neuromuscular Therapist. As the saying goes, it is neither brain surgery nor rocket science. And it doesn't cost as much either. Good luck

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-01T18:39:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55590>

> **shah78;56743 wrote:** The migraine issue and the anxiety issue are just two symptoms of the same problem. they both "stem" from a misaligned axis and atlas bone as well as misaligned cranial bones.

This is not 100% true. I've had my atlas and axis aligned for the past 6yrs, and never had the migraines go away completely. However, once I started addressing the upside down Pg:E2 ratio with BHRT, my migraines started to diminish. They're not gone yet, but on their way. since starting BHRT, my atlas hasn't been out. - concidence? I think not. 100% hormonal driven. Prior to starting BHRT, I had to go to my chiropractor at least 3-4x/month to control the migraines, b/c my atlas was out, this always occured during hormonal surges within my cycle. This may not be true for everyone - but if you have migraines, you seriously need to look at what your Pg:E2 ratio looks like.

my N=1. ymmv

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-01T18:53:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55595>

BAB to re-joint the NT gates to your brain and in your kidneys to refill your neurotransmitter stores, epi-paleo rx and EPCOT rx to get your inflammation down, probably CT b/c the move to AZ means you're living in a place that's hot if you get that many sunny days in a year. Your living in the survival pathway in a hot place.......... your oxidizing.

I wish I knew what to do about the migraines.

I used to live in Tempe/Scottsdale and it's not a healthy place to be at. I know the sun is nice....... but you're basically cooking yourself. Unless you work in a movie theater.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-01T18:56:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55596>

Interesting, Shijin. I've been going to a chiropractor this year, and he is able to help with headaches. I have recently found that when I start to get a slight headache, I can "lift up" on my head (usually by pushing under the jawbones and skull, or with my fingers intertwined on the back of my head) and the symptoms often go away fairly quickly. Sometimes the headache recurrs several times, though, which means it's time for the ice pack.

As my diet gets cleaner, my headaches (which used to be real thumpers in years past, felt like my head would split open) are an infrequent nuisance only. The headaches improved with Paleo, improved more with the chiropractor, and improved even more with epi-paleo.

Mafixit, I don't mean to belittle your migraines, I don't think I've ever had a real migraine.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-01T19:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55601>

> **Nonchalant;56751 wrote:** Interesting, Shijin. I've been going to a chiropractor this year, and he is able to help with headaches. >
>
>
> Mafixit, I don't mean to belittle your migraines, I don't think I've ever had a real migraine.

Agree that diet is a big factor in some migraines, as is alignment. Grains have the biggest impact on migraines/depression/anxiety ime. but addressing that only didn't find relief, nor did chiropractic adjustments - yes they helped but not to the level they've been touted. even just a small amount of BHRT has kept me in alignment - I've gone in recently when I've had migraines and been in alignment - which further validated that I needed more Pg. I'm still getting them, and I don't think I absorb the hormones as well as other women b/c of my PCOS (high T and all) so we might need to look at other options...

I didn't begin to see real improvement in depression/anxiety until I was off grains/diary for close to 2yrs. I'd also wager that a bad gut is going to play big time in this, as well as the upside down Pg:E2 ratio.

### shah78 — 2012-10-01T19:31:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55606>

Chiropractor atlas/axis techniques are akin to Hershey quality chocolate. If you have to go 3 or 4 times a month each month,you've made my point. A skilled NMT can do it in one try!(once they get to "know" you. ) REALLY! And I absolutely agree that the hormones are "a" key to the migraines and the anxiety. And I agree, get into the cold tub four or five times a day . This will help everything. But metabolic tweeking is not everything. EVEN The chiroprators know that alignment is critical. Use all the tools in the toolbox. Deal with the body (alignment), deal the metabolism( hormones )and deal with the chi (accupucture/tantra/circadian rhythm hygiene). Play basketball with two arms and not with one tied behind your back. Much easier and efficient. That's my definition of "optimal" healing.

### mafixit — 2012-10-01T20:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55616>

> **shah78;56761 wrote:** Chiropractor atlas/axis techniques are akin to Hershey quality chocolate. If you have to go 3 or 4 times a month each month,you've made my point. A skilled NMT can do it in one try!(once they get to "know" you. ) REALLY! And I absolutely agree that the hormones are "a" key to the migraines and the anxiety. And I agree, get into the cold tub four or five times a day . This will help everything. But metabolic tweeking is not everything. EVEN The chiroprators know that alignment is critical. Use all the tools in the toolbox. Deal with the body (alignment), deal the metabolism( hormones )and deal with the chi (accupucture/tantra/circadian rhythm hygiene). Play basketball with two arms and not with one tied behind your back. Much easier and efficient. That's my definition of "optimal" healing.

I am in chiropractic treatment and receiving neuro-cranial integration treatment - my chiropractor is the one who sent me here because she felt there was definitely something else out of balance. I am also received acupuncture once a week and since I started treatment 3 weeks ago, the migraines are down, but I cannot get rid of the dizziness or the anxiety (they are not related - my dizziness is not an anxiety symptom). I spoke with my chiropractor (actually both - the one doing the treatment and the one doing the acupuncture). It seems that misalignment is not my issue.

I moved to AZ because I NEED the sunshine. I had winter depression living in Chicago all my life. I love the heat - it makes me happy - because even with the physical anxiety symptoms I can still feel happy. I used to get depressed when the temp dropped below 75 degrees. My demeanor is so much better since I came here and I try to live life to the fullest out here, and I LOVE the outdoors. Which is why I just don't understand the anxiety thing going on here. I literally wake up every morning between 4 and 4:30 a.m. with anxiety. Sometimes it disappears shortly after I get up and move around, sometimes it stays most of the day. I guess I will be patient and wait until all the results of the labs come back and I get my consult - several doctors have pointed to hormone issues, but their answer was a pill for the headaches/anxiety, not a look at the root cause.

In the meantime, I am icing my carotid arteries and will start CT slowly by cold showers, until I get my consult and it is determined that is what is best for me based on my labs. Paleo diet is started, grains are gone, but on the advise of my chiro I had already started that protocol a couple of months ago. Also I cut out any artificial sweeteners.

Funny thing - my anxiety feels like you feel when you are cold and shiver, so CT can trigger an anxiety attack because my physical reaction to cold mimics anxiety completely.

Hanging on, but it's hard to wait.

Karen from Arizona

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-01T23:46:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55679>

Are you making sure that you're eating a LOT of seafood? If you've started paleo and not made it epi-paleo (seafood-based) your T3 is going to tank. This is going to make your adrenals more stressed, too. Sounds like you've got both going on. You need as much DHA (the fat from seafood) as you can get, as well as the iodine from seafood/seaweeds. Daily, if not more than once a day. You probably also need a fat-heavy meal (or a bullet-proof tea sort of addition to one meal) to up your fats so you can make hormones.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55798>

Just reading the other posts reminds me of why Jack calls it Quilt. So many pieces, so many different variables. AO chiropractic has been the only thing to help with my neck since my car accident. Funny, or maybe not so funny, how my hormonal issues went on overdrive since the car accident as well- but have been calming down since finding my AO chiro. I am finally getting in to see a BHRT doc in a few days, but am wondering if I have been as off as I thought?

I had migraines up until my early thirties, which was about the same time the TOM became horrible- symptoms that I have learned that are indicative of being E dominant. (super heavy/clotty etc).

We are being done such a disservice by docs that dont look at the bigger/whole picture and just want to treat the symptoms- not the root cause.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-02T15:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/anxiety-now-and-optimal-living.4046/post-55803>

Yes there are many pieces to our puzzle. My experience has been that since I have systematically reduced inflammation all the aches and pains have disappeared. I used to go for massage Thai, craniosacral, chiropractor, deep tissue. Since embarking on the circadian/ct/paleo thing ALL of my body complaints have evaporated. I have not been for massage in over six months and curiously feel no need for it. Inflammation is at the source of all these problems. Oxidation causes inflammation. Connect your dots and feel good. Unless you are physically injured pain is caused by inflammation. No inflammation=no pain. Stress can cause inflammation. Look at your life.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-02T00:25:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-with-my-labs-gi-metametrix-blood-work.4048/post-55694>

glucose up think about this from the [Primal cortisol blog](http://http://jackkruse.com/primal-cortisol-response/): Night time artificial light also raises cortisol levels, because our pineal-thyroid circuits have not evolved an off switch for artificial light as yet. Global control of gluconeogenesis is mediated by glucagon. Glucagon is released when our blood glucose is low and is unaffected by the light cycle. Glucagon generally is released from the human GI tract at night when we are sleeping to maintain our blood glucose while we sleep and not feeding.

you know about your high FBG.

# Thread: Bloating after consuming anything (even water). Tried all alternative solutions.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 65
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2012-10-02T00:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55688>

Hi everyone,

Every time I eat no matter what food (even plain room temp water) I bloat out a lot (pregnant looking), Heart beat gets strong (palpitations), and I get tired (although the last 2 have been less so since taking sub-lingual B12).

I am generally fatigued and have had to quit all exercise because of this, constipated and have tons of other smaller symptoms such as a coated tongue, bad breath, poor teeth, eczema, dizziness on standing. Also I get hunger pangs in the middle of the night occasionally reminiscent of an ulcer.

I DO NOT get diarhea, acid reflux, vomiting or nausea.

This all started after I took to many NSAIDs September 2011 and possibly got an ulcer. I took PPIs and anibiotics to get rid of it although the sensation still occasionally returns.

I have tried digestive enzymes and betaine(lots) for low stomach ph, licorice root, slippery elm, cabbage juice, l-glutamine, prebiotics, kefir, sauerkraut, kombucha, aloe vera, parasite cleanse, low carb, no fod-maps, gaps, enemas, leaky gut Rx, etc.

The only thing that seems to help is reducing the volume of food. I figure all food is irritating my gut in some way.

I have tested negative for h pylori, and other blood tests for food intolerances, anemia, etc.

The bloating/distension seems to be not caused by gas. For example I can eat breakfast, bloat out, pass no gas by next meal(6hrs+) and the bloating will have reduced. I also don't feel the pain experienced with gas moving about. I think the bloating is my gut swelling up to protect/fix itself.

I eat 2 keto paleo meals per day according to your leptin Rx which is great by the way, it was the only thing that got me really health (6 pack, lots of energy, good sleep, etc) before I got into my current perdicament. I am 21 and male.

Very desperate for help and find this is ruining my life. Any help massively appreciated.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-02T00:16:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55690>

it sounds like a Gut flora issues.......Metametrix GI fx test would be helpful.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-02T01:25:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55702>

From what you write, the bloating symptoms are recent? So it sounds like something is either worsening or being uncovered? In addition to gut flora, I'm wondering about other issues alongside this. Have you had a hormone panel run? And thyroid testing? The low energy and dizziness seem to indicate crashing T3 and adrenal issues, including cortisol/aldosterone potentially. If you have eliminated carbs you have lost a boost to your T3 but if you're not supporting T3 with seafood/seaweed (DHA, iodine) you will suffer for it. Hunger in the middle of the night potentially smacks of an altered cortisol-melatonin rhythm, so gherlin is being upregulated at the wrong time. Which circles back to the thyroid/adrenal issue. Of course, gut and brain health underpin all of this which comes back to what Dr K said. I'd certainly defer to his judgement over mine!

### kathylu — 2012-10-02T01:50:27+0300

### seanb4 — 2012-10-02T14:07:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55785>

First off thanks for all the quick responses, it is appreciated.

I would love to et some labs done but sadly this is not an option for me (just graduated, loads of debt, no job). I can go to my local GP (live in the UK) but it is hard to convince them to do anything.

I have thought it was a gut issue and have tried prebiotics and probiotics which have no effect on the bloating. Also the lack of gas is confusing, I thought bloating from gut flora issues was caused by overproducing gas but I have almost no gas. The bloating is however directly tied with eating, bloating gradually increases from when I start.

@ kathylu I have tried many things to solve candida but nothing happens. Also I am missing lots of the symptoms and there seems to be no difference going from high carb to low, etc.

@ kiwilauren Interesting theory but I don't understand how NSAIDs/antibiotics would cause a change in T levels, before that I was really healthy thanks to the leptin Rx. I also took iodine for a while and saw no change.

@ Joann thanks. Can't remember which ones but I took them for 2 weeks. I have tried kefir, kombucha, sauerkraut and supplement probiotics over the last year in fairly high amounts, raw sauerkraut 400g per day, litre of kefir per day, etc. I do not think my gut would still be inflamed from the NSAIDs because I stopped taking them October 2011.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-02T21:02:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55901>

I can't be of much help like the above posters, however, when my dh had h. pylori 12 years ago, his stomach was in pain a lot, especially when it was empty. So first thing in the morning would be the worst for him. During the day the pain would dull. After going to the ER and begin told it was anxiety (and given heavy anti-anxiety meds), he eventually tested positive for h. pylori, and was treated with anto-biotics for it, and it really helped. ### Joann — 2012-10-02T22:29:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55934>

fwiw, A friend of mine took so many excedrin for a headaches she wound up with pain and nausea and couldnt eat. went through upper endo, which showed inflammation on the way to ulcer, but not yet. like you she was given ppi's and a diet for several weeks and anti nausea meds. I dont think she took antibiotics though. it resolved.

For me, and this is just fyi......I stopped eating all raw food since it was causing bloating and cramps, no dairy, and watched what did the most symptoms and eliminated them. even water can cause an acid response.

my thinking is the if burning pain .....pepto bismol worth a try. if only bloating, then gut flora is off....which could mean more antibiotics and start from scratch to introduce more "good" bacteria than "bad". then I would cook everything. but that is my thinking from my own experience with this nightmare.

get better soon

### MamaGrok — 2012-10-03T01:37:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-55973>

Welcome to my world! I'm posting so I can read responses. I'm coming to the conclusion that my bloating is not an immediate response to something I ate in the last 24 hrs, but more of a state of an unhappy gut. Yes, it goes up and down with some foods, but it's always there, and sometimes goes up and down in ways that make no sense at all.

It went down last week when I started back on CT & seafood, and is back up this week (although I haven't done serious CT in four days, I've kept up the seafood).

According to my labs, it's not candida or h. pylori. It doesn't seem like gas. I do get some weird pain & runs episodes, though, like last night when I literally - LITERALLY - felt like I was in transition stage of labor for about 30 minutes, except all the pain was in the gut, not the womb. Woke me up with massive pain, wave on wave, building to a peak then receding, 60+s long, gradually moving down, but resulting in ... nothing. Until an hour later!

Yeah, it's crazy confusing stuff.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-03T10:56:34+0300

@Joann Yea your friend seems similar to me. I have played around with my diet enough over the last year to be convinced that it is not diet related, some foods produce bad symptoms but ALL foods produce bloating. You say gut flora is off which I think you might be right but like mamagrok I produce no gas, just bloating. My understanding is bad gut flora produces gas which causes bloating. @villjamur stevenson I have tried a water fast for 2 days but was still bloating upon drinking salt water, this caused me to give up. I was also still constipated at the time.

I had a blood test and breath test for H pylori before any treatment. Both were positive. Then I got a triple treatment for H pylori. It did not work according to a breath test and my symptoms. So they gave me another triple treatment. It did not work so I was sent to a specialist. During the endoscopy they got a sample to see what antibiotics would work. Then, I was on my third treatment so called a quadruple treatment. Then my breath test was finally negative and all my horrible symptoms were finally gone like they never existed.

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-10-04T15:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56205>

> **seanb4;57288 wrote:** Why is that?

Bloating without any type of gas, belching or otherwise, tends to eliminate the GI - not necessarily, but it is suggestive.

However, that fact combined with your fasting results - seems to indicate it is NOT the GI.

Bloating because of excess gas is caused by excess fermentation in the gut. Bugs are not going to go on a feeding frenzy on water or salt water. So the fact that you bloat even on a fast with salt water, leads me to believe it is some sort of fluid retention problem. And the kidney's are the first thing I would suspect, the master regulators of fluids in our body.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-05T00:11:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56339>

> **seanb4;56972 wrote:**
>
> I would love to et some labs done but sadly this is not an option for me (just graduated, loads of debt, no job). I can go to my local GP (live in the UK) but it is hard to convince them to do anything.

I think you need to find a GP to work with you. I, too live in a socialised medicine country and I know what it's like. But that doesn't mean it's impossible. You may not find a GP to test you for everything you want, but convincing them to test for some things isn't always that difficult. I told my doc I had made major dietary/lifestyle changes and wanted to make sure things were looking OK (he ran full thyroid panel (no RT3, but TSH, T3 and T4), full lipid panel, E2, P, DHEA and T, kidney function, liver function, hsCRP, D3, and a few others. Even if you only got some of this accomplished it would help paint a picture. And be very helpful. Given your symptoms, I'd stress you need candida testing too.

This is bloating after consuming anything which doesn't seem to be gas related and doesn't respond to elimination diets.

If your interested I will start a thread on paleo hacks where us 3 can put in our symptoms, things that possibly triggered it, things we've tried, and the results from any tests we've had. Hopefully by doing this we can narrow down the problem. Let me know cause I am literally obsessed with finding out what is wrong with me/us.

Also with the thyroid thing, I noticed my thyroid slowly getting worse from the time I got ill, ie I started getting colder, more fatigued, etc. So it's possible this thing affects the thyroid by damaging the gut and preventing certain T hormones from being converted or something like that. Maybe your thyroid problems started because of this problem?

Also + I had an endoscopy so I think they would have seen an obstruction although I too have had the same thought.

edit: I am trying CT to hopefully rule out the thyroids role in all of this.

You know it's not spine curvature when you go from looking normal to 6mo pg in one day! (me)

I was obsessed with finding the cause & cure, too, until last week when Dr. K said I probably have Hashi's. I was just about to start another 2-3 months of elimination diets to find the cause. Now I'm just drowning in the world of self-pity about Hashi's, and expect to get back to problem-solving soon, although I really have come to the conclusion that the bloating is not directly related to the actual food intake closely enough for me to find the cause.

My thyroid problems actually go way back further than the bloating, but the serious stuff started around the same time. I think the leaky gut / thyroid issues cause the bloating in some way I don't yet (and may never) understand.

kkcarlton on this forum & MDA also has mystery bloating that persists despite water/broth fasting. She & I have both done SCD/GAPS.

I am more incline to think that hashi's is caused by whatever is wrong with us but is not THE cause.

I think your leaky gut + thyroid theory is interesting as I noticed twice in the past year all my symptoms went away when I cut on dairy and then again when I cut out fodmaps. However this was a one trick pony because the next time I would go out drinking all my symptoms would return and cutting out dairy/fodmaps wouldn't affect it. It is almost as if whatever this is adapts to any of my adjustments.

Anyway for me taking nsaids could have caused leaky gut which was then further exacerbated by PPI's and antibiotics. This could have then lead to poor conversion of T hormones in the gut and thus thyroid problems. However eating gaps/scd (done this for months) and taking probiotics, l-glutamine, etc should have then helped heal the gut allowing better conversion of t hormones and showing an improvement in my symptoms. I have found this not to be the case which \*\*\*\*s all over that theory.

I am hoping I can rule out the thyroids role in all of this when I cold adapt as my current understanding is the thyroid stops being used and the brain starts controling thyroid function insted in cold adapted people.

### MamaGrok — 2012-10-06T22:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56625>

I did the metametrix GI Effects test. I have also done rounds of diatomaceous earth, etc.

Eat some almonds & check your stools. If the bits are the same size as when you swallowed them, you're not digesting. I don't digest most nuts, corn, the skins of beans/blueberries/etc., nor does my youngest daughter. Veg maldigestion is tested for simply by checking for veg matter, which you can do yourself if you have the stomach for it. People say that fat malabsorption will give grease floating on the toilet water, but I've never had that.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56664>

Wish someone would post on something that worked.

In my case I know the exact day my gut problems started. after antibiotics. Never to return to what it was previous to antibiotics. After Genova stool test found no pathogenic bacteria, no acidophilus, no bifidus or very low ...even e coli which is suppose to be there was too low.

That told me anti's wiped everything out. Curiously, while taking antibiotics again, my symptoms were gone I could eat. then after return to problems. So I became crazy careful with food. no salad bar, everything cooked. After about 6 months I was able to eat cheese again. If I took anti's again I could not eat cheese or any dairy.

If the anti's wipe out too much then what ever you eat introduces new bacteria and a chance for the wrong ones to take over. Think that is the theory of SIBO......they take meds first, then limit food intake to a special drink.

Strangely, my DHEA is \*not\* low (no, I don't understand why not) and my sleep has been fantastic 99% of my life (only got weird for a few months after I started the LR, then went normal again). Thanks for the recommendation for a pregn. source & bile acids. I did betaine HCl for a long time with no result. Actually, I stopped after a year b/c I thought I had maxed out when I was getting runs. In retrospect, I think that was \*this\* problem and not the HCl. ### bigknitwit — 2012-10-07T07:02:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56678>

I am avidly keeping an eye on this thread.

I have bloating problems that seem to be mostly an issue in the evenings. Dinner is a problem, no matter what the food. If I eat a decent sized dinner, my stomach feels so stuffed/stretched it's very uncomfortable to hours... but it still doesn't turn off the desire to be eating. I have to fight that one with my better judgement. I have actually started wondering about not eating dinner, just to avoid the problem a little better. I'm gong to start taking HCL supplements. Does one take them before, during, or after the meal? I have read during, and I have read 30 minutes after as well. Any recommendations? What about digestive enzymes? Can they be used as well as the hcl? How do I time it all with the food to make everything work how it's supposed to?

I can also pinpoint exactly when things went south for me. I had a very bad (oral) herpes infection, and the sores in my mouth made it such that I could not eat anything, nothing at all without severe pain. I dropped 14 pounds in 10 days. Then I maintained that weight loss (my last pregnancy weight), but started an elimination diet for dd, who was nursing and having crazy food issues. I ate rice, pears, turkey, and finally added in butter, for 6 weeks. The day that diet ended was the day the weight started coming on. Any food that wasn't a fresh meat or non-starchy vegetable = water retention, and lots of it. No gas, no real bloating back then even, just growing out of my clothes at record speeds. My GI bacteria test done this past spring showed low bifido bacteria, but other markers were decent. No candida, no parasites.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-07T12:44:05+0300

@Joann Do you have the bloating without gas too?

@chocolate I have used all those things at one time with no effect.

@bigknitwit You sure the bloating is just in the evening and not whenever you eat. I find that in the morning my bloating is at the lowest, after breakfast it gets quite bad but dies down a little by tea time then is it's worse after tea.

You can use enzymes with hcl, Now Super Enzymes is pretty cheap and works well for some people.

It seems like it probably isn't a parasite/yeast/bacteria issue as others have had them come back clean. Although it may be one that they don't test for/ hasn't been discovered yet...

Does anyone get morning hunger pains/pangs that seem ulcer like and not due to hunger? I still get these even though my endoscopy showed no ulcer or inflammation. What the hell is it !?!

I have a similar thing - lowest bloating in the morning, and then it progresses as the day goes by. What's so annoying for me is that even with the discomfort of it all (the skin around my belly button is sensitive after 4 pregnancies!), my appetite is still strong. I want to be eating, and I especially want to be eating nuts (not for me, AI). My bloating problem has definitely increased this fall, coinciding with my thyroid hormone levels dropping off. But last fall (pre LR, pre JK), I remember having trouble with bloating, especially in the evenings as well. However, I was trying intermittent fasting (eating only after 4:30pm) as a means of weight control, so I was eating a lot...

No morning hunger pains here. Just the unending desire to eat.

Do you eat a BAB? Mine has to be 65g protein or more to stop cravings later in the day. I've tried lowering it (after 6mo at 65g) and it just doesn't work for me at this point.

No sensitive belly skin after 5 babies, but the discomfort of stretching is still very uncomfortable. I've told dh many times it feels like someone is in there pressing out on all sides with all his might.

Dr. K has told me no nuts, not even Brazil nuts, period, b/c of AI.

I used to tell dh that I wished someone would put an air release valve in my belly. This was years ago, before I knew to make any attempt to reduce carbs. ### MamaGrok — 2012-10-08T02:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56835>

I never had cold hands & feet, but the rest is me. I had constipation my ENTIRE LIFE until some time shortly before I hit 30. Some dietary change did it; I don't know which.

BKW; 75-100gm is close to a pound of meat/fish; are you sure? I believe you; just wanted to be sure. I know you're a geek like me and can do math, but sometimes we get brain fog from our ailments.  Are you eating sat fat? No matter how much fish I eat, it doesn't fill me unless I get some sat fat. I usually add 2T CO to wahtever I'm eating, and I usually mix seafood & land meat to get my 65g b/c I just seem to need the animal fat for satiety.

I often look 6mo pg after eating. Bleh.

IDK the logic behind the AID diet, but I do know that nuts are seeds just like grains & other seeds, and \*all\* seeds have a protective seed coat that prevents them from germinating until it's time. It is also intended to help the seed pass through animal's digestive systems so it comes out the other end and can sprout & grow. So we're not \*supposed\* to digest them. that's why all traditional societies soaked/sprouted/soured their grains/nuts/seeds before consumption. Unless you do something to trigger the germination process (and then arrest it), like soaked/dehydrated nuts, you really won't be able to digest them well, healthy or not. Unhealthy, it's all the worse.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-08T10:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-56873>

Okay so I wasn't too bloated this morning, so on an empty stomach I started drinking some cold water and tried to pay attention to the effects.

Almost immediately after swallowing the first couple of gulps I felt a very slight pressure/heaviness in my stomach and my lower abdomen wanted to expand slightly.

Upon drinking the whole glass I could feel and see that my abdomin had expanded, slightly but noticably enough.

So now I'm thinking what could react that quick to water? Do normal peoples stomachs do this to an extent? Is it the stomach reacting with inflammation to anything that stretches it? Are our stomachs a lot more sensitive to everything?

### Zorica Vuletic — 2012-11-14T16:00:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-after-consuming-anything-even-water-tried-all-alternative-solutions.4052/post-64241>

The two things I can think of which might help if all other things seem to be under control re: hormone and DHEA status would be to use inulin (benefibre) and the betaine HCL and/or other digestive enzymes.

I am in experimental phase of using betaine HCL and benefibre. I have used betaine and benefibre before and probably saw improvements but there were so many other variables/interventions involved that make it difficult to to parse out. I then discontinued all interventions since epi-paleo b/c I felt so amazing and 'cured'. lol Things were so super for end of July, August and most of September. I did notice great deterioration since end of September and that I have been continually 'fighting' something off. If not for epi-paleo, I would not be confident in my health at all right now. I did keep catching some kind of infections and it was getting progressive/unable to recover properly. So now I go back to using betaine and fibre, so far so good (in a few days) but it's hard to tell for sure since I seem to be getting rid of residual sx simultaneously.

So let me know if inulin + betain/digestive enzymes help at all. (O btw, the inulin I only started since yesterday and the betaine I've been taking for ~5 days). If i get the same results as I did in July...then I KNOW it's the ticket for me and that epi-paleo is just a super charger on top of that.

I have tried fasting before and eating reduced amounts of food for a while both of these keep bloating at a lower level but I have found even when water fasting my stomach bloats from drinking water (slightly but noticeably).

I am going to give fasting another go after I am done trying to cold adapt but I am not making much progress on that front either. 1.5 months of ice baths, face dunks, ice vests and I am still cold all of the time...

# Thread: Red LED Alarm Clock
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-10-02T01:35:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55704>

I feel kinda dumb right now. I thought I had the artificial light at night problem solved by staying off the computer and electronics after 6pm, turning off the TV by 7:30pm, switching out bright lights with dimmer, cool lights, and wearing blueblockers if I had to break these rules.

I've had the same alarm clock for years. It has red LED numbers. It is on my nightstand about 15" from my face as I sleep every night. Occasionally, I will waken about 3am and not be able to get back to sleep. I always thought this was a bi-phasic, normal sleep pattern or some weird surge I was getting.

I read an article about 'time-stamping', apparently your brain registers it when you check the clock several times in the night, and this can lead to sleep disturbance. Well, I rarely check the clock at night unless I happen to get jolted awake at 3am, but I started turning the clock toward the wall a couple weeks ago so I couldn't wake up and peek at the clock.

My sleep has been fantastic since I turned the clock around. I'm thinking the red LEDs must have been messing with my circadian signaling. All this time I thought red LEDs were fine at night.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-02T02:06:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55709>

I just started wearing an eye-bra ([http://www.amazon.com/Bucky-Blinks-Sleep-Paper-Flowers/dp/B006WMJ7G8/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1349132660&sr=8-1&keywords=Sleep+Mask+40+winks](http://http://www.amazon.com/Bucky-Blinks-Sleep-Paper-Flowers/dp/B006WMJ7G8/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1349132660&sr=8-1&keywords=Sleep+Mask+40+winks)) and have slept great the last few nights. I love this thing! I have a red LED clock that projects the time on the ceiling. I wonder if I was having the same issue as you. I thought maybe my DHEA was getting too high and disrupting my sleep. I will continue to monitor but thanks for posting that.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-02T03:04:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55730>

AKMan, we have the same thing. Don't know if DH will agree to turning it around. He did agree to getting rid of the clock with the blue lighted dial though.

Patty Cakes, does the eye bra keep in the heat? I think I'm going to have to make my own eyemask. I end up tugging them all off in the middle of the night, just to get cool.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-02T04:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55754>

Interesting. I remember TS Wiley saying to cover every LED clock you've got. So that's what I've done. Thankfully we have birds in our neighbourhood. I know if it's quiet out I'm awake too early (they start singing just before sunrise). So I'm clock/light free in the bedroom.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-02T05:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55757>

> **Nonchalant;56890 wrote:** Patty Cakes, does the eye bra keep in the heat? I think I'm going to have to make my own eyemask. I end up tugging them all off in the middle of the night, just to get cool.

It was 107 here today! Now it has been cooling down at night and I have only worn it all night a few times and partial nights a few times but I haven't gotten hot eyes. It's made from a neoprene material and not heavy and once you get it tight enough on the face it's comfortable and blocks the light. I get hot during the night sometimes but not hot flashes as this hot seems differnt and with an estrogen over 400 I doubt it's flashing. But my body gets hot not my eyes. No different than w/o the mask. Under $10 a BBBeyond!

### stacyj — 2012-10-02T15:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55791>

This is interesting. I almost always wake up in the middle of the night at the same exact time. I always wake up in the morning- working or not at the same time. I will have to try turning the clock around.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-02T15:10:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55795>

**Any artificial light** at night anywhere on your body is a problem for melatonin. In the January, 1998 issue of the magazine Science, Scott Campbell and Patricia Murphy of Cornell University Medical College in White Plains, New York, report on a study using light therapy applied to the backs of people's knees. This therapy apparently was effective in setting the body's clocks. Although controversial, this therapy is promising as a treatment for the blind, and suggests that areas of the skin may be able to set the body's clocks (this may help explain why blind individuals have varying degrees of "body clock symptoms" (little if any insomnia, no problems with hormonal cycles, etc.). In the July 11th Science News magazine, John Travis says:

"Despite the recent back-of-the-knee studies, eyes still seem to be the primary route by which the mammalian circadian system senses light. If scientists cut the optic nerve conveying information from the retina to the brain, mice lose their normal circadian rhythms. To resolve this seemingly contradictory evidence, investigators argue that retinas must contain light sensitive proteins other than the traditional opsins."

The implication is that there are cells in the retina that are not rods or cones, but that are specialized circadian photoreceptors. Aziz Sancar of the University of North Carolina School of Medicine contends that he has found such a photoreceptor. The chemical in this light sensitive receptor is made in the human retina as well as in human skin. This would explain why both the skin and the eyes affect the biological clocks of humans.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-02T17:21:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55823>

I can't stand a clock in my room. I have one, but it only illuminates if I push the button. I never push the button LOL After years of sleep problems, I have figured out that the best way for me to lie awake for the rest of the night is to check the time! I have a pretty good sense of time elapsed, and once I have that data point, I'm toast. All that said, I've been sleeping much better over the past several months, stirring through the night, but generally not waking. I want to get to the "rock-like" state though...

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-02T17:27:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/red-led-alarm-clock.4054/post-55824>

> **Jack Kruse;56984 wrote: Any artificial light** at night anywhere on your body is a problem for melatonin. In the January, 1998 issue of the magazine Science, Scott Campbell and Patricia Murphy of Cornell University Medical College in White Plains, New York, report on a study using light therapy applied to the backs of people's knees. This therapy apparently was effective in setting the body's clocks. Although controversial, this therapy is promising as a treatment for the blind, and suggests that areas of the skin may be able to set the body's clocks (this may help explain why blind individuals have varying degrees of "body clock symptoms" (little if any insomnia, no problems with hormonal cycles, etc.). In the July 11th Science News magazine, John Travis says:
>
>
>
> "Despite the recent back-of-the-knee studies, eyes still seem to be the primary route by which the mammalian circadian system senses light. If scientists cut the optic nerve conveying information from the retina to the brain, mice lose their normal circadian rhythms. To resolve this seemingly contradictory evidence, investigators argue that retinas must contain light sensitive proteins other than the traditional opsins."
>
>
>
> The implication is that there are cells in the retina that are not rods or cones, but that are specialized circadian photoreceptors. Aziz Sancar of the University of North Carolina School of Medicine contends that he has found such a photoreceptor. The chemical in this light sensitive receptor is made in the human retina as well as in human skin. This would explain why both the skin and the eyes affect the biological clocks of humans.

A blind person would still be subject to artificial light on the rest of their skin though. If light on the skin is enough to set the body's clocks, than we would expect a modern blind individual to be mismatched like the other people with whom he/she lives. Are they saying their symptoms are less severe, thanks to no input from their optic nerves? But then, they say mice lose their rhythms completely when this nerve is cut. But the mice would still have their skin pathways (unless mice don't have those pathways the way humans do?).

# Thread: Website Update:  Monday, Oct 1, 2012
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-update-monday-oct-1-2012.4056/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tellmisty — 2012-10-02T02:59:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-update-monday-oct-1-2012.4056/post-55729>

Hi everyone,

Whew! Okay, taking this opportunity to fill you guys in on how things are coming along:

1) **Blog comments - they are populating now; we are expecting them all to be loaded in this evening. WHEW! This has been a bear... we've had issues with different comment management tools not pulling all the comments in, and our poor programmers have had to import over and over and over all sorts of ways. We think we've nailed it this time. There are over 17,000 comments, so bear with us! Almost there!

2) **Consults - We're adding a step to the process to give you a worksheet to fill out for your health history. I need to finish creating the PDF tonight and send to Darren, so if this isn't online tonight, it's because of me, and we'll have it up tomorrow. We'll be sending an email out to everyone who's booked a consult to let them know they can book from their dashboard, so be watching for it. In the meantime, if you need to book a consult you've paid for, [instructions are here](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?4423-Website-Build-Update-instructions-for-consults).

3) **Forums** - Whew! This is a doozy. We are still working on this one ... moving the forums over is coooooomplicated, and we're looking at this from every angle to minimize issues. Hang tight. It might be end of the week before this is done. For now, things are working the same as always.

4) Webinars** - All webinars are available in the member area. If you are a Silver (Karnivore) member, you'll see the last three months' worth of webinars (including the September Q&A). If you're a Gold member, you'll see the entire webinar library.

5) Cookbook** - If you've purchased the cookbook, it should be available in your dashboard under "My Downloads." Now, it appears that quite a few of you used a _different_ email address when you bought the cookbook vs. the email address that's tied to your membership. SO, if that's the case, login with the proper email address that's tied to your cookbook order to access it ([here are some instructions to get your password](http://http://jackkruse.com/member-dashboard/support-faqs/) if you don't know it offhand). If you want us to merge the accounts, [open a support ticket](http://http://jackkruse.zendesk.com) and share both email addresses.

We are creating a training video for you guys to show you where to find information in your dashboard, and to point out where you can find things on the site. I expect the guys to have this done tonight. I'll post it here and we'll probably also send it out to you all via email. I think that will help a lot with some of the confusion on where to find things. As always, if you have any issues, [open a support ticket](http://http://jackkruse.zendesk.com). The ninjas will help ya!

Misty

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-04T04:49:13+0300

> **Jack Kruse;57406 wrote:** i dont evem know what tapatalk is.........lol

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-04T05:30:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/website-update-monday-oct-1-2012.4056/post-56162>

Tapatalk is an app for Apple, Android, Blackberry, and Chrome devices to make web-based forums more accessible on the limited screen space of mobile devices. It uses the device's Internet connection (wifi or cellular) like any other app would (at least on Apple devices, though it may use other ways of data access on other platforms).

So basically, instead of having to navigate a complex web page with tiny text on a tiny phone screen, you can have the forum content displayed as if it were an app especially designed for your mobile device's screen. It makes using this and other forums soooooo much easier. Well worth the $5 I paid for it.

The Tapatalk mobile app can access forums that have the Tapatalk server-side plugin installed. They make plugins for all the popular forum software platforms (phpBB, vBulletin, bbPress, etc).

It appears you all might be moving to bbPress forums, so we're in luck. Easy for you guys to enable, and easy for us to use.

BTW, I use Tapatalk to browse the Cold Thermogenesis forum while I get my CT on in the tub.  (With the Lifeproof case on my phone and blue-blockers on my head, of course!)

# Thread: to much protein, omega 3 ???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-much-protein-omega-3.4081/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### littleElefant — 2012-10-02T23:32:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-much-protein-omega-3.4081/post-55955>

Hi all,

that's the first time I post a message here.

For some weeks now I try to eate epi-paleo style.

I eat raw seafood two or three times a day, the rest is coconut, raw veggetables, vegetable juice and avocados mainly.

I love this livestyle and can feel how this food heals me.

But I hold myself back a lot because I'm sort of scared to make myself sick again. There is so many contradictory information out there.

I m insecure about protein portions and about pufas.

To much protein often is linked to acidose, premature aging, inflamation, kidney problems, clogging the lymph system etc.

To much omega 3, to much pufas. Also linked to inflamation, aging, oxidativ stress in the cells. I read you should not eat more than 3 grams of Omega 3 per day. But that would mean you could hardly eat more than 100 g mackerel or harreng or so.

I love sea food and animal protein but I hold myself back and try to not eat more than 300 g a day.

Not a very optimal situation

littleElefant

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-03T04:32:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-much-protein-omega-3.4081/post-56008>

When we have people who are obese or LR this is the best time to use protein for a short period (12 months) to control cravings and slowly ramp up fats as the protein falls......as one approaches optimal......how best to do this naturally? Start the epi paleo template as soon as cravings for carbs are controlled. How do you accelerate weight loss with the template change? You add CT as you go........it is layered appraoch that takes finesse.......and discipline but it is not hard to do when you understand why.
