# Thread: Seafood Breakfast Idea Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-10-17T07:43:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58680>

Hey everyone. I've been having to eat eggs and canned tuna for quite some time and now I have a little more money to be able to eat some "real" fish and maybe some shellfish for my first meal of the day.

I'm from the midwest and don't have any ideas for good seafood recipes. I almost never ate fish growing up. I used to hate shrimp as well. I've made my fish dinners pretty decent now using spices and whatnot, but for breakfast I've only been doing a tuna scramble as I like to call it.

I just started eating shrimp, but I've still never cooked it yet. I'd like to try clams and maybe oysters as well, but that can get expensive since there really isn't much meat in them. So, I think I'll mainly try to stick to fish and shrimp for now.

I'd love to know what you guys eat for breakfast on the Epi-paleo diet. I need to eat a decent sized breakfast so that I'm not hungry before dinner. I can't really eat lunch because I won't have enough money for food since I'm buying fish now instead of land animals.

I'd like to cut back on the amount of eggs I eat if possible, but I will take any breakfast ideas even if they include eggs. No cheese though.

It doesn't have to taste "breakfasty", I'd be ok with just some good recipes for fish/shellfish. So, what do you eat for breakfast??

### Michael — 2012-10-17T08:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58682>

My breakfast often isn't fish, but there is some food that works quite nicely in the morning (or any other time).

I sometimes have a kipper (or two) with my breakfast. That's actually a very traditional breakfast dish -- the sort of thing Sherlock Holmes might be eating in an old movie. Grilled mushroom and tomato would be good with that.

Another is smoked haddock (the un-dyed type). You can poach it in water for about 4 minutes. That often used to be served with a poached egg on top. Something like steamed broccoli or asparagus would complement that well.

And there's kedgeree. That's a sort of 19th century Anglo-Indian breakfast dish. The usual version has rice in it, but maybe cauliflower could substitute for that.

Quicker would be cold cooked fish or some smoked salmon or trout.

Then there are Scandinavian dishes with fish that's been marinated in vinegar and spices. Here's a Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall recipe along those lines for rollmop herrings. He soaks them in a kilner jar for a while with a mixture of cider and cider vinegar and some spices. You'd maybe want to substitute a little honey for the tablespoon of sugar, and I wouldn't use zest of orange unless you know the orange is unwaxed - but something like this:

[http://www.guardian.co.uk/lifeandstyle/2010/jan/23/herring-recipes-hugh-fearnley-whittingstall](http://http://www.guardian.co.uk/lifeandstyle/2010/jan/23/herring-recipes-hugh-fearnley-whittingstall)

inger here makes a Scandinavian-style dish of fish with these sorts of flavours, but she doesn't marinate it just mixes it up and eats it fresh.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-17T14:36:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58695>

I use breakfast to load up on Saturated fat. I always have seafood for lunch and at least for dinner 3x/week

Recently I've pushed the fat content up to deal w/the nausea I get w/taking cycloset

If your going to do seafood why does it have to be a breakfast type meal. Why can't you just make a dinner type meal and nosh that?

We do seafood stew regularly.

Bacon. & clams & mussels is a big hit

Fish "tacos" wrapped in blanched cabbage

Shrimp salad

Tuna & sardine salad

Crab cakes

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-17T16:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58707>

I do the same thing Gretch does.......but if I have left over from the night before I take full advantage of it for the BAB then.

### Michael — 2012-10-17T16:31:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58711>

There's another one for you, Lovelife - leftovers. Besides just eating them "as is" you could use some flaked fish and/or some shellfish as an omelette filling.

### ealachan — 2012-10-17T16:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58712>

My "fish for breakfast" go-to recipe is half a can (or sometimes a whole can) of salmon cooked in a little bacon fat, with a couple strips of bacon crumbled into it, and two runny-yolked eggs over the top. Super good on a bed of lightly cooked kale, as well.

### BJK77 — 2012-10-17T17:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58715>

I do the same as Shijin and load up on saturated fats for my BAB and then have lighter seafood meals for lunch and dinner.

The few times I had a seafood BAB I ate bacon wrapped salmon or just noshed a pound of shrimp. My veggie of choice these days is always some type of seaweed - dulse, kelp, arame, kombu, etc. If I'm feeling especially hungry, I'll also throw in some avocado or mushrooms.

### Rosekelly — 2012-10-17T18:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58723>

Frozen clams. Bacon. Add garlic and onions. Yum!

### Lyndra — 2012-10-17T18:50:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58724>

I do something simple - mash together half an avocado and a can of sardines, spread on a sheet of nori, then roll up and cut into slices. Very tasty and easy.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-17T19:10:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58726>

> **Lyndra;60260 wrote:** I do something simple - mash together half an avocado and a can of sardines, spread on a sheet of nori, then roll up and cut into slices. Very tasty and easy.

Hmmm! I am going to try this! Caveboy eats many cans of sardines, but I'm having a tough time getting into them.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-17T20:55:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58766>

Thanks everyone for your suggestions!!

For those asking why I want to eat fish for breakfast.

I do eat fish for dinner. Here is my dilemma... I don't have the money to buy any grassfed meat. So, I can eat beef that is grainfed or try to just eat fish/shellfish. What do you guys think is better (the fish is not always wild caught either). I figured it would be better to eat fish than regular meat, but maybe not.

I also don't understand why we shouldn't use any saturated fat when we cook our seafood? I know that it was stated in one of the blog posts, but I never saw or understood why. Also, what do you guys cook your fish in then if you can't use any butter, duck fat, or CO? It will stick to the pan if I don't use some kind of fat to cook it on. I can't grill because I don't have one. I guess I could bake it, but that ends up taking so much longer than just cooking it on the stove-top.

I have $150 for food for a month. The cheapest fish I can get is Swahli or another white fish I can't remember the name of.. not wild caught for $10.99/4lbs. As you can see it's pretty tough to eat this way and get enough food. I was eating two eggs, can of tuna, a potato (I know not great, but helps hunger), half onion, some butter or duck fat, one piece of fish (about 4-6oz), 1 quarter pound burger, cup of cooked broccoli or seaweed salad. That was on about $125/month. Sometimes no potatoes. That was breakfast and dinner. Skip lunch because not enough food. Sometimes I was hungry still, but I'd wait out till dinner. I've always had a large appetite, though to me this isn't a lot of food by any means, but my roommate can somehow only eat once a day and be fine (though she smokes and drinks coke all day). So, to some I guess this is plenty of food, but to me it's not much. Fish has never really been filling to me compared to other meat.. I'm assuming it's the lower fat content. Which is why I usually need to fill up on a good amount of fat and meat or I'm hungry. I love food!

### Michael — 2012-10-17T21:15:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58771>

> **Lovelife;60302 wrote:** I figured it would be better to eat fish than regular meat, but maybe not.
>
>
> Yes, it is. See this blog post. Explains it all:
>
>
>
> [http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http://http://jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)
>
>
>
>
> > I also don't understand why we shouldn't use any saturated fat when we cook our seafood?
> >
> >
> > Because saturated fat has the effect of blocking the uptake of DHA, which most of us need so badly.
> >
> >
> >
> > Also, what do you guys cook your fish in then if you can't use any butter, duck fat, or CO? It will stick to the pan if I don't use some kind of fat to cook it on. I can't grill because I don't have one. I guess I could bake it, but that ends up taking so much longer than just cooking it on the stove-top.
>
>
>
> It depends what you're cooking. Duck fat, which you mention, is actually mostly unsaturated, like olive oil. Actually, you can probably use olive oil so long as you keep the heat nice and low. But butter/ghee wouldn't matter occasionally: it's just not ideal, for the reason stated above. Much of the time, you won't need to fry. Something like trout is often stuffed and gently fried, but a lot of fish is usually just simply poached in gently simmering water, or fish stock, or a _[court bouillon](http://http://www.bbc.co.uk/food/court_bouillon). It takes just a few minutes, doesn't damage the proteins so much, since it's gentle moist heat, and creates little smell in the kitchen.
>
>
>
> If you didn't use fat to cook_ the food but want to get the fat-content of the meal up, just have a dressed salad with it or put a little olive oil on any accompanying cooked vegetables.
>
>
>
> I have $150 for food for a month. The cheapest fish I can get is Swahli or another white fish I can't remember the name of.. not wild caught for $10.99/4lbs. As you can see it's pretty tough to eat this way and get enough food.

Talapia perhaps?

Liver and kidneys are often fairly cheap these days - I guess mostly because people are sort of halfway to vegetarianism and don't like to eat anything that looks like it actually came from an animal. If it's from something like an non-intensively reared lamb, it's good. Frozen lamb's offal from Australia or New Zealand should be grass-fed.

Canned sardines and salmon are pretty cheap considering.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-17T21:21:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-breakfast-idea-help.4351/post-58772>

I often eat seviche for breakfast, just take the jar out of the fridge, strain some seafood from the lime juice, and eat. Really fast, really simple, really good. Breakfast from start to finish in 5 minutes.

# Thread: hack my 12 week epipaleo labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2012-10-17T16:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58709>

Male 51 165lbs 5'9" Type 2 diabetes, Hashi's .

Went paleo 6/2011. Leptin reset 11/2011. Started CT 2/2012. Epi-paleo 7/7/12.

Less than 50gm carb/day

7/7/12 Started topical 7keto rotating with dhea topical 25mg/day and dhea 50mg sublingual

7/20/12 Started Metformin 2000mg/day

9/15 Started topical pregnenolone 25mg/day

9/13 Adjusted Synthroid 112mcg to levothyroxine75mcg with liothyronine 10mcg

I have been eating major shellfish, fish, 3 eggs/day,some grassfed beef, seaweed everyday, some brazils,macadamia,coconut,100%chocolate, very little veg except for herbs,garlic,onions,spices.

I have had very little or none of the following during the past 12 wks: Nightshades, anything I believe contains acetaldehyde, brassicas,dairy, industrial oils, fruit, sugar , grains.

I drink too much coffee.

I CT 5+ days/week 20 min swim in 50deg or less

I am very active and feel great most of the time except for mild fatigue around 2-3:00 pm

I abide by circadian rhythm and eat the leptin rx. No extended cardio.

[Dulalabs 10-5-12.txt](<http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/60245=516-Dulalabs 10-5-12.txt>)

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-17T19:33:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58729>

Is the T3 really FT3? Assuming it is, it appears to be low. I like mine to be in the very top of the range....that is where I feel best. Your FT3 looks pretty good which means you are probably not converting FT4 to FT3. I'm sure you know all this but conversion happens in the liver and gut. Plus that TSH is high (but you know my feeling on TSH!) which tells me you are still probably experiencing the hypo symptoms. My worst hypo issues was fatigue between 2-3pm. I feel your pain. Looks like your TSH shot up from August labs (probably Hashis doing it's thing).

I think you may need to look into adding more T3 to your regime. It looks like you just adjusted it but the T3 doesn't have much (if any) half life so should have shown up in these results if the timing was right. The good news is your ABs are pretty low in the scope of things.

I don't know enough about the NA/K ratio but it looks off to me. Potassium seems low. Eat more bananas...LOL. I'm totally kidding!!!

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-17T20:43:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58761>

Thanks for your input Patty. Do you know how AB activity relates to thyroid function? If AB's are lower should thyroid function be improved or not? Co2 was low indicating acidosis so endo had me stop metformin. CO2 improved within a week, but now BG is high.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-18T00:20:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58826>

Dali, can you explain how to read the top set of labs. For example, are trigs 40 or 86.33 or something else? I'm just having trouble deciphering (the second set are more straightforward).

Also, can you explain this: "Co2 was low indicating acidosis so endo had me stop metformin." My doc is considering putting me on metformin but wants to monitor BG and also said something about acidity that I didn't understand. I'd like to know more. Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-18T01:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58833>

> **Dali Dula;60297 wrote:** Thanks for your input Patty. Do you know how AB activity relates to thyroid function? If AB's are lower should thyroid function be improved or not? Co2 was low indicating acidosis so endo had me stop metformin. CO2 improved within a week, but now BG is high.

The metabolic acidosis story and metformin is way overblown.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-18T01:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58834>

> **Dali Dula;60297 wrote:** Thanks for your input Patty. Do you know how AB activity relates to thyroid function?

My understanding is that when ABs are high they are working on destroying the thyroid tissue. When ABs are low the tissue is being spared. However, for me personally I haven't noticed any change in meds no matter what my ABs read. Have you noticed a difference in meds based on your ABs?

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-18T01:38:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58835>

Range of trigs is 40 -160. My result is 86.33. This is a 41 point increase in 6 weeks. My CO2 was at 18.2. My endo says this indicates renal tubular acidosis. I dropped metformin for a week and retested. My CO2 is back in range @ 22. Some people have this reaction to metformin according to my pharmacist. I used metformin for 2 years after my diabetes diagnosis and did not have a problem.I had just recently started using it again.My BG is up average 30 points since stopping metformin.

### Dali Dula — 2012-10-18T01:44:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58838>

> **Patty Cakes;60371 wrote:** My understanding is that when ABs are high they are working on destroying the thyroid tissue. When ABs are low the tissue is being spared. However, for me personally I haven't noticed any change in meds no matter what my ABs read. Have you noticed a difference in meds based on your ABs?

I have not had the understanding nor the test data to form any correlation with AB activity. I am fortunately not presenting with serious symptoms. Passing afternoon fatigue and some brain fog.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-18T01:50:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58840>

My afternoon fatigue is completely gone with a dose of T3 at that time!

### Huck — 2012-10-18T02:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hack-my-12-week-epipaleo-labs.4358/post-58842>

Free testosterone is low.

Why are you testing dhea and not dhea-s?

What about pregnenolone, estradiol, dht, SHBG, and progesterone.

Reading this thread this morning, it made me realize....I don't even know where that Ped Egg thing even is anymore. Probably in my bathroom cabinet. My feet are so smooth now, as are my elbows, etc.

I had female itching...so terrible, no OB/GYN could figure it out. Tested for everything. They actually sent me to Baylor College of Medicine, so an 80-year old Professor and his Residents could look at me. Yes, I had 5 Medical Residents staring at me, in stirrups. Lovely.

30 days after starting Epi-Paleo, and itching was gone. Haven't itched in over 8 months. This thread reminded me how horrible it was.

# Thread: CT, vitamin C and hibernation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrÃ©s — 2012-10-18T14:17:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-58896>

First, the science.

It seems our eutherian cousins still able to hibernate increase their brains' ascorbic acid reserves before entering hibernation, [http://www.sciencenews.org/sn\_arc97/12\_6\_97/bob1.htm](http://http://www.sciencenews.org/sn_arc97/12_6_97/bob1.htm):

> Merging their interests, Rice and Drew decided to investigate antioxidant concentrations in hibernating arctic ground squirrels. They've now found that the amount of ascorbic acid in the blood of hibernating animals rockets to four times that measured during nonhibernating periods. Moreover, the amount of ascorbic acid in the cerebrospinal fluid that bathes the central nervous system doubles during hibernation. "It seems an important strategy to build up this extracellular store of ascorbic acid," says Rice.
>
>
>
> The researchers also noticed that when a squirrel roused temporarily from its torpor, a periodic occurrence for most hibernators, its vitamin C supply returned to normal within hours.

I think it is plausible that the mechanism for increasing concentrations of ascorbic acid on humans' brains may be still active on us and that it is activated upon cold exposure, since it could be a mechanism at play on brain protection under low temperature conditions, but I can't find any concrete evidence about it. Anyone measuring his/her spinal fluid content of vitamin C before and after CT?

Now, the personal anecdote. No, I haven't entered hibernation . I simply went and check the possible interaction between cold and vitamin C before merging them to treat a little common cold. I know I said that I would try CT upon any illness to see how it fares, but I had to be operative and just decided to smash it with all of the hammers at hand.

We were at Disneyland Paris this last rainy weekend, me, my daughters, my girlfriend and her daughter. Maximum temperature was 63Â°F (17Â°C) on Friday, 57Â°F (14Â°C) on Saturday and lower on Sunday. On Friday I wore my jacket but I got my feet wet, my weakness point. I got a little common cold. I brought there only a little vitamin C supply. From there on, I wore just my T-shirt waist upwards all the weekend (I think I was the only kruser there) and ate a generous share of butter to keep BAT on. I have to say that I improved a lot till Sunday that I got my feet highly wet again. So, upon arrival to Spain on Sunday I took a high dose of vitamin C. On Monday afternoon I [titrated on vitamin C](http://http://www.orthomed.com/titrate.htm) (1g each 5 minutes; 34g total) while wearing my [DIY-ice-vest](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1652-Effective-Efficient-CT&p=30971#post30971) (actually ice-vest v.2 with 6 panels of ice, 1 pound each): cold crushed.

I think about [Dr. Kruse position about vitamin C](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?3996-Brain-Gut-12-is-now-Live&p=53150&viewfull=1#post53150) as that of a purist (I hope it doesn't sound disrespectful since I don't try it to): we have evolved with enough adaptations to not having to rely on it, and we should turn on all of those adaptations instead of relying on high doses of exogenous vitamin C. Correct me if I have misinterpreted.

I think that I see the beauty of this argument/approach, but I think that we should use vitamin C whenever it is convenient, since all of the machinery to its use is still active on us.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-18T14:47:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-58899>

I agree with this concept fully

### nonchalant — 2012-10-18T17:01:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-58926>

> **AndrÃ©s;60436 wrote:** On Monday afternoon I titrated on vitamin C (1g each 5 minutes; 34g total) while wearing my DIY-ice-vest (actually ice-vest v.2 with 6 panels of ice, 1 pound each): cold crushed.

Interesting approach. I've been waiting for illness, eager to try CT with it, but I keep resisting the cold, darnit.

Sometimes my throat starts to get sore, and I get hopeful, but it all clears up quickly.

Patience, Grasshopper...

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-10-19T11:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-59132>

> **Jack Kruse;60439 wrote: I agree with this concept fully
> Ok! I finally think I grasp your point of view.
>
>
>
> Nonchalant;60466 wrote:** Interesting approach. I've been waiting for illness, eager to try CT with it, but I keep resisting the cold, darnit.
>
> Sometimes my throat starts to get sore, and I get hopeful, but it all clears up quickly.
>
> Patience, Grasshopper...

LOL! I suppose we are going to get sick a lot less often now...

### freesia — 2012-10-19T21:04:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-59219>

Most intersting!!! Thanks Andres!

I've just come through winter and I had the usual couple of cold viruses hit me via my children bringing them home from school...

I didn't try CT right when I had a high temperature or a pounding head, but getting back in a cold bath (sub 50F) at the tail end of my virus completely obliterated it.....much like using high dose Vit C (I use 4 - 5g per hour) has treated me in the past...but faster.

I'd be really interested in measuring spinal fluid before and after CT.

Thanks again for your observations...and the link.

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-10-21T13:32:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-59447>

> **freesia;60760 wrote: Most intersting!!! Thanks Andres!You're welcome!
>
>
>
>
> > **freesia;60760 wrote:** I didn't try CT right when I had a high temperature or a pounding head, but getting back in a cold bath (sub 50F) at the tail end of my virus completely obliterated it.....much like using high dose Vit C (I use 4 - 5g per hour) has treated me in the past...but faster.
>
> Precisely as me. If I hadn't got my feet wet again on Sunday, only air CTing would have put in remission this little common cold of mine.
>
>
>
> freesia;60760 wrote:** I'd be really interested in measuring spinal fluid before and after CT.

He, he! With enough _patients_ volunteering, perhaps Dr. Kruse would get in position of measuring it...

### AndrÃ©s — 2012-10-24T20:29:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-vitamin-c-and-hibernation.4378/post-60011>

> **Anna K.;61590 wrote: I'm glad to hear that there is an evolutionary reason for taking mega dose of C.Actually, I don't think Dr. Kruse would agree on the "mega" part for humans.
>
>
>
> Anna K.;61590 wrote:** Are there any dangerous of mega dosing C for a long time?

Of all of those supposed evils of ascorbic acid from conventional wisdom (some discussed [here](http://http://www.vitamincfoundation.org/faq.shtml) or [here](http://http://www.vitamincfoundation.org/faqdoc.shtml)), I only think plausible for doses larger than 9g/day that of possible copper (ceruloplasmin) deregulation: see [here](http://http://www.vitamincfoundation.org/forum/viewtopic.php?f=14&t=2145) and [here](http://http://www.drrisley.com/html/VitaminC.html). I like my daily intake around that of a carnivorous mammal production just in case, [around 3 grams scaled from dogs and cats](http://http://www.seanet.com/~alexs/ascorbate/198x/stone-i-orthomol_psych-1984-v13-n4-p280.htm). With this dose my acne is completely held at bay. Of course I go much higher when ill, for five days at most.

# Thread: Lethally Hot Earth Was Devoid of Life (Triassic)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lethally-hot-earth-was-devoid-of-life-triassic.4396/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-19T08:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lethally-hot-earth-was-devoid-of-life-triassic.4396/post-59125>

National Geographic:

> Extinctions during the early Triassic period left Earth a virtual wasteland, largely because life literally couldn't take the heat, a new study suggests.
>
>
> [http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2012/10/121018-triassic-extinctions-hot-global-warming-science-environment/](http://http://news.nationalgeographic.com/news/2012/10/121018-triassic-extinctions-hot-global-warming-science-environment/)
>
>
>
> Ars Terchnica:
>
>
>
>
> > Based on their findings, the Great Dying was accompanied by a huge increase in water temperatures in the area, which rose from 21Â° to 36Â°C over the course of 800,000 years. After a period of decline, they surged again, this time reaching at least 38Â°C, and possibly exceeding 40Â°Câ€”that's over 100Â° Fahrenheit. And this was ocean water; temperatures on land were likely to have been even more extreme.
>
>
>
> [http://arstechnica.com/science/2012/10/too-hot-to-handle-triassic-temps-may-have-killed-tropical-species/](http://http://arstechnica.com/science/2012/10/too-hot-to-handle-triassic-temps-may-have-killed-tropical-species/)
>
>
>
> Eurekalert:
>
>
>
> The dead zone would have been a strange world â€“ very wet in the tropics but with almost nothing growing. No forests grew, only shrubs and ferns. No fish or marine reptiles were to be found in the tropics, only shellfish, and virtually no land animals existed because their high metabolic rate made it impossible to deal with the extreme temperatures. Only the polar regions provided a refuge from the baking heat.

[http://www.eurekalert.org/pub\_releases/2012-10/uol-tcc101712.php](http://http://www.eurekalert.org/pub_releases/2012-10/uol-tcc101712.php)

Graphic:

[https://docs.google.com/file/d/0B691WmQZab8LZVdsYXRBR1pqOVU/edit?pli=1](http://https://docs.google.com/file/d/0B691WmQZab8LZVdsYXRBR1pqOVU/edit?pli=1)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-19T13:56:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lethally-hot-earth-was-devoid-of-life-triassic.4396/post-59139>

Good stuff here. Loved the paleo graphic in the google docs.

# Thread: Fish Remains in Archaeology - Hannah Russ
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-20T20:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59372>

> ... The archaeological record of fish as a food source is further hampered by the loss of many coastal sites (due to changes in sea levels) where evidence of extensive fishing might be found. **Furthermore, archaeologists have tended to neglect fishing because it does not leave behind stone tools; neither does it fit the â€œMan the Hunterâ€￾ stereotype for prehistoric peoples, who are more often portrayed as hunters of large game. Hannah found, during her own research for modern-day equivalents of fishing amongst hunter-gatherer people of the present-day, that **even modern ethnographic studies tend to ignore the highly important role that fish and fishing plays** in diet, culture and technology.
>
>
>
> Fish was a vital food source for prehistoric peoples. Not only is fish highly nutritious, and often relatively easy to catch** (not requiring stone tools for either the hunting or eating process), it should be remembered that although many fish were small, several species of prehistoric fish were much larger than modern day equivalents, some even growing as big as a large mammal, so that a single catch could provide a substantial food source for a number of people.
>
>
>
> There were over 28,000 species of fish available to prehistoric peoples. ...

[http://www.uwhg.org.uk/reports/uwhg\_meetings/01\_apr\_10/01\_apr\_10.html](http://http://www.uwhg.org.uk/reports/uwhg_meetings/01_apr_10/01_apr_10.html)

### Michael — 2012-10-21T11:32:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59445>

Of course, as Hannah explains, you don't necessarily need a fish hook to catch fish, but here is a story about some of the oldest fishhooks found:

> Two 11-square-foot pits dug in Jerimalai Cave on the east end of East Timor, an island nation off northwestern Australia, have provided some of the earliest evidence of fishing technology. Though there is little evidence of fishing activity beyond 10,000 years ago, fragments of fish hooks found in the cave date to between 16,000 and 23,000 years ago, making them the oldest ever recovered.

[http://www.archaeology.org/1203/trenches/jerimalai\_cave\_east\_timor\_fish\_hooks.html](http://http://www.archaeology.org/1203/trenches/jerimalai_cave_east_timor_fish_hooks.html)

### Michael — 2012-10-21T11:38:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59446>

> Hannah’s research interests are in understanding the role of fish and other marine resources in past diet, focusing mainly on the Middle and Upper Palaeolithic periods in Western Europe.
>
>
>
> In 2010 her PhD research on this topic was awarded the Eila Campbell Scholarship by the British Federation of Women Graduates for academic excellence.

[http://www.sheffield.ac.uk/archaeology/people/russ](http://http://www.sheffield.ac.uk/archaeology/people/russ)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-21T14:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59450>

awesome thread Michael.......

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-10-22T20:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fish-remains-in-archaeology-hannah-russ.4420/post-59682>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59423>

man, i cannot sleep. crap man, where did insomnia come from? i take cold showers, i use my blue blockers, i drink coffee during the morning only. i am eating my fish.

i just bought some Unisom pills, bad i know, but it knocks me out. makes me feel feverish when waking up.

any help will be appreciated. im scare what this is doing to me, lowering my dhea content, making more leptin and insulin resistance i bet though i am fit.

### ColdBren — 2012-10-21T04:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59425>

If I do CT after a certain time of the day, I can't sleep. I know some respond differently though. Do you take the cold showers late? I wonder if using a tiny bit of melatonin for a short period of time might help....probably better for you than the unisom?

### diane — 2012-10-21T15:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-59452>

Everyone's different - so try a few things and see what works for you. Try cutting the coffee in the morning - hard I know (I love it!) but it really affects my sleep. Melatonin before you go to sleep, and one of the things that's improved my sleep the most was a sleep mask. When I do wake up during the night, it's still black, I don't focus on the fact that I've woken up, and have no idea what time it is. Which lessens my frustration!

The other thing is watch the timing of your last meal. That one really impacts me as well - if I eat late or have wine, my sleep is crap even if I do the blue blockers. When I eat by 6pm - so much better!

I actually take Melatonin and it doesn't help me sleep. I take for other reasons though, releasing growth hormones, antioxidant and it makes me feel super human.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-29T17:59:40+0200

you cant sleep your Cortisol/DHEA/IGF1/Melatonin cycle is broken........its time to test then and rebuild it.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-29T18:49:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cannot-sleep-consistantly.4427/post-60741>

I suggest you follow Dr. K's advice and test. Start with a 4-panel ASI (salivary cortisol & DHEA-S). It's highly like your cortisol is not peaking at the right time of day and your adrenals aren't functioning well. While coffee will perk you up in the morning, it will only harm your adrenals. Cut back if you can't just quit cold turkey but get off it. Eat first thing in the AM and make sure to get bright sunlight exposure in the morning, then wear the blu-blockers after dusk and avoid computer time in the evening. I would also ditch the sleeping pills and avoid alcohol. It may take a while but you will come around. I've been struggling with my sleep for almost 2 years and it is finally starting to improve.

I dont drink and smoke and try to eat as healthy as possible....... mainly eating fish, oysters, and grassfed beef (too expensive though)...practically drink water the majority.

I spend about 10 minutes outside viewing the sun when I can during the morning on my days off, when I work, I dont have the luxury until I go for my break around 10am in the morning.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-21T06:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59434>

I'm not flinging anything.

But I am NOT going to say she asked for it by dressing "seductively." That's most definitely a male perspective ... "she MADE me do it." Grow up, boys and learn to take some responsibility. Real men don't need to do that.

I've suffered sexual harassment. It is not pretty, it is horrifying, and it was just because I'm female. My clothing had nothing to do with it. So I'm afraid, Cavemam, I'm on the other side of the fence from you on this one. The road you're on leads to a burqua because the poor male can't control himself.

Look at it this way, if she'd been at the beach in a bikini would you feel the same way?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-21T06:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59436>

Let me see. You wear a boobie shirt and latex pants to a geekfest, and then act shocked when you're harassed?

What's the word....yeah...context.

The guys in this situation are pigs, and the chicks that dress provocatively are attention mongering. Hence the term provocative. There is another term I'd normally use, but I'll edit.

I don't see any reason to place blame solely on the male side here...

you put your pony on parade, and he's gonna get judged.

You publish your views, you're gonna have critics.

You wear leather pants and a booby shirt, you're gonna get catcalls. (Yep. I had a pair. I didn't wear them for comfort, I wore them because I looked, to quote one guy, like sex on a stick. 'twas many moons ago, but hey. Why not? I might be a used-to-could, but by God, I used-to-could)

If she were forty pounds overweight (lol...a la moi) she'd be bent because she wasn't getting the attention she wanted.

Fault on both sides, as I see it. Yes, a guy should be capable of self mastery, but that does not, IMO, negate a woman's responsibility to dress appropriately to her ability to handle unwanted attention with a little grace.

A bikini on a beach is entirely different, and in my view, far less sexualized, than the context here. But that's just my $.02, and it's worth every penny you paid.

And, Cavemam...I always did like archery

### ealachan — 2012-10-21T19:14:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59487>

> **Jack Kruse;61028 wrote:** I thought they did it to get paid? Was it not their job to dress up and just look like the characters? Or am I nuts?

It depends on the con. Some definitely have people who get paid to dress up like certain characters, while others just have zealous fans that like to get all dressed up and have their picture taken a lot.

Also, in response to the thread in general and for what it's worth, we go to GenCon every year. There are 40,000 people there all weekend long, probably a good 1/4 of which have some kind of costume on for most of the time. I've never seen or heard of any woman being harassed for what she was wearing while at the convention itself. Maybe it's going on and just no one is talking about it, I don't know...but I've certainly never seen it myself.

I have a HUGE issue with the "she was asking for it" reasoning, personally...but I'm not the argumentative type, so I'mma go find something else to read now.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-21T22:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59509>

> **Anna K.;61051 wrote:** There should be a line in harassment between just staring and any kind of physical or verbal abuse.

Me too, I think someone saying things that reduce someone to a sexual object (channeling jack. . .) in that context is a very different thing than in a workplace, while someone is being physically threatened. I think women whining about stuff like this reduces the severity of the impact of actual physical violence and rape.

not so humbly I submit that this woman is doing more to degrade women and supporting the objectification of them than any of the men ooogling her.

Of course men should control themselves - but really, she's got her lady parts on display, with white fluffy fringe! Looking at her is the most reasonable reaction if we accept our animal selves - hell, I wouldn't be interested in a sexual manner, but I sure would look!

I think in a non-sexual setting, when someone says something sexist that makes a woman uncomfortable - it's very bad manners. Making others uncomfortable in social situations is bad manners. I'm from the south, we do not enjoy bad manners.

I didn't mean to cause flame on this - I was just curious to discuss the relevant portions of our true animal nature and gender roles - but clearly this topic is too big, we are not all operating from the same ground rules.

Again, why can't we disagree and still discuss?

Just curious - I'm not setting up to espouse my opinion, I am interested in exploring gender roles and how the abiding or disavowing of them might effect our health. Mismatches and all.

### ealachan — 2012-10-23T17:18:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59821>

I told myself I'd stay away from this thread, but...\*deep breath\*...

I think that if you're going to show your goodies, you need to expect that people are going to look at them, **BUT**...I DO NOT think that just because someone has their goodies on display that it gives anyone else the right to comment on them or assume that said goodies are free for the groping.

In other words, my cleavage (or whatever) is mine. If I choose to show it off for whatever reason, that's my choice, and it doesn't automatically entitle the Peanut Gallery to provide commentary. I think a lot of people, Americans especially are just plain rude. We share our opinion regardless of whether it's wanted, all under the guise of "free speech". Free speech is a glorious thing, but it wasn't meant to give people license to be jackasses.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-23T18:55:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59836>

> **janagram;61407 wrote:** It's just to juicy to stay away from!
>
> Teaching high school opened my eyes to the "new feminism"...where girls flaunt it in ways we never would have for fear of all the repercussions mentioned above....the smart ones are doing it for power, and the dumb ones are just doing it....

This is interesting!

I tend to think women's sexuality IS one of our 'powers', not mine of course - but some nubile 20somethin with a good hormone panel. I think one of the negatives towards women is that the feminism of the 60's made it bad to have traditional female roles. I think we out thought ourselves on that one. So now instead of the wise women teaching the younger ones the ways to USE those powers they are left floundering thinking the way to use sexuality is to lay it out spread eagle for all the world to see. Idiots!!!!!

So for the record, I'm very far from a religious minded person. . . but I think human women/men to LEAN towards specific gender behaviors - and that maybe even we THRIVE and are healthier for heeding them.

I don't mean ANYBODY should be forced to do anything, nor kept from doing anything - my only religious leaning is libertarianism.

But, to dismiss the idea that a significant portion of women come with gender preferences built into their software is not thinking expansively enough.

It could just be an old ladies view that the way the new kids are doing it is going to hell in a handbasket etc. . . .but I think if we teach our daughters that they have power, AND HOW TO WEILD IT. . . the world just might be better off for it. Our daughters certainly will.

Of course I have a son - and we joke about finding a wife that will give me lots of grandbabies. hehehe. So much for her freedom of choice

### Michael — 2012-10-23T19:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59846>

I think this a thread for the ladies, so I've avoided commenting on it.

However, as a point of interest, what is appropriate clothing and, therefore, what counts as good manners (what used to regulate our sense of what should and should not be done in public, but which, sadly, no longer does) is context dependent. It depends on social norms. (c.f. [Sittlichkeit](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sittlichkeit).)

These women are not underdressed:

ALL OF THIS SETS A BAD PRECEDENCE FOR CHAOS THAT SHE MUST LEVERAGE HER BODY TO GET ANYWHERE, AND THAT INTELLIGENCE DOESN'T COUNT. NOT TO MENTION THE PRECEDENCE IT SETS FOR HAVOC - THAT GIRLS/WOMEN ARE ONLY TO BE OBJECTIFIED B/C OF HOW THEY LOOK OR DO NOT LOOK, AND THAT GIRLS/WOMEN'S INTELLIGENCE DOESN'T COUNT FOR ANYTHING. WAIT FOR IT...EVEN DORA THE EXPLORER HAS BEENG GIVEN BOOBS AND AN ASS - BUT SHE'S SUPPOSED TO BE A TODDLER THAT OTHER TODDLERS CAN IDENTIFY WITH - WTF??? DICK AND ROBERT HEINLINE BOOKS), GRANTED ITS ONLY BEEN OF RECENT THAT THE COMIC/SCI FI WORLD HAS BEGUN TO ACKNOWLEDGE THAT WOMEN ARE FANS OF THE GENERA - I LOVED HOW THE RE-BOOTED BSG MADE STARBUCK A WOMAN WHO KICKED ASS AND TOOK NAMES W/O APPOLIGIES . MIDRIFF.

CHAOS LOVES COMICS. SHE IS THROUGHLY PISSED THAT STAN LEE WROTE CAPTAIN AMERICA INCORRECTLY. IN HER MIND CAPTAIN AMERICA CAN ONLY BE A GIRL. AND YES MUCH TO MY DISMAY, CHAOS HAS BOUGHT INTO THE WHOLE PRINCESS CULTURE CRAP DISNEY IS FREAKING SELLING....THANK GOD FOR BRAVE AND MERIDA - WHO ISN'T YOUR TYPICAL PRINCESS. WE DON'T HAVE GOOD ROLE MODLES FOR OUR CHILDREN THAT SET THE EXAMPLE OF WHAT THEIR OPTIONS MIGHT BE... SO I HAVE TO TELL BOTH CHAOS AND HAVOC. GIRLS & BOYS LOVE MATH AND SCIENCE, GIRLS AND BOYS CAN LIKE THE COLOR PINK AND PURPLE, GIRLS AND BOYS CAN PLAY WITH DOLLS AND TRUCKS. GIRLS AND BOYS CAN COOK AND CLEAN. IF I DON'T DO THAT SOCIETY PUTS THEM IN A BOX AND TRIES TO FORCE THEM TO STAY THERE....

OPTIMAL GENDER ROLES EMBRACE THE UNIQUENESS OF THE INDIVIDUAL AND LEVERAGES THE STRENGTHS AND WEAKNESS TO BENEFIT THE TRIBE - REGARDLESS OF WETHER THE INDIVIDUAL IS MALE OR FEMALE.

Gretchen, I'm with you. I've got a boy and a girl and the same issues arose... one could only find pink middrift-baring tops and skimpy bottoms, the other had only camouflage to choose from. We finally solved this issue (and helped the influence of culture, too). We banned shopping in regular stores. Now the entire family (forced initially by budgetary contraints) shops only in op shops (I think you'd call them secondhand shops in the US?). The other thing: I bought a used sewing machine. I did not turn into Martha Stewart but it was useful to hem things, and make alterations on our op shop purchases. Now my daughter, who is about to be 12, cannot fathom why a) everyone wants to look the same or b) why you'd pay $40 for a top when you can buy a cooler one for $3. She still loves the look of high heels, though ... some things are harder to change! Sort of like with following light cycles and temperature and natural food, we found that avoiding the commercial/marketing bombardment really helped the kids carve out their own sense of self. (I'm not saying we're immune - we're not - and they love mainstream films, too, but, like you, I'd had a guts-full of the 'image' companies were pushing on my kids.)

### ealachan — 2012-10-23T23:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-59894>

> **Anna K.;61467 wrote:** If you want to show the cleavage and appreciate them, fine, but why are you surprised when guys stare at you? it's their eyes and they can do what they want with them? They just can't help it, the are big and into your face.. If they are not looking, they are probably gay. so they look and then women get upset and say why are you staring??? well?
>
>
>
> and another point, if everybody is dressed up and you are the only one with the cleavage at work, or a nose ring, or simply look very sexy, or the opposite of sexy - completely covered up in burka, everybody (including women) will look at you initially simply because you are different from the environment we are used to. That doesn't mean you should change, just realize the fact that people will look and accept it.
>
>
>
> and to clarify is a big difference between looking and abusing (verbally or physically).

I think that, for the most part, we're arguing the same side here.

FWIW, I never said I minded that people (men OR women) look at my cleavage. Looking is fine. Judging me as a slut inside their own head is fine too, because one's thoughts are their own. The minute they are moved to open their mouths and give voice to that judgement, actually calling me (or anyone) a slut (or whatever) in a serious / judgemental manner because of the way they're dressed...that's when the problem starts, at least in my world.

In my opinion, there's no shame in being a slut, in liking sex, in liking one's body and wanting to show it off _if that's what one is comfortable with._ What's not ok is someone making someone else feel bad for being themselves, or imposing your will upon them in some way. I wouldn't ever sit here and say "showing cleavage is the best and most appropriate way for women to dress", because I wouldn't ever want a woman to dress in a way that made HER uncomfortable.

Also, I have now read the all-caps rant of Shijin's and I absolutely agree that American society sexualizes children. I think it does deep harm to kids today, in ways that we're possibly not even realizing yet.

I haven't had much luck in second hand/ thrift shops...but I know I should give it a go. my problem is regardless of where I buy my clothes I'm going to end spend at least $50 getting them tailored/hemmed - I've got such large shoulders I need to have the shirts brought in at the waist otherwise I look like I'm wearing a tent. I'd almost rather wait until something goes on a sweet sale say $45 for a nice wool suit, that would regularly be close to $300, and tailor it. Fortunately, I've been shopping in my downstairs closet - yeah for having mulitple sizes in the house! that's free. I think what I was comfortable w/when I was 25 is completely different than where I am today. I was also less secure about myself then too. Whats crazy I was in rocking shape back then and I got less "eyes on me" then than I do now. is that the LR kicking the hormones off? or is it I just don't care about others and I'm happy and self confident now - where I wasn't then. All I can say is I do love getting the "how you doin'?" every now and then at work.... even though I'm all buttoned up and married... big EGO boost.

### Glamazon — 2012-10-27T15:18:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-gender-roles.4429/post-60426>

> **Cavemam;60980 wrote:** [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2220586/Comic-Con-Mandy-Caruso-dressed-racy-costume-recalls-sexual-harassment-fans.html](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2220586/Comic-Con-Mandy-Caruso-dressed-racy-costume-recalls-sexual-harassment-fans.html)

>

>
> Let the arrow throwing begin.

I think we get onto a slippery slope here.

After reading through the whole thread it is interesting to see bias.

Harrassment is never appropriate, sexual or otherwise. People don't deserve to be bullied or harmed for their choices. Blaming people for what happens to them in a public place? Is there an implication or judgment that "because your sexual standards are lower than mine you deserve what you get"? This trods the ground of victim blaming (assigning guilt) and slut shaming (being too sexually available). I can always hear my parents or church saying, "well, what did you expect when you act the way you do?"

I may not agree with people's choices but I will defend their right to have them.

Harassment, especially sexual harassment, is about power not sluttishness or provocative appearance.

Now, get off my lawn!

Yeah, this has been illuminating in helping me see my own biases (thanks ealachan!). I think everybody has the right to dress the way they want to, though I still believe that employers are going to want to have a say when it comes to how you look on the job. But even that has changed a lot over the last few decades and no doubt will continue to evolve.

# Thread: Depression soars as seafood intake drops in the UK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-21T19:42:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59489>

> To offer a window into this paradigm of thinking, I'm going to make reference to a particular constituent of our diet - omega-3 fish oils. Firstly (and famously), there's a strong correlation between a nation's fish consumption and the prevalence of depression \[1\], meaning countries with a high intake of fish (for example, Japan) have much lower rates of depression than countries with a low intake (such as the UK).
>
>
>
> Whilst tantalising, this type of data is mere correlation, subject to any number of 'confounders'. However, the fact that omega-3 fish oils (EPA and DHA) are critical for the structure and function of the brain, and play a role in how neurotransmitters work, does add biological plausibility. Then, we find that patients with depression have lower levels of omega-3 \[2\], and not only that, the lower the level of omega-3, the worse the depression \[3\].

[http://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/glen-matten/depression-food-cases-of-depression-soar-\_b\_1977632.html](http://http://www.huffingtonpost.co.uk/glen-matten/depression-food-cases-of-depression-soar-_b_1977632.html)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-22T00:51:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59538>

I was reading some similar research about New Zealand. They have also looked at selenium levels (especially as our soil is deficient) and found that the south island (which has a very low Pacific population and therefore low amounts of seafood eaten) has a population dangerously deficient in both DHA and selenium (and I suppose therefore iodine too). They did further studies to compare fish oils versus fish and found that while fish oils gave the DHA they didn't successfully give the Se. Another reason to go for the real food. But I'm baffled why I can google this research and docs don't seem to know about it. (We have skyrocketing rates of prematurity here in NZ and they seem to have no clue as to why.)

### Kiera2 — 2012-10-22T00:59:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59541>

> **KiwiLauren;61088 wrote:** But I'm baffled why I can google this research and docs don't seem to know about it. (We have skyrocketing rates of prematurity here in NZ and they seem to have no clue as to why.)

I think it's because most doctors don't consider googling a priority over running their practice. Actually, I think family medical centres should employ a researcher. Then they could distribute a weekly 'hey, guess what I found' newletter.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-22T04:10:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59567>

> **KiwiLauren;61088 wrote:** I was reading some similar research about New Zealand. They did further studies to compare fish oils versus fish and found that while fish oils gave the DHA they didn't successfully give the Se. Another reason to go for the real food. But I'm baffled why I can google this research and docs don't seem to know about it. (We have skyrocketing rates of prematurity here in NZ and they seem to have no clue as to why.)

This is why the area has the highest rate of IBD and crohn's world wide too......just saying.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-22T04:11:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59568>

> **Coriander;61091 wrote:** I think it's because most doctors don't consider googling a priority over running their practice. Actually, I think family medical centres should employ a researcher. Then they could distribute a weekly 'hey, guess what I found' newletter.

No one will pay for good info......that is why it will never happen. Patients need to do it themselves.

### Michael — 2012-10-22T10:09:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/depression-soars-as-seafood-intake-drops-in-the-uk.4437/post-59585>

At least there's a ripple effect, Lauren.

Cast a pebble into a pool, and who can say how long before the ripples subside?

Unfortunately, I suppose that's true of our bad actions, too. Anyway, while looking for something and restricting the date-range in Google I stumbled on a handful of "Are you eating enough fish?" articles on websites. This was shortly after BG13. There is a gently simmering low-level interest in fish anyway, but was the timing co-incidental? I suspect not.

Let each of us keep throwing those pebbles in. Maybe we'll bring about change in ways we've never even thought of.

# Thread: 3 weeks hardcore CT no adaptation.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T13:37:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59593>

Hi,

For the first 2 weeks I did an hour every day at roughly 58F, the 3rd week 45mins every day at roughly 56F. Full torso, arms and legs out.

I still find it takes a lot of will power to complete these sessions and it hasn't gotten any easier since day 1.

I have noticed:

\-My skin stays the same colour throughout apart from sometimes when I get out of the bath a tiny bit will be slightly peachy coloured for about a minute.

\-I am still cold throughout the day (particularly hands and feet) but at night (around 8-9pm) my hands and feet start to get noticeably warmer.

\-It does not take me as long to warm up from baths any more but I never get truly warm.

\-I had slight headache, runny nose and cough early on but these have all more or less gone now.

My body isn't showing any signs of warming up when in the cold. Now I think this could be for a few reasons.

\-Temperature is not low enough, skin needs to be

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-22T14:19:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59595>

no adaptation gets you this.......

### ealachan — 2012-10-22T14:56:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59599>

my only comment is that it's likely going to take a lot longer than three weeks to fix years worth of damage. CT is not a magic bullet.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T15:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59602>

> **Jack Kruse;61159 wrote:** no adaptation gets you this.......

I don't understand Jack.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T15:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59603>

> **ealachan;61166 wrote:** my only comment is that it's likely going to take a lot longer than three weeks to fix years worth of damage. CT is not a magic bullet.

I demand a magic bullet!!!

In all seriousness how long did it take you before the CT sessions stopped being really hard?

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T16:04:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59609>

Sean,

I was doing 4 to 5 days at 53 degrees from Feb. til June and then spotty afterwards. I became a "little" cold adaptive but it was always tough to get in and always took a few minutes to warm up and I could never make it longer than 30 min. before the shivering totally took over. I have a thyroid problem and iirc Jack has mentioned that if you have a t3 issue that it will be hard until you get some of that fixed. Heck, I was still detoxing like crazy after a month. I think some of us have different issues and it is going to take a lot longer than others. Keep CT'ing and working on finding what is all broken and fix it; then I think you will have better results. It is amazing what you will find that needs fixing when you really look. Jack led me with his flashlight and I am still finding things after 1.5 years. Without him, I would not know what I know today and no local doc would have either.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-10-22T16:05:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59610>

Hormone replacement changed CT significantly for me

### Cowboy — 2012-10-22T16:10:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59611>

> **Jack Kruse;61159 wrote:** no adaptation gets you this.......

why does Jack seem like Yoda with some of these lines? ( Yoda from Star Wars, a late 70's sci fi series for the younger crowd)

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T16:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59621>

> **vkiernan;61176 wrote:** Sean,
>
>
>
> I was doing 4 to 5 days at 53 degrees from Feb. til June and then spotty afterwards. I became a "little" cold adaptive but it was always tough to get in and always took a few minutes to warm up and I could never make it longer than 30 min. before the shivering totally took over. I have a thyroid problem and iirc Jack has mentioned that if you have a t3 issue that it will be hard until you get some of that fixed. Heck, I was still detoxing like crazy after a month. I think some of us have different issues and it is going to take a lot longer than others. Keep CT'ing and working on finding what is all broken and fix it; then I think you will have better results. Without him, I would not know what I know today and no local doc would have either.

Thanks vkiernan, yeah I suspect I may have a T3 issue due to the constant cold hands and feet I experience. I gotta admit it's kind of demoralizing to learn that you did this for 4+months with little adaption.

I suspect that my other issues are causing low cold adaption however I do not no the cause of my other issues and the only testing I can get is through my local GP.

I will keep on with the CT but probably nock it down to say 3-4 times a week.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T16:29:20+0300

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T16:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59624>

> **Cowboy;61178 wrote:** why does Jack seem like Yoda with some of these lines? ( Yoda from Star Wars, a late 70's sci fi series for the younger crowd)

I know, right? Confusion!

### ealachan — 2012-10-22T17:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59629>

> **seanb4;61170 wrote:** I demand a magic bullet!!!
>
>
>
> In all seriousness how long did it take you before the CT sessions stopped being really hard?

It doesn't really matter much, because I am not you, and the process is not linear. You may have some sort of serious underlying issue that I don't have, or vice-versa. Trying to compare your experience to other peoples' in order to figure out whether you're getting any benefit is bound to frustrate us.

And for what it's worth, CT in water is still hard for me, but it's mentally hard, not physically hard. It's a mental fight with myself to get off my ass and run the cold bath, but once I get in, I'm fine.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T17:07:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59632>

> **seanb4;61188 wrote:** Thanks vkiernan, yeah I suspect I may have a T3 issue due to the constant cold hands and feet I experience. I gotta admit it's kind of demoralizing to learn that you did this for 4+months with little adaption.
>
>
>
> I suspect that my other issues are causing low cold adaption however I do not no the cause of my other issues and the only testing I can get is through my local GP.
>
>
>
> I will keep on with the CT but probably nock it down to say 3-4 times a week.

I would not drop back on the sessions. Keep hardcore at it like you have been. As for testing, you will have to do some on your own if you are going to get to the bottom of it. I have spent $1000 over the last couple of months and you would not believe what it has told me. I have good ins. but it doesn't cover these things so I have to do it myself. Have you had a consult with Jack? If not, you really need to start there. He has led me down the right path without question. My doc has never even heard of the tests I had done. How sad is that?? Unfortunately, because of that she doesn't know how to interpret them either. But, I do have Jack and he keeps leading me.

Not demoralyzing to me, hard to take - yes; but, I know that I'm pretty damn broken and it is going to take a lot longer time. It did help me in many ways though so it was not all for not. It is hard to see others making progress when you are not. That really does tell you something though; like you need some major testing to find out why!!! Can't move forward until you know what is all wrong. No matter what you think, it doesn't mean a thing until you get tested and find out the truth!

### shah78 — 2012-10-22T17:22:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59634>

Dear sean, you seem to have fallen prey to the neolithic idea that suffering is a virtue. (See Judeo-Christianity) No self respecting Paleo hominid or Pliocenic cold water mammal would put up with such torture!. I bagged the hour sessions and hours of "aftercold "long ago. I did two thirty minute sessions daily this past summer,then reduced to two twenty minute sessions in New England fall conditions, and now I do two thirty to forty minute Florida fall sessions. I imagine I will be back to twenty minute sessions once "winter" hits Florida in December. Let me repeat, voluntary suffering is a SO NEOLITHIC! Be easier on yourself. Please!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-22T17:26:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-59639>

> **seanb4;61190 wrote:** Thats very interesting to note. I will try to get some thyroid tests from the old GP.
>
>
>
> Would you be able to just take amour and see what happens without testing?

You really need testing for thyroid. It's extremely important that you know what is going on in your body before you add drugs. If you don't need it you can go hyper and the symptoms of that are miserable. I highly recommend against it! Get a FT3, FT4, TSH, and antibodies. Your GP should be able to do this.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-22T17:52:11+0300

### QuantumRadical — 2012-10-29T07:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60681>

> **Anna K.;62310 wrote:** I like that a lot!

On the other hand, from an evolutionary perspective, voluntary suffering is a non-concept. When it gets cold, it's cold and you have to live with it. The neolithic thought is that we can make ourselves warm anytime we want, and mostly we can. To make the conceptual switch to an evolutionary perspective requires us to reconsider what we label as "voluntary suffering" when in fact it is merely reverting to what nature "intended." I agree that it is a neolithic thought that "suffering is a virtue" but is getting cold really a "voluntary suffering?" or is it just how we want to label it so we can rationalize it away, in our own neolithic way? Food for thought--and that would be seafood!

### shah78 — 2012-10-29T08:08:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60682>

Getting cold is not suffering. but you have to get out of the tub sometime! I choose, and advise Sean,to get out sooner, rather than later, especially since he was the one complaining. Same as the gym. You have to leave the gym sometimes. I choose to leave after six to eight minutes.,rather than after two hours..... Groucho Marx said the same thing to the women with the six kids who "loved her husband". Groucho mentioned that he loved his cigars, but took them out of his mouth sometimes. .

### QuantumRadical — 2012-10-29T19:12:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60748>

> **Anna K.;62432 wrote:** Randy, just to clarify, I agree with the concept of being cold adapted and being exposed to cold on a regular basis, but not sure 3 or 4 or 2 hours a day are needed for most people, so I just do 20-25 min 2xtimes a week.

Absolutely no disagreement. Wasn't trying to say that longer is better or anything like that. Just commenting on how we tend to label things--because we can. We should only do CT as long as each of us feel that it is effective, whether that is 2 minutes or two hours, or even not at all. Cold is still COLD!!

### freesia — 2012-10-29T22:33:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60786>

Sean I had this phase (couple of months) before I was comfortably adapted where I had more than clear-cut adaptation going on. I had extra detox because of the CT (and 23and me results showed how that could be a real problem in my case).

I figured out that unless I paced myself carefully, I had a kind of hangover going into each new CT session which meant I really ended up feeling like I was going backwards rather than forwards....

I got over that phase and am definitely adapted, and never felt better.

Just mentioning to show you there's not just one way to adaptation, and I agree...suffering is _not_ what it's about.

Save your suffering until you're optimal and can laugh it off

### shah78 — 2012-10-29T23:13:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60796>

Dear freeia, How does that 23andme result show a detox -CTconnection? I love CT ,but after 200 sessions I still feel I'm detoxing whenever I go into the low- mid forties.

### freesia — 2012-10-30T03:09:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-60858>

It doen't necessarily show a CT connection but mine did point to potential problems with estrogen metabolism, Glutathione peroxidase and superoxide dismutase. Anything stimulating exta detox is going to be hard on me. CT stimulated extra detox in the beginning...and I felt like crap.

DR K may correct me if I'm wrong  but once cold adapted and CT'ing, Glutathione peroxidase, as well as other important detox related enzymes are permanently upregulated.

It's like building up a long neglected muscle I guess...got to go at the right pace so you don't injure it or stress it.

Hope that makes sense.

### seanb4 — 2012-10-31T17:52:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/3-weeks-hardcore-ct-no-adaptation.4446/post-61097>

# Thread: Let's talk canned fish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 86
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-10-22T17:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/post-59640>

So I just don't believe canned fish is worth the effort.

I know "jack said" etc, but everything I look at indicated the high temps needed for canning would effectively bastardize everything good about it, except protein molecules.

discuss?

QUESTION EVERYTHING!

### Michael — 2012-10-22T17:53:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/post-59651>

My feeling is that fresh would be best, but I don't eat that way. I don't always have the time to get it or cook it. (I get 30 minutes for lunch.)

Actually, I think best would be if one could pull it out the river and cook it on the bank rather than going to a fishmonger.

I think something of the same as regards vegetables. If I had a garden or allotment I'd grow high quality organic produce using heirloom varieties. I'd go out and pick them minutes before eating them. I'd have fruit in proper fruit cages, and I'd allow it to ripen on the plant before picking it. (For supermarkets, they pick 'em unripe and allow them to "ripen" in storage.)

The gap between what most of us can do and what would be ideal seems not negligible. The same for how every man, woman and child would have eaten in the best pre-modern environment.

I do the best I can. I shop almost every day as it is. I have to cut some corners. If people can do better than what I do, I'd say go for it.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-22T18:31:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/post-59659>

> **Michael;61218 wrote:** My feeling is that fresh would be best, but I don't eat that way. I don't always have the time to get it or cook it. (I get 30 minutes for lunch.)
>
>
>
> Actually, I think best would be if one could pull it out the river and cook it on the bank rather than going to a fishmonger.
>
>
>
> I think something of the same as regards vegetables. If I had a garden or allotment I'd grow high quality organic produce using heirloom varieties. I'd go out and pick them minutes before eating them. I'd have fruit in proper fruit cages, and I'd allow it to ripen on the plant before picking it. (For supermarkets, they pick 'em unripe and allow them to "ripen" in storage.)
>
>
>
> The gap between what most of us can do and what would be ideal seems not negligible. The same for how every man, woman and child would have eaten in the best pre-modern environment.
>
>
>
> I do the best I can. I shop almost every day as it is. I have to cut some corners. If people can do better than what I do, I'd say go for it.

Ok, let me try again - perhaps up in Vancouver this read differently

Other than "Jack said", I see nothing but examples that all the fishy goodness is destroyed in the canning process and it's only contribution is it's a fatty carb-free protein food. People seem to be approaching it from the Jack-stated-view of "all fish is better than everything else"

Not to mention the high heat very well may oxidize and make the fat in the fish inflammatory. People take such care to avoid rancid and oxydized oils - what makes canned fish end up as a net positive?

I am questioning that. If all the fishy goodness is destroyed in canning, then why does Jack say it's better than all non seafood foodstuffs.

The more you know, the better you do!

### Michael — 2012-10-22T19:12:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/post-59663>

Well that's what I thought. I'm not saying anyome has to agree with any of it.

### Inger — 2012-10-22T19:52:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/post-59670>

I think Jack does not mean it like that...hmmm. You need to feel he's spirit, then you understand. It is important to not get stuck in phrases people say, this is just my own Nr.1.

Words.. they are just words.. they can be doing harm if people get stuck in them and does not see the spirit.. I am getting more and more aware of this issue.

I do not eat canned fish ever except in emergency situations. Why, when fresh is so much better? Jack says fresh and raw is best. Everyone has to choose for themselves how "good" one wants to get. I am not doing the best I could eather, I take it quite easy I know things I could still do better..but I never complain if I am not seeing so fast progress..lol cause then.. I also know too well why! There is always something to improve that is for sure.

We can go as slow as we want just no need to complain then..huh..

I am seeing it like that, Jack is not really recommending canned fish, but he tells it is much better then some other common Paleo foods!

What he really recommends.. is MHS and such..lol Cavemam, have you tried that? Since you kind of take Jacks recommandation to your heart it seems?

The most practical thing to take for lunch ever, is fresh raw salmon cut into pieces and then add fresh grated ginger, a little raw honey, applecider vinegar / lime juice / vinegar, seasalt, chopped raw onions and maybe cilantro or dill if desired. I often make it ready in the morning for lunch at work. I add chopped dried seaweeds too. I just put it into a glass jar and eat directly from the jar when lunchtime. Boah... it is totally delish...!!! And makes one so nicely satiated! I use to make it from one pound salmon and I usually eat all in one setting.

And hmmm.. one more thing.. I would not take the words of anyone without thinking of myself. I would not feel too great from most of the recipes in Jack cookbook..lol I just feel best from raw and quite plain foods. That is why I do not buy it (I might if I had money just to support him for sure. I am so very thankful for all his help!). I need fishhead smoothies and such to feel real good - that broken is this lady..huh! I would never go and eat canned fish because "Jack says so". He is no Guru and I am sure he want not to be eather. He want us to think ourselves.

I am rather thinking he does not say loud all strange foods he thinks is healthy because not to offend people. People get offended and escape when it goes too hard, like the MHS. That is sad but true. Jack is wise and does not do it that way because he knows he can help more with beeing a bit easier on the food. That is my guess.

### kathylu — 2012-10-22T20:45:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-canned-fish.4449/post-59681>

> **Cavemam;61246 wrote:** so I ask the crowd - anybody got any info one way or the other on if the canning process destorys the nutrients and overheats the oils, thus oxidizing them and rendering them inflammatory?
>
>
>
> Jack? What info did you have to make your conclusion? If someone was going to take things based only on "saw it in a blog post from Jack", they probably would never have made it this far in their journey (to be reading and interacting on these forums).
>
>
>
> Here I go again, questions questions. . .

I took a quick look for some data on this topic and can't find a whole lot. To summarize, "normal" cooking methods, i.e. poaching, grilling, steaming, baking do not appreciably alter the fatty acids or phospholipids in fresh fish. Frying is at such a high temp that damage can occur, so best to avoid that. Canning is a relatively low heat process, certainly less heat that baking, so I wouldn't worry too much. Oils become "rancid" not so much from heat but oxidation, which takes exposure to oxygen and time, though heat can certainly speed up the process. Some oils are quite resistant to oxidation, coconut for instance and it is true that fish oils (O3's) are subject to faster oxidation. However, there is very little oxygen in canned products, so again, I don't think it's a huge concern. My opinion is that the nutritional quality of canned fish would be quite similar to cooked fish.

However, I agree with you...canned fish certainly does not taste as good as freshly prepared fish! I do use it for convenience though.

Oh, how I love the internet! Here's a description of fish canning...most fish is processed at 121 degrees centigrade for about 10 minutes. That's rather gentle heat, less than steaming, baking or broiling.
