# Thread: What is the main and backup fuel sources for gum tissue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Glen PDQ — 2012-10-30T02:27:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/post-60844>

Anyone know for a fact the main fuel source of gum tissue when the body is NOT in ketosis and liver glycogen stores are still sufficiently available and carb intake is not low?

Anyone know what the backup fuel source for gum tissue is when the body is chugging along during ketosis assuming the body is getting sufficient protein and fat but liver glycogen stores are low/depleted?

The answer may seem obvious, but keep in mind that during (ketosis/ketogenic diet) the brain gets the majority of the ketones, and the muscles, as an example, switch to burning fat instead of glucose, sparing the ketones for the brain.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-30T02:31:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/post-60847>

### Glen PDQ — 2012-10-30T03:50:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/post-60865>

### John Ottman — 2026-04-16T18:50:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/post-371230>

After 10 yrs of very routine dentist visits for cleaning and checkups, and repairs, I left my dentist in search of a new one. Found one about a year later. I was not in a rush as my teeth had been good for a couple years now.

This Dentist did a full exam lots of pictures, couple x-rays and during the the gum line check found 3 deep pockets around 7mm on my molars - the side where it doesn't have a tooth next to it. My wisdom teeth were removed 25 years. The rest of my pockets are 2's and 3's. Hopefully I'm explaining the location of deep pockets in in a way that makes sense. Boy did it hurt when they stuck that tool in those molar gums.

This was pretty shocking to me, and disappointing to say the least. I felt I had everything under control and especially so after the past 2 years of living the way Jack teaches.

I have no doubt that before coming across Jack's work my previous life style had impact on this. Is see up above where Jack said periodontal disease is due to low Co Enz Q10 levels... is 400 mg of CoQ10 still recommended along side everything he teaches or has there been more data to suggest other protocols?

I see the sunrise daily, ground, get mid day light an evening light. All in all about 2-3 hrs a day, and weekends much more. t. I do CT and eat clean. Limit RF and other tech and light position best I can living in San Francisco, CA

Thank you

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-19T03:38:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/post-371473>

Chewing mastic gum also is a hack for perio diseases. But you need the hard resin.

### John Ottman — 2026-04-21T07:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-main-and-backup-fuel-sources-for-gum-tissue.4565/post-371674>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=371473)
>
> Chewing mastic gum also is a hack for perio diseases. But you need the hard resin.

Thanks Jack! I appreciate the reply. Been chewing it since the podcast with Alexis dropped. That was a fantastic podcast.

These three deep pockets really threw me for a surprise. My teeth have gotten so much better(whiter) in the past two years since following the science you teach. Guessing the deep pockets starting a while back and wasn't caught by my previous provider

# Thread: I wanted my life back
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael — 2012-10-30T13:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60887>

I'm just listening to Abel James' "Fat Burning Man" podcast. This week he has a lady on as a guest who was pretty much wrecking her life with a paleo eating plan till she woke up to what she was doing and backed off.

The plan, which I hadn't heard of before, is called Lean Gains and involves deliberate fasting and bingeing. It sounds very unhealthy - particularly for women whose hormones seem so much more complex than men's. I know "intermittent fasting" is something of a fetish on the fringe of the paleo world. I don't see anything wrong with skipping the occasional meal when one's not hungry, but these plans of forced starving and eating sound foolish to me.

Be that as it may, it was disastrous for this woman. Her sleep went first. Then she broke out in acne. Then she found her sense of satiety was destroyed, so that the binges, which she at first had to force down, eventually only stoked her hunger. Finally, she seems to have developed a range of psychological problems, including secretive behaviour, what sounds like a mild form of paranoia, and a range of obsessive behaviours, such as weighing herself multiple times a day.

She must have totally screwed her biochemistry.

She says that her having owned up publicly has antagonized some women who do this. She's says they have a website on which they exchange photos of themselves with distended sromachs, when in the bingeing phase. They're now posting hate material about her - which she sensibly doesn't read. It all sounds very creepy. Maybe Abel should've posted it on Halloween.

The lady said: "I just wanted my life back". I hope she's getting that.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-30T14:06:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60890>

I never got the IF fanatics, nor the followers of lean gains... intuitively it never made sense... when I tried to IF 1x/month before starting the LR - I was always hungry and never satisfied... I tried it 2x and decided it wasn't for me.... I seriously believe that there are ppl so desperate to loose weight NOW - that they'll do anything to do it... even sacrificing their health.

While I loved MDA - what bugged me was how every one pushed IFing, and Carb cycling or counting calories on me - b/c it would fix my weight struggles... only 1 person said - I know how to fix you - I fixed myself, I've been where you are - and that was JAck... pretty compelling... what's crazy is that b/c it wasn't fast sudden success that some ppl see in IFing, or Carb cycling - they say its bunk... especially the young xfit guys... but there were women who derided anything that didn't give them license to eat how every they wanted - IMO lean gains/carb cycling is just an excuse to eat foods that isn't good for you...

### Jack Kruse — 2012-10-30T14:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60892>

That is why the people at MDA, PH, and Wolf's site had issues........not everyone is easily fixable and this mixed message hurt lots of people. What happens every friday on those sites are not everyone's results.......contrary to what they say in their books and podcasts. That is clinical medicine 101. Patients want explanations why.........not BS.

### shah78 — 2012-10-30T15:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60902>

Dear Micheal, I commend you for your first 458 posts. Great out of the box "stuff" This one is your first stinker! The woman admits to eating bread, oreos and bagels to do her carbo loads and having/using a SCALE many times a day.Leangains is not /never been,Paleo. She blames Mark Sisson for touting 16 hour fasts. She,(and then YOU ) refer to this as "starvation". Hardly. To their credit these" fasting mavens" say women should do 14 hour fasts instead.......The woman has an eating disorder. She had one before she started Leangains, or what she thought was leangains. .....Personally I think Leangains is "way too Rube Goldberg" for my tastes. However I do 16 hour fasts whenever it feels comfortable. (Maybe 15 days a month). When I want to eat at nine am I eat my Salmon/carbs. When I'm not hungry I wait till eleven or twelve..... This is "reality internet". She is the "SNOOKY" of the Pleosphere. I smell Theatre.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60903>

### Michael — 2012-10-30T15:27:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60905>

> **Shijin13;62597 wrote:** While I loved MDA - what bugged me was how every one pushed IFing, and Carb cycling or counting calories on me - b/c it would fix my weight struggles...

Yeah, Mark himself is very sane and rather middle of the road, but some curious and extreme fashions wash across those forums. I think the IF tide has gone out again now - probably because enough people have tried it and found it didn't work.

But I can remember when people were regularly posting things like "eating breakfast is conventional wisdom". AFAIK "conventional wisdom" is a dietary term coined by Taubes to represent what people who were too quick to believe their own hypotheses considered was wise. Mark Sisson obviously liked the term and bandied it around quite often. By the time it had reached the forum posters it meant "whatever dietary practice I dislike or don't currently believe in". It all gets a bit precious ([sense 2](http://http://oxforddictionaries.com/definition/english/precious)) in the end. I mean most people believe it's wise to look before crossing the road. There's nothing wrong with many things most people believe.

The other two strategies are still in full swing. Calorie-counting is a hard creature to kill - it's like the many-headed hydra in the myth. My understanding is that there's data going as far back as the 1950s (Keckwick and Pawan) and extending into the present to show that things are not as simple as that, but it's simply impossible to get many people to discard the idea.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-30T20:38:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60978>

I tried intermittent fasting last fall for a few months, as a last ditch effort to have my cake and eat it too and not blow up. I tried not eating until 4:30 pm everyday. But then by 4:30 I was ready to eat everything in the house, starting with all the junk food that I had "earned." Then, if I needed to make real headway, I would fast longer. 24 hours without food would drop the scale 3.5 pounds. 48 hours would take off another 3, and then if I was really really dedicated, 72 hours would take off another 2. That's 8.5 pounds in 3 days. Hard to beat!! Except that the weight is mostly water, and comes back on with a vengeance, and then there was the feeling of being perpetually cold no matter what I wore (thanks to thyroid hormones being shut down to RT3), the exhaustion, the unending dry mouth/unquensable thirst, and then the shame of being unable to control oneself. Actually, having written all that, I'd say it's coming close to the description of an eating disorder.

### Michael — 2012-10-30T20:50:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60979>

> **bigknitwit;62689 wrote:** Actually, having written all that, I'd say it's coming close to the description of an eating disorder.

Yeah, what the woman on the podcast felt, too - although she couldn't see that when she was swept up into the mindset surrounding it. She was very lucky to have Abel and George as friends to help her out again.

### shah78 — 2012-10-30T22:10:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60991>

I say the same thing on every subject it seems. Your CT should be challenging and pleasant, with little to no suffering. Your weight lifting should be challenging and pleasant, with little to no suffering. Your IF should be even less challenging and pleasant, with little to no suffering. What is with all this suffering? Have I missed the "Thou shall suffer when curing your ailments" eleventh commandment? Way too Neolithically Judeo-Christian for a "devout" evolutionist like myself.

### AKMan — 2012-10-30T22:49:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-60995>

If you could find and afford enough seafood and seaweed to eat that, and only that, every day...there would be no overeating, undereating or a need to count calories or IF. It would be very, very hard to overeat on fish and seaweed.

However, I doubt many are doing that. Most of us also eat eggs, cheese, veggies, butter, pork, beef, chicken, fruit, starch, and other things. It's all this stuff that makes a need for IF, calorie counting, crossfitting, cyclical ketogenic diets--all that.

I love IF. For me, IF means skip breakfast. It's very natural for me. I get up at 6am, drink a little coffee, go to work, take an exercise break, and eat a nearly perfect epi-paleo lunch of raw salmon or oysters, canned oysters, or cooked fish. Then don't eat again til supper, which is paleo, but not epi-paleo most days.

I don't recommend this for everyone, but for me it works great!

### TribalSpice — 2012-10-31T03:01:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-wanted-my-life-back.4574/post-61020>

Let me start with a quote out of "Good Health in the 21st Century" by Dr Carole Hungarford, which incidently if you don't own a copy, seriously think about it next time you are on booko.

\*

"The biochemistry of breakfast

Insulin resistance is the end result of of continuously high levels of circulating insulin. \*In the West, we rarely fast. \*Breakfast is meant to be the breaking of a fast. During that fast, our insulin levels should have fallen, stayed low until all the food is absorbed from the stomach, has entered and left the circulation, and has been distributed to \*the cells of the liver, fat and muscles. \*During this time, the cells of those organs have minimal exposure to insulin and thus maintain insulin sensitivity.

\*

Suppose two individuals eat the same high-calorie diet. One is a grazer and spreads the meal across the waking hours of the day; the other eats in two sittings, with a long gap between the last meal of the day and the first meal of the following day. \*The first individual develops insulin resistance and a fatty liver, but the second stands a good chance of avoiding these complications. Why?

\*

Through out human evolution, hormonal influences developed to tie in with various circadian and diurnal cycles. Hormonal patterns and the impact of various activities can now be studied by laboratory measurement. One of the hormones of interest is DHEA or dehydroepiandrosterone.

\*

DHEA is made of cholesterol, and it is a precursor to all the eostrogens and testosterones we produce. Taken as a medication, it is a banned drug for Olympic athletes because it increases their performance and is deemed to give them an unfair advantage. \*It is also known as a longevity hormone. \*Pathological states aside, DHEA levels are an indicator of the health of the individual. \*According to Ray Kearny, an immunologist from the University of Sydney (and a member of the delayed-breakfast brigade), contracting our eating hours by having a late start to the day improves DHEA output and appears to reduce cancer risk.

\*

We produce DHEA in opposition to growth hormone and the glucocorticoids. High levels are reached in the early hours of the morning, as these other hormone levels fall. \*The rise in DHEA production is stimulated by bright sunlight, so it makes sense to be out of doors then. \*With our first meal, further glucocorticosteroids are produced and the DHEA surge is suppressed. \*The longer breakfast is delayed, the longer the DHEA peak lasts and the greater the overall output.

\*

Early morning urine has a high concentration of DHEA. It is probably for this reason that some monks drink a glass of their own urine first thing in the morning. (Should we suggest this to the Olympians?)

\*

The health benefits of the overnight fast include both the restoration of insulin sensitivity and the optimisation of DHEA production. \*Less quirky than consuming odd drinks and easier than under-going long fasts is simply delaying breakfast until hunger asserts itself. \*Take a morning jog to complete the picture.

\*

Further support for 'no breakfast' came from a report in The Lancet that normal children were unaffected or performed better without breakfast.

\*

This all happened as a matter of course for our primitive ancestors. They ate at dusk, and finishing the meal around the campfire. \*Without artificial lighting they were finished with food relatively early in the evening. \*Although they arose with the sun, there is good reason to believe that some time elapsed before they ate. \*Stored food would be eaten by small animals, or would attract large animals to which humans were also prey. \*Almost all food was fresh (with all the attendant health benefits), whole and acquired work. \*They had to find, even hunt, kill and cook breakfast before they could eat. \*Maybe they snacked on a few nuts and berries as they went. \*You work first, you ate second. \*You burnt energy, and then you replaced it. \*We do the reverse. \*We eat first, preemptively refueling in case our supplies run low. \*The price is called metabolic syndrome.

So in short, we really aren't giving our body enough "down-time" from insulin or enough time with peak DHEA. \* DHEA as mentioned above is the building block for all our sex hormones thus playing a huge role in fertility, but it is much more than that. \*DHEA helps keep our cells "young", it plays a part in cognition, muscle strength, your immune system, sex drive, cardiovascular health.. the list goes on."

I started officially doing IF about 14 months ago, my weekly debilitating headaches disappeared within 2 weeks. I wasn't doing it to lose weight I am 57kg and 169cm (126lb, 5ft 7) and have been for a long time. The transition to IF wasn't hard, actually it was better because it really is how my circadian rhythms told me to eat. Now I get hungry at 11am and again at 6pm. I eat until I am comfortably satiated, I have never counted calories and always preferred fats and protein over carbs anyway. It sounds like this woman's metabolism was totally stuffed to start with and all the sugar and carbs just stuffed it even more.

# Thread: Sleep worse during full moons

### freshveggies — 2012-10-30T15:44:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-worse-during-full-moons.4576/post-60908>

Does anyone know why my sleep gets worse during a full moon? I get very little sleep during this time of the month.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-30T15:56:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-worse-during-full-moons.4576/post-60910>

We didn't want to say, but since the person suffering usually has no idea. . . A rare and old world condition called Lycanthrope. If you had done the 23andme, it would tell you - I think it's a snip on the MTHRF 215467.

Or, you are doing very well - and you SHOULD be sleeping less as it's much brighter out at night. You should be out foraging for shrooms and all that kind of stuff.

Happy Halloweeeeen!

### ealachan — 2012-10-30T15:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-worse-during-full-moons.4576/post-60911>

I'm guessing it's the extra light from the moon? That's what does it for me. I have to block my bedroom window from about a week after the new moon until a week before it, because it's just too damn bright in our room to sleep well, otherwise.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-05T06:39:40+0200

Remember the moon controls our tilt at 23.5 degrees. This tilt does two things........give us seasons and circadian cycles.....but its gravity also pulls on the surface water and gives us a tidal bulge.............but it also gives us a magna bulge..........and this alters the native EMF in places where the mantle is high to the crust. If you happen to be sensitive to it you wont sleep as well. This is why the moon has always been associated with bad mojo.........it is a magnetoreception perception. # Thread: Freezing your eyeballs for better vision
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T04:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61031>

I have noticed the last few weeks some vision problems. Like I was squinting like an oldie, and tonight I had to drive and I noticed the nightime glare etc was back.

Early this year, I had noted how notably my vision had improved. Night vision had done a 180, and I kept braggin to everyone how my magic paleo diet (and cutting diet soda) had improved my eyes.

As I was driving and trying not to wreck tonight - I realized one factor that has changed.

For many months I was doing 20 min daily face dunks in ice water with a snorkel. I was able to open my eyes in the 29-35 degree icy waters. I stopped over the summer, and since I've picked up CT this fall, I've just been doing baths. No face stuff.

Coincidence or evidence of the supernatural. Could icing my eyeballs effect my vision? I wonder if I could freeze half my head/ears and improve my hearing.

### Marie — 2012-10-31T04:55:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61037>

I've definitely noticed an AMAZING change in my vision after a quick dip in the Pacific Ocean, so I'd be very interested in whether I should be using those frozen eye masks and a little mg to fix my old eyeballs...

### Melinda — 2012-10-31T05:46:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61040>

Very interesting theory. I had really bad headlight dazzle a few years ago and now I'm OK and I attributed it to getting rid of sunglasses during the daytime...I had auto-darkening lenses in my Rx glasses. I embraced the sunlight so now I can take the headlights. But I also did lots of face dunking which I haven't done for some time. I'll start tomorrow and see if my vision gets better.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-31T06:20:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61041>

From one of JK's blogs....

For men or women, you might notice your own free testosterone level is really low on a high Vitamin D3 supplemented level, and your libido is lagging badly or your body comp is not coming around. Or you might try reading in low light conditions and see how you do. Many will notice that they can’t do it well any longer. If you listened to my recent Nov 2011 podcast on [JoanneUnleashed.com](http://http://joanneunleashed.com/) you might have wondered why I read in low light and red light levels during my own reset. I was checking myself for signs that I was pushing my Vitamin D3 status too high. Affected individuals are unable to distinguish images in low levels of illumination. Vitamin A deficiency affects vision by inhibiting the production of rhodopsin, the eye pigment responsible for sensing low light situations. Rhodopsin is found in the retina and is composed of retinal, which is an active form of vitamin A, and opsin a protein made by the retina. Basically, if you “over do” the use of Vitamin D3, you will not make enough rhodopsin (from a relative Vitamin A deficiency), and you will suffer from night blindness. I pick this up in patients who notice night time visual driving issues. Most people won’t read in low light levels like I do to assess themselves for this issue. However, you can use this simple test too. Their doctors usually send them to the ophthalmologist, but most are cured once they tweak their diets and add back some vitamin A containing foods.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61062>

I pick this up in patients who notice night time visual driving issues. Most people wonâ€™t read in low light levels like I do to assess themselves for this issue. However, you can use this simple test too. Their doctors usually send them to the ophthalmologist, but most are cured once they tweak their diets and add back some vitamin A containing foods.

Great Find KL! I love your posts. Come on over for coffee after you drop the kids off at school!

So, guess what I've been doing for about 3 weeks now? Super Vitamin D3 Dosing! My levels have been tanked and unbudging since I started checking back in April. All summer I did major sun + oral and sublingual dosing. My level didn't move even one point.

So after doing the best google research and consulting with random people on the internet, I started an experiment of dosing 30-50K iu a day by lady-parts.

So, looks like it might be time to check and see if Vit-D went up. Probably my serum levels are stagnant, and all I'm doing is sucking out all the Vit-A in my foods to process the D overload, and causing more harm.

I'm 99% sure that type of supplementation is at worse useless, and at best bad and sets something out of balance - I really think this, but yet I keep trying different things. I guess if anything, each FAIL teaches me lots. I'm tempted to start in on Vit-A, but then there we go farther down that path of unbalancing things and unintended consequense.

I will start face dunking again to test on that (no downsides to that!)

Unfortunately it's going into winter now and my veggie consumption is dropping rapido!

SO - I think I will keep going on the D3 Vag experiement until I can get my blood drawn (it's test T time again, too) and perhaps supplement the K&A until then.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T15:15:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61068>

> **Nonchalant;62780 wrote:** Cavemam you opened your eyes during the face dunks?!? I never considered that.
>
> Currently in my ice water baths when I turn over on my tummy I also dunk my face a few times. And I also immerse my neck and (closed) mouth for a good long time, to tighten up the neck even more. And I'm sure to get my underarms in for the same reason.

I really was! My method for face is big bowl of ice water at around 32 degrees and head in with snorkel so I could just sit there and veg and listen to a book or podcast. I found it super duper relaxing. I quit over summer - but I have been thinking I should do it again for several reasons. . .

65 degree baths seem to be doing nothing by way of cold adaptation. I don't think they are really preparing me for "deep ct". I was hoping not to drag ice all around the world this year and just go with what nature provides. . .but right now it's just an obnoxious chore and I'm not finding time for it as much as I know I should.

I am a big fan of CT, don't get me wrong - but it is NOT easier and less stressful to get accomplished than the old school chronic cardio stuff. At least right now that is what my experience is. I think I could probably head to the elliptical at the gym easier than go buy ice and sit my ass down for 45 mins and do nothing but freeze.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-10-31T18:01:34+0200

I'm feeling the same thing here!

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T18:12:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61101>

> **Cavemam;62784 wrote:**
>
> I think I could probably head to the elliptical at the gym easier than go buy ice and sit my ass down for 45 mins and do nothing but freeze.

But you don't freeze while you're doing CT. When you were dunking your face, you were vegging out, relaxing. Not freezing. I don't think I could stand CT if I felt I was freezing for more than the first 3-4 minutes. I often get sleepy, laying in the tub. Very relaxing.

You could wait until your tap water gets down to 55F or so. Probably won't be long, where you are.

### Lyndra — 2012-11-02T01:25:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61442>

My vision got much better last spring/summer. I was doing a lot of new things, including BHRT and 20-30 minutes of CT per day. I stopped the CT for the summer and my vision has declined. Since I'm still on the BHRT, I can only attribute the changes to CT. For me, it wasn't necessary to submerge my face as I was just soaking in the tub.

### ljbprrfmof — 2012-11-04T02:03:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezing-your-eyeballs-for-better-vision.4586/post-61885>

> **Cavemam;63192 wrote:** Welllllll, to clarify - didn't mean I was literally freezing as in frozen body and uncomfortable. . .but contained and bored while freezing, and not feeling like it's accomplishing as much as that time could by me in other ways. I think. Mebby. Dunno!

I noticed my CT showers seeming to get shorter and shorter. I attribute part of it to boredom and another part to a newer showerhead and diverter valve. In my old shower, I was able to focus water on my back and chest at the same time because the diverter valve was nicely worn. Not so in my new shower. It is working perfectly. New landlord is a plumber.

# Thread: I just made the weirdest drinking chocolate..
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-10-31T13:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-61051>

I have made smoothies from soaked seaweeds only, lately a few times.. and I was about to make some for dessert today after eating my raw moose burgers. Then it all of a sudden popped into my head, I could try to make a chocolate from it?

I added organic 100% cocoa powder and a teaspoon of wild raw honey, the soaked kombu and fresh water into my mixer. Let it run for a while, and it turned into a nice smoothie. Hmmm it tasted great! Really great! I drank it all... I figured if I add a raw eggyolk and a spoonful of coconuoil it might get even better.. so I made one more smoothie with kombu, raw honey, cacao, a yolk, coconutoil. OMG that was even better! Now my stomach is sooo full of seaweed..lol But I feel great so far.

Now, I could never believe it will turn out so delicious. Something to try if one is not yet so into seaweeds. Ok this would be more of a summer drink maybe because there are a few carbs for sure.

### Michael — 2012-10-31T14:02:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-61053>

You surprised me, Inger. I thought there was going to be at least one raw fishhead in there for sure.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T15:11:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-61067>

So when are you going to publish your cookbook, Inger?

### Inger — 2012-10-31T15:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-61070>

@ Michael..lol No way! I do not like the taste of fish heads at all, that is why I always hold my nose drinking them..

But this I wanted to be delicious you know, and a fishhead would have ruined the taste I am quite sure.. Feel free to try though..lol

Do not forget to report how it turned out..

@ Nonchalant.. I am not sure anyone would want a cookbook with fishhead smoothies, raw lambshearts and seaweed chocolate..lol

But hey.. you got me to think... one could never know!

### Michael — 2012-10-31T15:46:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-61075>

Maybe one could do something like 8 fl oz coconut milk, 1 tbs cocoa powder, 1 tsp raw honey, and some soaked seaweed.

How briny does it taste? I don't know how other flavourings would go with that seawater taste. Vanilla escence probably wouldn't work. Maybe some spices would. Cinnamon? Chilli?

### Inger — 2012-10-31T16:05:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-61078>

You know Michael, it tasted not briny at all! IDK if it was that I had soaked and so removed the saltiest taste, because I used new water, not the saltier soaking water wich I love to drink as it is. It still was a tiny touch of saltiness there but in a nice way, I used to add salt to cookies too! You can buy chocolate here with salt in it too..

To use coconut milk..mmm.. good idea!

Cinnamon and chilli.. sounds cool! I need to try that! Wow!

Tummy still feeling very happy after the all the kombu...  I guess my tummy starts to get used to digesting seaweeds. I had the feeling I got some bloating in the beginning... for sure I cannot know if it was the seaweeds but I think so.

### chocolate — 2012-11-13T15:32:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-just-made-the-weirdest-drinking-chocolate.4590/post-64008>

[http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/seaweed-extracts-inhibit-gum-disease-bacteria](http://http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/seaweed-extracts-inhibit-gum-disease-bacteria)

> Researchers from Korea's Silla University have determined that many seaweed extracts inhibit bacteria known to cause gingivitis and gum disease.
>
>
>
> The researchers tested 57 different species of seaweeds harvested from the coast of South Korea. The research screened each of the extract of these seaweeds against the gum disease-causing bacteria Prevotella intermedia and Porphyromonas gingivalis. They found that nearly a third of the seaweed extracts - 17 different species - inhibited the growth and colonization of these pathogenic bacteria.
>
>
>
> Extracts from two forms of Sea Lettuce, Enteromorpha linza and Ulva pertusa, along with Sargassum (Sargassum sagamianum) were found to significantly block the growth of both P. intermedia and P. gingivalis.
>
>
>
> They determined that one of the major mechanisms of this inhibitory effect against oral bacteria was their ability to block collagenase enzymes that allow these bacteria to expand and grow.
>
>
>
> The researchers determined that the phenolic content of these seaweeds was the primary component inducing their antibacterial effects. Isolated phenols were then tested directly against the oral bacteria and these proved lethal to the bacteria.
>
>
>
> Oral bacteria are the cause of gum disease. Gingivitis and periodontal disease take place as these bacteria build up in the gumline. As their populations increase, pockets form in the gums. The waste products of these bacteria are also extremely acidic, and they erode the enamel and produce tooth decay.
>
>
>
> **Worse, these bacteria also produce waste streams that can leak through the gums and pollute our blood vessels. This can produce artery disease and significant damage to the heart.**
>
>
>
> This study confirms 2011 research from Korea's CHA University in Kyonggi. In this study, Enteromorpha linza was found to significantly inhibit plaque, gingivitis and bleeding on probing. A mouthwash using the seaweed extract was found to significantly reduce the P. intermedia and P.

# Thread: secret to happiness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ruby — 2012-10-31T13:21:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61052>

Brain scans reveal French monk has 'abnormally large capacity' for joy - thanks to meditation

The scans showed that when meditating on compassion, Ricard's brain produces a level of gamma waves - those linked to consciousness, attention, learning and memory - 'never reported before in the neuroscience literature', neuroscientist Richard Davidson said.

Read more: [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2225634/Is-worlds-happiest-man-Brain-scans-reveal-French-monk-abnormally-large-capacity-joy-meditation.html#ixzz2AsHy3Kgd](http://http://www.dailymail.co.uk/health/article-2225634/Is-worlds-happiest-man-Brain-scans-reveal-French-monk-abnormally-large-capacity-joy-meditation.html#ixzz2AsHy3Kgd)

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T15:08:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61066>

"You can spend time noticing the sensations of the natural process of the breath and if you notice your mind wandering simply bringing it back to the breath. This is known as mindfulness.

You can apply it to other sensations to bring you into the 'now' rather than dwelling on the past or future. Examples are heat and cold and sounds that you hear."

Meditating while doing CT, gotcha. Moving just a bit will bring on that sensation of cold...

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-31T23:02:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61162>

Ricard's book, Happiness, is a great read. And Davidson's work is fabulous... he's shown the effects with the marathon meditators like Ricard, but also even just after 6 weeks (though to a lesser extent). Ricard does NOT define happiness the way most do (it does not depend at all on external conditions) and talks about how we must practice what we want to be good at, including the ability to be joyful.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T23:23:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61167>

There are two things for consideration in understanding happy and how it relates to meditation. (and the incorrect assumption that everyone can/has the ability to meditate and respond spiritually).

1\. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monoamine\_oxidase\_A](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Monoamine_oxidase_A) This is ONE discovery, fairly recent, I'm sure there will be more.

2\. VMAT2 is a vesicular monoamine transporter that regulates the flow of mood-altering chemicals in the brain - commonly refered to as either the god gene or the meditation gene. See this interesting page and video: [http://www.thoughtsinperspective.com/2012/04/funvax-genetic-warfare.html](http://http://www.thoughtsinperspective.com/2012/04/funvax-genetic-warfare.html)

So the expression of these genes that one has are a very large influence on if you are religious, spiritual, meditative etc and recieve the positive brain reactions to those things. Some get it more, some get it less. Meditation only works on those that have a high expression of these.

Shuffle up and deal!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-01T12:22:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61240>

Long-term use of artificial light and the SAD also causes long-lasting reductions in VMAT2 expression/activity. Cocaine has this effect as well.......but here is a funny thing......narcoleptics can not become addicted to cocaine. It becomes impossible. Why? It destroys the Hypocretin neurons in the brain that use VMAT2 most.

### Michael — 2012-11-01T13:47:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61253>

VMAT2 was mentioned in the PLOS study on memory and omega 3s. Pretty important all round, then?

### Michael — 2012-11-01T14:38:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61261>

What's almost disquieting is the thought that there might be certain experiences, certain ways of apprehending the world, that might have been easily available to, say, Australian Aborigines that may be far more elusive for us, not because of currently dominant views in the West of what the world is like, not even because their world had fewer distractions, but simply because their brains were better nourished.

### [email redacted] — 2012-11-01T17:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61296>

> **Jack Kruse;62970 wrote:** Long-term use of artificial light and the SAD also causes long-lasting reductions in VMAT2 expression/activity. Cocaine has this effect as well.......but here is a funny thing......narcoleptics can not become addicted to cocaine. It becomes impossible. Why? It destroys the Hypocretin neurons in the brain that use VMAT2 most.

Since we can't actually count our working HCN's, perhaps I should see if I could get high from Cocaine to "measure" the activity in my HCN's? Narcoleptics can't get high at all right? Thus, if you can get high then there would be a correlation between the high and HCN's?

Does cocaine have legit medical uses? Can one get a "dose" for any non-illegal reasons?

I realized last night, that the long standing pleasure pathways seem to simply lie dormant without use - but they are fully capable of hopping right back on and light up the brain with a passion.

2 cheetoes and a mini reeses cup went in my mouth - it was an experiment really, brought on my smelling that orange pumpkin full of halloween crap. I had sniffed - and BOOM, brain lit up. After having the little amount of the cheetoes and reeses, I thought I was going to have an oxytocin release the pleasure was so deep and strong. It was almost MORE pleasurable to my brain than in the past when I would have that "treat" with regularity.

Thankfully there was no diet coke handy, I'm pretty sure that would have been the final trigger for a full on binge. I love the diet cokes more than anything.

So, this a.m., weight was DOWN, blood sugar was fine, mood was good and I went strait to my Fat only coffee and I have zero hunger and expect to coast through to Noonish before eating anything other than fat.

### nonchalant — 2012-11-01T19:21:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61331>

> **Michael;62991 wrote:** What's almost disquieting is the thought that there might be certain experiences, certain ways of apprehending the world, that might have been easily available to, say, Australian Aborigines that may be far more elusive for us, not because of currently dominant views in the West of what the world is like, not even because their world had fewer distractions, but simply because their brains were better nourished.

I think you may be right about that, Michael. I think our current culture also reflects this.

### Michael — 2012-11-01T20:51:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-61373>

Seeing M. Ricard I was put in mind of an old series - I think it ran on American TV - about Tibetan Buddhism. Anyway, it's on YouTube. It's called "Secrets of the Yogis":

Here's the first part (there are about eight):

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;91Hqkwb2Szc\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=91Hqkwb2Szc\[/video\]\[/video\]

There's some weird stuff - like sequences of movements, a kind of callisthenics, that hasn't been shown in public before, because if you did it wrong it would be dangerous. You'd break an ankle or something.

And those really arduous meditation practices. (How much of this is "Buddhism" and how much shamanic practises that have entered _this form of Buddhism from the Bon religion would be an interesting historical question.)

The effects it has on them are really interesting. I think many people in the West are actually fairly extreme pessimists and don't believe that situations can be changed. These people believe that everything_ can change. And that even includes one's character in their view. Certainly that seems to be an accomplished fact with some of these monks. It's astounding that imprisoned monks actually do seem to have regarded their Chinese torturers with compassion. If you think of the effort some people in the West have expended to "prove" that compassion doesn't actually exist - Friedrich Nietzsche springs to mind - and yet it's reality for these men.

The really old guy who was their most practised meditator reminds you of the strangeness of all this, though. He really doesn't seem to be fully present in the world any more. He believes that he's seen his many past lives here and there ... and in the seven hells. It kind of brings you up short again and makes you ask yourself what you might gain going through this curriculum but also what you might lose.

### Simon Wright — 2020-03-27T09:38:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/secret-to-happiness.4591/post-288037>

Out of interest does any one have any research that sunlight creates VMAT2 expression? I have been able to put my finger on it myself.

However it is noted that people with high VMAT2 expression have less fear than others. I have certainly noticed since regularly sunrise gazing my resilience to fear has been greatly heightened.

thx

Simon

# Thread: question for those who No-Poo or do limited -Poo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 76
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2012-10-31T15:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61069>

So right now I 'Poo my hair 2x/week with wen. I'm trying to cut back to 1x/week. the past couple of weeks I've been washing Sundays (After swimming) and Wednesdays. This week I washed my hair on Sunday. While I needed to wash my hair today I didn't. Instead I rinsed it, and then put some of my home made moisturizer (CO, Cacao Oil, Macadamia Nut oil, and Shea butter) in my hair, dried it then pulled it back into a low pony tail. I figured since it was already oily from my natural oils, an little extra wouldn't hurt!

Tomorrow I'll wash with Wen. and see how it goes. I figure I'll do this for a couple of weeks, then move my wash to Friday, then hold off to wash until Sunday.

I'm not sure I'll every go fully no-poo, but I can manage the 1x/week with the wen.

When you went no-poo how long did it take for you oil gland to slow down? and how do you keep from looking like your hair is a bit dirty (even though it isn't)?

What does your no-poo or limited poo routine look like?

I've got tons of super baby fine hair - so even the littlest bit will make it look dirty. I'm also trying to grow out my hair b/c I've decided longer hair will be easier to deal with vs short hair

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T15:30:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61071>

Wen is loaded with silicones, or was last I checked into it, so it will likely never give your hair a chance to get totally clear of gunk. Silicone attracts dust and dirt particles, which is what makes your hair look dirty and feel weighed-down.

I've just recently gone no-poo again after a long hiatus (I had cut my hair really short and got sold on some styling products that I didn't need and...yeah. I was no-poo for almost a year before that). I use Suave Naturals coconut conditioner every other day and nothing else. On the in-between days, I just wash with water. If you are careful to really massage your scalp all over really well with your fingertips and RINSE RINSE RINSE, water will usually suffice, at least for me. I have baby-fine hair as well. The first time I went no-poo it took about a week to get my hair to a point where I was comfortable with how it looked, but I also had no idea what I was doing. This time around, it's been fine from the get-go...no greasies, no itchies, no frizz...just shiny, silky, pretty hair with much more wave to it.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-31T15:46:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61074>

I found this link... I'm due to buy some more wen soon...but may try this stuff instead since it doesn't have the whole SLS, or silicones...

[http://www.greenwala.com/channels/eco-beauty/blog/22237-Beauty-Changeover-Challenge-Shampoo-Conditioner](http://http://www.greenwala.com/channels/eco-beauty/blog/22237-Beauty-Changeover-Challenge-Shampoo-Conditioner)

You know whats crazy is dd hair is beautiful w/just 1x/we of washing w/wen. I can't imagine what it would would look like if we washed it w/out the silicone stuff...

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T15:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61076>

I've never heard of that stuff. I like the Suave coconut conditioner - easy to find, and super cheap. A big bottle is $1.50 at WalMart. Can't beat it, especially since I need a TON - a whole handful per wash for my shoulder-length hair.

If you're switching from Wen to something else, I'd get a little bottle of SLS-containing shampoo and wash with that a few times before you go truly no-poo. You need the SLS to strip the built-up silicones off your hair. Some people just use baking soda and rinse with ACV and say that gets all the silicones out, but I found that with my thick, super-fine hair, I really did need the SLS shampoo to strip first.

Also, what I do is technically called co-washing or conditioner-washing. I think MamaGrok is straight-up no-poo and she's said that over time her hair became totally self-cleaning.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-31T15:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61077>

I went from WEN to Morrocco Method. It took me at least a week to get past the greasiness. I really like it and it is very natural. I like the Heavenly Chi and the Sea Essence. I do use a very little bit of their cream rinse or conditioner only on my ends. The shampoo works best if you put a small amount in a bottle and and water to dilute it and wash 2x. I do this only 2x a week and I have long thick hair. My hair is looking and feeling more healthy. I also cut according to the lunar chart he has listed. I cut to strengthen or root work.[https://www.morroccomethod.com/shampoos?page=shop.browse&category\_id=42](http://https://www.morroccomethod.com/shampoos?page=shop.browse&category_id=42)

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T17:04:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61087>

I have tried and tried to go no poo - but I always felt it was somewhat dirty looking, and when meeting with clients it made me uncomfortable. I had longer hair, and could pull it back and that worked for the most part (looked like it was products up and slick) but when my hair started falling out this summer - it didn't work! it looked skanky.

So I cut my hair short hoping that would help. It did not, I keep "trying" no poo - but I've given up. I found a natural shampoo at target that had nothing but good stuff!! I'll take a pic of it later. Now I just put a teeny bit at the front/top to handle the greasy looking parts every couple of days, and that's it.

I figured the baking soda route was no different than a tiny amount o poo.

If by some miracle I have more than a couple of weeks without needing to see clients around xmas, I will try again.

We don't use any soaps or other products. I keep my oil mix in the shower for face cleaning makeup when I wear mascara. . the rest of the time the whole body is just rinse. My showers are so short!!!

we've never used soap or shampoo on my son and he's never looked dirty.

I'm sure its a matter of "time", but I just couldn't deal with worrying my clients thought I was skanky!

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-31T17:13:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61090>

I use Loreal cleansing conditioner, and I like it the best of any I've tried.

I've used deva care, curl junkie (awesome but $$$) and V05&suave, the V05&suave are too heavy for my hair, and deva is just so so.

I need a lot of protein in my hair, so I use Sally beauty ion repair (I think...the red and white) as a Nopoo as well.

Using a good Sulfate before you start, and checking your stylers for -cones and whatnot is a good start. Naturally curly.com has a forum with a Nopoo forum...tons of info there too.

### ealachan — 2012-10-31T17:18:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61094>

Mam, were you using baking soda to wash your hair more than once a week? I was overzealous the first time I went no-poo and was doing baking soda and ACV every other day, and it dried my hair out really badly, which made it start breaking and falling out.

Also, for what it's worth, I find that a lot of people who have issues with transitioning to no-poo don't realize that the scalp really needs a good scrubbing and thorough rinsing. It's the friction of your fingertips on your scalp that actually clean it when you're not using harsh chemicals. I think we get so used to just smearing on a harsh shampoo that strips our hair even with very little scrubbing, that we forget to go back to scrubbing / rubbing when the shampoo is gone. I know that's been my main issue with no-poo, anyway. On days when I'm lazy and just kind of give my hair a cursory rinse in the shower, I end up with greasies because I didn't manually break the scalp gunk up and get it all rinsed out.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-31T17:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61096>

I did not do the baking soda thing - I figured it did the same thing as poo, which was signal the scalp more oil needed producing, so I might as well go with a little poo.

Good point on the scrubbing. On my next attempt, I will make efforts to be all scrubby.

### kzimm — 2012-10-31T18:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61102>

Is Dr. Bronner's a no-no?

### FarmersDaughter — 2012-10-31T18:33:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61108>

> **colorado;62821 wrote:** Is Dr. Bronner's a no-no?

It's quite alkaline so it may cause a buildup in your hair...and rinse with a little acv in water (a couple tb/quart) will help reclose the cuticle and prevent biildup. YMMV.

There are good shampoo bars out as well that have super fatted soaps that are quite good for hair. But these too can cause a buildup if you have hard water.

### nonchalant — 2012-10-31T19:24:13+0200

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-31T19:49:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61130>

I have been without shampoo for several months now. My hair has a lot of natural oil (especially as I've been getting healthier!) and I've tried a few things. I think the baking soda with ACV rinse works really well. But I only use about a teaspoon of baking soda in a lot of water, then about 1+ teaspoon of ACV in a lot of water to rinse. Egg yolks also work well (I just use 2 egg yolks witha bit of water or essential oils, rub them into my hair, leave for a few minutes and then rinse out with cool water). Lately I've been using facial mud. A very diluted mixture (about a teaspoon into a quart of water) poured into and rubbed into my hair and scalp (I do this over a big bowl so I can re-pour what drips out)... do this for a couple of minutes, then rinse (lately it's been with a very diluted green tea) and go. All these methods work for me, but I find the mud/clay to be the best. None of them appear to strip my hair and none of them appear to require doing it too often. I love being shampoo and soap free - nothing wrong with shampoo, I just prefer it without.

### freshveggies — 2012-10-31T19:59:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61131>

My Morrocco Method seems like a clay mixture: Here are the ingredients. I have to add a little of this with a lot of water. Even Kombu, Kelp and Nori in it!

Water (Aqua)

Whole Leaf Aloe Vera (Microcystis purifera)

Marine Proteins of Kelp (Macocystis Pyrifera), Nori (Porphyra Laciniata), and Kombu (Laminaria Japonica)

Shea Butter (Vitellaria Paradoxa)

Horsetail (Equisetum)

Nettle (Urtica Dioica)

Yarrow (Achillea Millefolium)

Comfrey (Symphytum)

Cherry Bark (Prunus Seotina)

Fucus (Fucus Vesiculus)

Blood of the Dragon Rare Proteins (Jatropha Dioica)

Cactus (Opuntia Engelmanii)

Green Tea (Thea Sinensis)

French White and Green Healing Clays

Klamath Falls Blue Green Algae (Aphanizomenon flos-aquae)

Crystals from Tibet liquefied into a Crystalline Solution

Essential Oils of Wold Rose (Rosa Acicularis), Moroccan Jasmine (Jasminum), Myrrh (Commiphora Myrrha), Frankincense (Boswellia Carterii), and Mediterranean Olive Oil (Olea Europeae)

Raw Apple Cider Vinegar (Acetic Acid)

92 Trace Minerals and Rare Plant/Marine/Fruit Proteins as Natural Preservatives

### ealachan — 2012-11-01T17:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61295>

I agree. I did oil cleansing method on my face for a while (way before I went paleo) and it really helped a lot. Now I don't even have to do that - I just rub my face with lukewarm water in the shower every morning, then moisturize with a little coconut oil directly after my shower, and I'm good to go. I exfoliate with a tiny bit of baking soda mixed with water once every week and a half to two weeks, depending on if I feel like I need it or not.

Also, I've found that if I wear makeup (which is rare), coconut oil and a bit of tissue takes it right off, quick as you like. It appeals strongly to my deep sense of laziness...

### kathylu — 2012-11-02T05:14:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61487>

Ok girls, my initial thoughts on Wen: It's basically a conditioner with added astringent herbs...no soap, or detergent, but plenty of humectants (attract water) and silicones, which I am sure is responsible for the silky, addictive feel and the detangling action. I also noticed that it has a number of questionable ingredients...the main silicone (amodimethicone) is not even on the GRAS list (generally recognized as safe) of cosmetic ingredients. So, it is pretty loaded with chemicals and preservative agents...not exactly a "natural" product, no matter what their claims are.

I am thinking I will try an initial formulation of a humectant (glycerin), an astringent (aloe vera juice) and a conditioner (my favorite...coconut oil!), lavender essential oil for nice fragrance and possibly a touch of liquid castile soap for a cleansing agent. I know the idea is to get away from soaps, so I'll try a version with and without to compare. There are a number of "tweaks" that can be done, but I think it is better to keep it simple, cheap and natural if possible. Cosmetic preservatives are a nasty business, but if you are making this in reasonably sized batches at home, you won't need a preservative. I'll try to stay away from silicones, but to be honest, the formula may need a touch for the smoothing and detangling action.

Wow, where will I get test subjects? Well, me to start with...maybe hubby and son...look out!

### freshveggies — 2012-11-02T16:19:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-61532>

Also, here are the ingredients of my shampoo. Today I did not use any conditioner and probably won't need to as my hair gets healthier.

[https://www.morroccomethod.com/](http://https://www.morroccomethod.com/)

Water (Aqua)

Whole Leaf Aloe Vera (Microcystis purifera)

Marine Proteins of Kelp (Macocystis Pyrifera), Nori (Porphyra Laciniata), and Kombu (Laminaria Japonica)

Shea Butter (Vitellaria Paradoxa)

Horsetail (Equisetum)

Nettle (Urtica Dioica)

Yarrow (Achillea Millefolium)

Comfrey (Symphytum)

Cherry Bark (Prunus Seotina)

Fucus (Fucus Vesiculus)

Blood of the Dragon Rare Proteins (Jatropha Dioica)

Cactus (Opuntia Engelmanii)

Green Tea (Thea Sinensis)

French White and Green Healing Clays

Klamath Falls Blue Green Algae (Aphanizomenon flos-aquae)

Crystals from Tibet liquefied into a Crystalline Solution

Essential Oils of Wold Rose (Rosa Acicularis), Moroccan Jasmine (Jasminum), Myrrh (Commiphora Myrrha), Frankincense (Boswellia Carterii), and Mediterranean Olive Oil (Olea Europeae)

Raw Apple Cider Vinegar (Acetic Acid)

92 Trace Minerals and Rare Plant/Marine/Fruit Proteins as Natural Preservatives

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-17T18:39:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-64960>

So I'm on week 2 of the seat ripping process using the meiessence shampoo/rinse/conditioner. I noticed earlier this week w/wet hair I had a little wave. So this am I took a shower & just used the rinse & conditioner. I toweled off my hair ran. Comb through it and yes tiger was a little wave. I scrunched it & let my hair dry naturally And yes there's a little wave There's less now that I brushed my hair. Win for no poo

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T18:50:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65161>

Shijin I have two small bottles of the Wen and they're yours for the cost of shipping them if you want them. I hate it ... my hair feels no cleaner than when I used it. It came in my "test tube" subscription from qvc.

Sorry ladies but I shampoo every other day. I used to do it daily because my hair is fine and my scalp used to be oily. If I don't wash it it gets oily and stringy and just plain butt ugly.

If you're worried about chemical components go find some castille soap and wash with that. I found one [here](http://http://www.vitaminshoppe.com/store/en/browse/sku_detail.jsp?id=DR-1003).

Breck shampoo used to be pure castille soap. No additives of any kind until the company got swallowed up by some chemical conglomerate.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T18:53:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65162>

argan oil ... go all the way to the bottom of the page for the lowest amount/price

[http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Products/15715-buy-argan-oil-cosmetic-argan-oil-wholesale-organic.aspx](http://http://www.bulknaturaloils.com/Products/15715-buy-argan-oil-cosmetic-argan-oil-wholesale-organic.aspx)

### Glamazon — 2012-11-18T20:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65180>

I am a no poo kind of girl until the massage therapist gets the oil in my hair. When I am under the shower I spend more time on massaging my scalp with my fingertips than working through my hair. I am post menopausal with jaw length hair and shower almost every day and getting my hair wet at the same time. It seems to work for me. I just thought it weird, for me, to take all the oil out of my hair and then put it back in with artificial moisturizing ingredients.

I get a lot of compliments on the color of my hair (brown with silver gray streaks). I have given up the artificial coloring over a year ago. That was hard to do emotionally. I needed a major attitude adjustment about being pro aging, pro gray.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-18T20:35:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65181>

I just thought it weird, for me, to take all the oil out of my hair and then put it back in with artificial moisturizing ingredients.
>
>
>
> I get a lot of compliments on the color of my hair (brown with silver gray streaks). I have given up the artificial coloring over a year ago. That was hard to do emotionally. I needed a major attitude adjustment about being pro aging, pro gray.

I actually put Cavemam's moisturizer in my hair 1x/week to deep condition my hair btwn that and the meiessence hair products its starting to change I have body in my super fine hair!!! I'm hoping to get to the point that I won't need the shampoo at all just the rinse & conditioner.

I am working on letting go the coloring. I know it's not good for you but my hair is tge one thing I'm vain about. Ppl look at me and thing early-mid 30's. not 39! Though I'm sure the grey might give a level of distinction. I'm just not there yet!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-18T20:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65188>

> **Shijin13;67186 wrote:** I actually put Cavemam's moisturizer in my hair 1x/week to deep condition my hair btwn that and the meiessence hair products its starting to change I have body in my super fine hair!!! I'm hoping to get to the point that I won't need the shampoo at all just the rinse & conditioner.
>
>
>
> I am working on letting go the coloring. I know it's not good for you but my hair is tge one thing I'm vain about. Ppl look at me and thing early-mid 30's. not 39! Though I'm sure the grey might give a level of distinction. I'm just not there yet!!

It's been difficult for me to let go as well. I've had a lot of people tell me I look like I'm in my mid-40's not my mid-60's -- ah vanity.

Last year I went long enough that the loss of color was really predominant. However I have to say here that being (originally) a strawberry blonde has it's advantages. Your hair doesn't really turn gray (according to my stylist) ... it loses color. So mine began showing that strawberry highlight again as it began to lighten.

I caved and colored a few months ago but it's mostly gone now and I am getting compliments galore on my hair (again). I don't get it probably because I'm so used to the (fake) golden blonde color. I had bought another box of whatever it is I used last time but it's been sitting in the linen closet for a couple of months now. I'm not sure I'm going to use it. But it's there if I want it. Guess it's my security blanket.

### Glamazon — 2012-11-18T21:23:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-65197>

> **DarleenMB;67193 wrote:** It's been difficult for me to let go as well. I've had a lot of people tell me I look like I'm in my mid-40's not my mid-60's -- ah vanity.
>
>
>
> Last year I went long enough that the loss of color was really predominant. However I have to say here that being (originally) a strawberry blonde has it's advantages. Your hair doesn't really turn gray (according to my stylist) ... it loses color. So mine began showing that strawberry highlight again as it began to lighten.
>
>
>
> I caved and colored a few months ago but it's mostly gone now and I am getting compliments galore on my hair (again). I don't get it probably because I'm so used to the (fake) golden blonde color. I had bought another box of whatever it is I used last time but it's been sitting in the linen closet for a couple of months now. I'm not sure I'm going to use it. But it's there if I want it. Guess it's my security blanket.

I have a couple of boxes around. Very light blonde. When I did do my hair people thought it was professional because the silver streaks looked so awesome blended in with the blonde.

I resisted being anti aging which i thought meant pro youth. But pro aging is about being the wise and also healthy elder to me now. And frankly, I thought why did I care anymore if men in their 20s didn't find me attractive. The color was more about my vanity and being uncomfortable about getting older. (I'm 55) It took me a harsh year to be comfortable with it. My family does NOT like it.

It was all about changing internal paradigms that were/are deeply entrenched about the youngs and the olds and how I value myself. Back to vanity. Letting my hair grow longer into a new style helped to make the transition more happier. But, growing out hair is a bitch. It made me feel even uglier for awhile. The thinning hair was not helpful either. sigh.

Right now I am adopting a "I am 55 and f\*\*k you attitude," to the world and this horrible culture we live in.

More orgasms please.

### diane — 2013-02-08T21:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-74915>

Reviving this thread - so much great info!

I've been trying the no-poo route. It's now been over a week, and on Sunday I started the baking soda/ACV routine. This is a real test for me, because, well, not to be vain, but I have good hair. The stylists love it because it will hold curl, wave or straight. And it's thick.

The good news is my hair isn't greasy.But it's big. Like volume big. And the ends are a bit dry I think. Am hoping I can find a solution for some sort of CO conditioner? I'm going to ride this out another week to see if it becomes soft like everyone says. It's also much straighter right now - the natural wave and curl isn't coming out quite yet. And I do have quite a bit of it. I think my hair is craving the silicone - it doesn't quite know what to do without it. LOL. Kinda like coming off carbs.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-09T00:58:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-74960>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=74948)
>
> your hair didn't have a wave before?
> does it help to give your hair more body?

No... my hair is/was stick straight unless I take a blow dryer to it, and a ceramic curling iron... and it only stays for about 30mins!

but w/the miessence - shampoo, conditioner, rinse and spray its got body - when I wear it down... of recent I've been wearing it up in a bun (more professional looking) so I can get away w/ no-pooing only on Sundays... awesome! love it!

### kathylu — 2013-02-09T06:09:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-74984>

I'm completely off poo now. Every 5 to 7 days I wash with hot water, then 2 tbsp of baking soda dissolved in 4 oz hot water, rub gently but thoroughly, then finish with a white vinegar rinse (2 tbsp white vinegar in 8 oz hot water). I don't rinse the vinegar out. If my hair starts to look limp before "wash day", I rinse with hot water in the shower, then follow with a vinegar rinse. My hair looks great! More body, a little wave that I never had before and no tangles. It took about a month of "no-poo" to arrive at this point. I'm happy with it.

### caroline — 2013-02-09T08:22:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-75003>

After swimming in the ocean - I haven't rinsed the seawater out - seems like it should be a good idea?

When you rinse with vinegar - can you smell it? I guess I better go back and re read this thread - like there isn't enough to read around here.....

### kathylu — 2013-02-09T14:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-75015>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=75003)
>
> When you rinse with vinegar - can you smell it?

Only when your hair is wet. Later on, no.

### diane — 2013-02-09T16:38:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-75023>

Hmmm. I'm doing 1 tbsp of baking soda in 1c. water. Maybe it's too little? Then 1 tbsp. ACV in 1 c. water. But I do rinse it out. Perhaps some experimentation is due.

My hair doesn't smell bad either as it usually does if I don't wash it. And no vinegar smell either!

### sooperb — 2014-05-02T15:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-124552>

I've recently changed to using just bicarb to "wash" my hair. It certainly leaves my hands feeling soft. I can't air dry because of the way it's cut, it just looks raggy. I have also today not used conditioner, result, blow dryed hair is full of static, I used a dab of hair oil to control it it was so bad. I'll try the vinegar next

### LBK — 2015-09-09T22:27:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-176875>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=65181)
>
> I actually put Cavemam's moisturizer in my hair 1x/week to deep condition my hair btwn that and the meiessence hair products its starting to change I have body in my super fine hair!!! I'm hoping to get to the point that I won't need the shampoo at all just the rinse & conditioner.
>
>
>
> I am working on letting go the coloring. I know it's not good for you but my hair is tge one thing I'm vain about. Ppl look at me and thing early-mid 30's. not 39! Though I'm sure the grey might give a level of distinction. I'm just not there yet!!

I am considering letting the gray grow out as well. I'm almost 52 and started going gray about mid 30's. I'm not even sure if I'm all gray, never wanted to find out. My cosmetologist daughter said gray is "in" and younger women are actually coloring their hair gray. She recommended I just get highlights while I'm growing it out. Might as well give it a try!

### Scompy — 2015-10-05T21:12:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-178663>

Care with some of the treatments out there with apple cider vinegar and baking soda on the scalp...could also cause more hair to fallout more. I always think from the big picture here. Do we wash the hair on the rest of the body like we do our scalps? Why? Why not? Granted, most shampoos are very harsh.

From my reading, the hair also has a 6-week cycle when the hair begins to fall out more at times, and less other times. Thyroid may also play into it, including getting enough selenium (like Brazil nuts), Magnesium (like dark chocolate) and iodine (eat your seafood!).

### Butters — 2016-03-17T20:49:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-191100>

Question is, do we really need to wash our hair with anything?

I try out showering without any stuff. My body is not falling apart. I have sculf now but maybe thats only because my head wants to get rid of the stuff Ive used for 20 years.

### thomas — 2016-04-16T18:36:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-those-who-no-poo-or-do-limited-poo.4592/post-192848>

I have used only water for a few years now. I am still waiting for the apocalypse on my human body.
