### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-09T07:05:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63086>

I wonder if one's sex drive - male or female - is meant to be constant as was implied in earlier posts. Given that we understand our ties to seasonality in eating, light cycles and temperatures and given that the environmental conditions into which a baby is born would likely affect the child's survival (and what the mother was able to provide), wouldn't our libido wax and wane according to the seasons somehow. This is certainly true for other eutherian mammals. This is also perhaps a link as to why some of you are finding that the cold stimulates libido... 9 months later, voila!

For example, this article:

Current Women’s Health Reviews, 2007, 3, 182-189

_Season of Birth Effects on Reproduction in Women_

excerpt 1:

In many parts of the world, the external environment shows pronounced variations with season and therefore the season of birth may contribute to early developmental

processes. This seasonal variation concerns a variety of environmental factors (e.g., photoperiod, climatic parameters, food quality and quantity) and maternal factors (e.g. hormonal and metabolic parameters, infections, behavioural variations). However, most of these variables are highly inter-correlated, leaving it very difficult to ascertain single causative factors influencing early development. At high latitudes, winter, for instance, is associated with factors such as low temperature, short photoperiod together with low levels of ultraviolet radiation, vitamin deficiencies \[12\], high risk of infectious diseases \[13,14\], or increased risk of obstetrical complications \[15\]. Although modern life has considerably diminished the seasonal variation of most of these factors (owing to heating, air condition, artificial light or the year round supply of fresh fruit and vegetables), it has not completely vanished.

excerpt 2:

Numerous studies report that the season of birth influences early development of individuals with long-term consequences for a wide range of parameters such as body height \[e.g., 16, 17\], longevity \[e.g., 18, 19\], susceptibility to diseases \[e.g., 20-22\], as well as several parameters of performance \[e.g., 23, 24\]. Expectedly, those parameters investigated both in the Northern and the Southern hemisphere, exhibited a seasonal variation depending on the month of birth, shifted by half a year between the two hemispheres \[18, 25-27\], whereas no birth month variation was found in equatorial regions where seasons are absent \[28\].

excerpt 3:

Owing to the fact that glucocorticoids are among the key hormones controlling fetal development \[115\], and due to the considerable interplay between the hypothalamus-pituitaryadrenal- (HPA-) and the HPG-axis \[116\], an influence of prenatal stress on reproductive development of the fetus is conceivable. It is well established that the HPG-axis is affected by the HPA-axis, with corticotropin-releasing hormone (CRH) exerting inhibiting effects on gonadotropinreleasing hormone (GnRH) secretion, and glucocorticoids inhibiting gonadotropin and sex steroid secretion \[117\]. In addition, CRH receptors are present in most female reproductive tissues \[118\]. Emerging evidence further indicates that the interaction between the HPA- and HPG-axis may be modified by early environmental conditions \[119\], supporting the view that prenatal stress might be involved in birth season effects on reproductive traits.

### Inger — 2012-11-09T09:11:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63091>

>
> missmatch to me so I need to understand this better - pleeeeeease

Caroline I do guess Jack is right.. I always was a bit on the "guy" side in my thinking, maybe because my brother was my best friend as a kid IDK, maybe it impacted my thinking.

I always enjoy sex. It plain feels so really good...huh!

Now.. the problem I have instead is orgasm. I have never had a problem getting an orgasm by myself, and I at least one have every day in the morning (I can tell you coffee with cardamom as I drink it every morning makes one get quite a strong desire...lol). But... if I am feeling not totally safe with a partner I can forget about it. I will enjoy sex anyways but I will not get an orgasm. I wonder why this is.

I suffered quite a lot in my marriage it beeing so sexless. And I never had an orgasm with hubby.. I was not feeling safe. It is very sad indeed. Now.. one day I am confident, when I am getting more optimal, there will be someone out there to share my life with who has a huge sex desire..lol

The Kruse Protocol have made my libido even stroger. It was never weak but now I sometimes really wish it would not expand further because without partner it is not so fun..

Somehow men sense the womens libido I guess.. and I get quite a lot attention, also from guys in their 20ies too and it is somehow..weird?

### ealachan — 2012-11-09T17:47:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63130>

> **caroline;64929 wrote:** I am so glad you started this thread - I was too chicken!!! I hope Dr. K does a blog about this soon. There is something I don't quite understand - In a consult - Dr. K told me to stop thinking like a woman - and just do it. I get that \[oxytoxin release etc and the big pharmacy in the brain\] but - a guy's switch is always on shall we say but - a woman's not so much. It seems like such a
>
> missmatch to me so I need to understand this better - pleeeeeease

I think I get the "just do it" philosophy better lately when it comes to sex. I used to get myself so psyched out about "omg I really don't feel like doing it right now, I'm tired and this and that and blah blah a million other things", and then I COULDN'T do it because I couldn't shut my brain up. My husband and I actually had a big coming-to-Jeebus session about it because basically it was really hurting his feelings that I was turning down his advances constantly. I decided at that point that I'd try to just shut my head up and be more receptive sometimes...not in the sense of, you know, laying back and thinking about England while he got his kicks type of thing...but just trying to be more open to the idea of having sex and having FUN while doing it, rather than getting freaked out about it...and it really helped a lot. Also, I found that being honest with him, ie: "that feels nice but it's not getting me where I need to go, let's try this instead", has made the whole process SO much less stressful for both of us. He's not sitting there feeling like he's ineffective, and I'm not sitting there reviewing my grocery list because I'm bored....LOL

### curves64 — 2012-11-09T18:16:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63135>

For most of my life I've had a raging libido and I've always received a lot of male attention. It's not that I am the most beautiful woman in the world but at the age of 55 I still easily easily attract the attention of men in all decades of life. I think it's because I love all things sexual and I welcome and enjoy their attention and I'm confident in my sexuality. I'm picky who I date, but not long ago, I had a very handsome 29 year old lover just for the fun of it. The problem is that chronic stress and menopause diminished my sexual energy by half. I could probably "get by" with things as they are but who the hell wants that! My current bf (whom I love and adore) was shocked to learn that my sexual energy was decreased. I'll never forget the look on his face when I told him. It was a combination of sheer disbelief and delight. lol

I'm reading CT6 and trying to further connect all the dots....

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-09T18:41:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63146>

Great topic! I have started & deleted answers, as I'm pretty shy about this stuff, but here goes...

I've always had a strong desire and never had trouble reaching the big O. Heck, I've reached the big O without even touching myself. Planes, cars, beauty salons... But I digress...

Like Inger, when I'm with a partner, it has to be someone I not only trust but also have strong feelings for. I've never been one of those women who can be with someone I don't really care for, so in that sense, my sexuality goes straight out the window. But with someone I care for an trust? Heck, all they have to do is show up for the act, and I'll figure out how to move my body so that it happens. I clearly feel a difference between clitoral O and internal, G-spot O, and I can clearly say, G-spot O is 1000 times better. When I find a guy who can give me both at the same time...well, he's a keeper.

But at 50 years old, I worry that this desire is waning. As is the intensity. Sigh. I've been single now for close to a year, but in my previous relationship, while I could still reach O, it wasn't as strong. I was eating kinda-Paleo (meat & eggs one day, carb-y cr\*p the next). I started epi-Paleo, and in particular started seafood & CT, around June of this year. I feel on some days...oooh, what is that feeling, maybe I should start dating again soon...! And other days, oh well, I can live without. But I do not want to live without, and I refuse to walk the path of the "old lady with a bunch of cats". Plus, I'm highly allergic...

Interesting point about CT increasing sex drive. I, um....can almost reach O while IN the tub. The only reason I don't is 'cause I don't want to clean up all the water that'll get on the floor! LOL. So yes, I can testify that it's helping me.

OK, embarassed girl needs to go hide...

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-09T19:23:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63159>

> **curves64;65005 wrote: Would you say there is a third way as well? I'll call it vaginal friction for lack of a better term. I love the way a man feels inside of me. Is that TMI?
>
>
> No, not TMI....but yes, I definitely agree. It's the reason real men are just....better....than BOB's.
>
>
>
> curves64;65005 wrote:** And PrimalPam, just so I understand, you're saying CT and eating epi-paleo are moving things in the right direction? I've CTed twice in the last 12 hours now after reading Diane's share last night. It's clear what motivates me. lol

For me, it's the combination: I only do CT for 45 mins at a time, 50-52F. In June, I started 5x/week, plus eating completely clean epi-Paleo. Also, I had a Genova nutrition test done by my new doc (yay! She gets it!) and she started me on Vit. D and Vit. B. The combo of the nutrition tweaks via the supplements, plus the seafood about 5x/week, plus the CT 5x/week, was what did it.

In Sept., I started traveling. I went on 5 trips back-to-back-to-back. I skipped a lot of CT, and honestly, I ate what was served, and drank stuff I normally don't drink. And I can absolutely feel a difference. Sex drive is down, weight is up, general 50-year-old woman sluggishness has returned. I'm clearly adrenal fatigued (reading the blogs), and it doesn't go away with just a few months.

I remember Dr. K. answering someone who said, "But I've been doing all this for 10 straight weeks and I don't feel any different!!", and he said something like, "10 weeks? That's all?" LOL. I can't remember exactly how he put it, but we are in such a fix-it-now society, and we just want a pill to make the weight go down, the sex go up, the tiredness to go away. I got his point that day, that, oh, this'll be a long haul type of thing.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63169>

Twenty years ago I read about the Tantric Art of Voluntary Ejaculation. It took me seventeen years of practice to get " good enough" at it, to actually start to claim that I was "good enough" at it. Curiously, I discovered that my fibromyalgia/chronic fatigue greatly reduced after the love making sessions. A year and a half ago I started to go to Accupunture. The results were very similar to the tantra, reduced symtoms. I find the cold tub does the same thing. All three interventions bring me to the same place. All three interventions have led to much improved performance in the gym as well. The cold therapy has blown away the other two in the gym results (more muscle/less fat) though. In addition, I can do an unlimited amount of cold tubbing , and not have to depend on an other human being to "help me out".Also unlike the tantra, cold therapy does not carry the "inherent danger" of "failure' . If I spill water(and I mean actual bath water!) out of the cold tub, its much less of an "issue/failure".Tantra "failure" is always a pleasant "failure", but still less then ideal. The Tub is always "ideal",though obviously never as pleasant!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-09T20:37:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63179>

> **ealachan;64979 wrote:** I think I get the "just do it" philosophy better lately when it comes to sex. I used to get myself so psyched out about "omg I really don't feel like doing it right now, I'm tired and this and that and blah blah a million other things", and then I COULDN'T do it because I couldn't shut my brain up. My husband and I actually had a big coming-to-Jeebus session about it because basically it was really hurting his feelings that I was turning down his advances constantly. I decided at that point that I'd try to just shut my head up and be more receptive sometimes...not in the sense of, you know, laying back and thinking about England while he got his kicks type of thing...
>
>
> I almost sprayed hot coffee all over my screen.
>
>
>
> but just trying to be more open to the idea of having sex and having FUN while doing it, rather than getting freaked out about it...and it really helped a lot. Also, I found that being honest with him, ie: "that feels nice but it's not getting me where I need to go, let's try this instead", has made the whole process SO much less stressful for both of us. He's not sitting there feeling like he's ineffective, and I'm not sitting there reviewing my grocery list because I'm bored....LOL

Lines of communication ... I know that's a big part of my problem. so good for you for having over come that!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-09T20:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63180>

> **PrimalPam;64996 wrote:** Great topic! I have started & deleted answers, as I'm pretty shy about this stuff, but here goes...
>
>
>
> I've always had a strong desire and never had trouble reaching the big O. Heck, I've reached the big O without even touching myself. Planes, cars, beauty salons... But I digress...
>
>
>
> Like Inger, when I'm with a partner, it has to be someone I not only trust but also have strong feelings for. I've never been one of those women who can be with someone I don't really care for, so in that sense, my sexuality goes straight out the window. But with someone I care for an trust? Heck, all they have to do is show up for the act, and I'll figure out how to move my body so that it happens. I clearly feel a difference between clitoral O and internal, G-spot O, and I can clearly say, G-spot O is 1000 times better. When I find a guy who can give me both at the same time...well, he's a keeper.
>
>
>
> But at 50 years old, I worry that this desire is waning. As is the intensity. Sigh. I've been single now for close to a year, but in my previous relationship, while I could still reach O, it wasn't as strong. I was eating kinda-Paleo (meat & eggs one day, carb-y cr\*p the next). I started epi-Paleo, and in particular started seafood & CT, around June of this year. I feel on some days...oooh, what is that feeling, maybe I should start dating again soon...! And other days, oh well, I can live without. But I do not want to live without, and I refuse to walk the path of the "old lady with a bunch of cats". Plus, I'm highly allergic...
>
>
>
> Interesting point about CT increasing sex drive. I, um....can almost reach O while IN the tub. The only reason I don't is 'cause I don't want to clean up all the water that'll get on the floor! LOL. So yes, I can testify that it's helping me.
>
>
>
> OK, embarassed girl needs to go hide...

WOW! For gosh sakes DO NOT GO INTO HIDING that is my trick. THANK YOU for posting this.

And yes on the different orgasms. YES YES YES. I've been to scarified to bring this up so now that you have let's see if I can overcome my near fatal shyness.

Only had a G spot once and I thought the top of my head would come off.

Clitoral has been what kept me going through all those long years after DH ended our sex life (wow .. I didn't get struck by lightening) but it has taken a powder. WHERE"D it GO?

Vaginal had 'em back in the day but never felt them. There's a disconnect between THAT part and my brain.

OK I feel like I just hit the TMI cutoff so better post before I delete.

### [email redacted] — 2012-11-10T01:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63247>

> **diane;65066 wrote:**
>
> In terms of long term relationships - it got me to thinking. Most people will say it's a female issue - but what if it's also related to reduced T levels in the male? Too familiar pheromones? Or maybe it's once the mystery and excitement is lessened and life gets in the way, it's hard to see past the other things you have to deal with in life with your partner and find it hard to see them in a sexual way. Not sure - just theories.

I would never say it's just a female issue. In general, women tend to be more reactive sexually, so it may appear that way.

From what I understand, it's all about keeping the attraction there. And there are things to do to....um, cultivate it, I guess you'd say. Too many get married and think, "OK, now we'll live Happily Ever After" and just think it will be great forever. It's not that easy. My reading has led me to believe that you can work at it and keep the attraction (the "spark") going despite life getting in the way, but it takes some work to get there.

I'm finding that Low T is certainly a factor for me. (Well, more the whole cascade. Pregnenolone steal is one of my issues.) But also finding that working on "creating" increased attraction for both of us has helped.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-11T01:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63489>

Ok I've read through this whole thread and I love it. Great insight into ppl on here

As to the original question asked by the original poster. I'm not peri menopausal but I do have PCOS

Before having kids my sex drive was so/so. After kids, while I was breast feeding I had no interest. W/in 6 wks of doing the LR my libido came back to pre baby levels I added BHRT back in march I've seen some improvements and I've also seen it take a nosedive. When DH & I make a daily effort it gets better but it's no where near where I'd like it to be. But orgasms have gotten way better. I never understood why other women friends would say nipple stimulation during sex = best orgasms ever. Working the past 8 months that switch turned on. OMG that plus clitoral stimulation = best orgasm ever in my life I can't move after one of those boneless bliss

I have discovered that whole BOB can get me there faster its not as enjoyable as manual or oral

As for g-spot stimulation the only time I've O'd that way was when I was in the last trimester of pregnancy.

I do concur w/Jack on sex w/your love and partner who makes you a better person is the best sex ever!

### shah78 — 2012-11-11T16:36:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-63604>

Jack, I've been extolling the virtues of voluntary ejaculation on this thread. I believe (have experienced the phenomenon) that one hundred acts of sex without ejaculation is more beneficial than one hundred sex acts with ejaculation. In fact without ejaculation , there is a lot better chance for hundreds more acts during the year. the so called refractory period is not confined to its short term phenomenon. Ejaculation may release oxytocin which is great, but voluntary ejaculation and ching recirculation with the partner provides an even more powerful hormone surge, a surge which lasts for days/weeks.Early on my wife mentally plotted a "graph" following the number of days without ejaculation(but with plenty of sex) and my mood/energy/libido. Ejaculation deflated the y axis like poping a balloon. I'm almost a different "man" in the pre and post days. Check out Montak Chia's TAOIST SECRETS OF LOVE. Many epi-paleo themes.

### ealachan — 2012-11-13T16:39:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64021>

> **caroline;65935 wrote:** Okay - can we talk?? I need to buy a B.O.B. I had no idea that there was such a bewildering variety of things out there - I know - hard to believe - but there it is. So...any recommendations/personal experiences - things I should know?

It depends on what you need / want it to do. A lot of people like Hitachi Magic Wands, but a) they're expensive, and b) they're actually TOO powerful for some people (me included...yowza!). My personal suggestion is to pick out a couple of inexpensive things that look interesting / useful, and give them a try, then go from there. You'll probably figure out pretty quickly what you like and what you don't, what works and what doesn't. If you're only spending $10-$15 on something to test it out, you're not going to feel as bad if it doesn't work for you than if you'd paid $70 or $80. Just my 2 cents, anyway.

> **ealachan;65974 wrote:** It depends on what you need / want it to do. A lot of people like Hitachi Magic Wands, but a) they're expensive, and b) they're actually TOO powerful for some people (me included...yowza!). My personal suggestion is to pick out a couple of inexpensive things that look interesting / useful, and give them a try, then go from there. You'll probably figure out pretty quickly what you like and what you don't, what works and what doesn't. If you're only spending $10-$15 on something to test it out, you're not going to feel as bad if it doesn't work for you than if you'd paid $70 or $80. Just my 2 cents, anyway.

Thanks for your imput. I did buy quite a small one - paid $60.00 and it just doesn't do it. I am hoping it's just not me!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-14T17:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64262>

> **RiverNeighbor;66242 wrote:** I think maybe you have an inner lady gaga!

LOL well she has been at the top of my playlist for about a year. I LOVE "Bad Kids" because it starts as an homage to Joan Jett replete with overdriven, screaming guitars and vocals and then segues into the most frothy, pink, sweet pop confection EVER. Euryhthmics predates it with "Better to have lost in love" ... pounding drums (check) heavy rhythm guitar (check) and just when you think you can't take anymore the song lifts off from the earth and just flies away on the most heavenly soaring sound track. I love contrast.

then there's all the late 70's funk ... Rick James YOWZA, Wild Cherry, "Play that funky music" (just to grab some quick ones off my iTunes)

and don't forget Def Leopard "Pour some sugar" ... juvenile yes but it makes me bounce off the walls.

Bon Jovi ... Aerosmith ... all that old Southern Fried Rock ... the list is darn near endless.

### Inger — 2012-11-15T13:29:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64445>

I am feeling lost in this thread.. I have absolutely no desire for any sex toys or anything. They just never intrested me one bit.

To me they are like compairing a real diamond to a fake plastic diamond. Toys have no scent.. they are cold, dead.. they are not beautiful like a real penis. Like the eyes, the body.. I need the real feeling, I need to taste, smell, feel.. I need the person behind. To me sex is connected with that always.. I just cannot separate it from something "spiritual" I mean something alive that has a spirit/mind. because at that very moment it is dead to me.

I am feeling weird. I wonder if I need to work on that or if it is okay. When I read about all this here I start to wonder if there is anything wrong with me..

But I agree with that music can be very erotic.. that I agree with 100%..

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-15T13:46:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64450>

no.......nothing wrong with you. Your instincts are correct but sex toys Inger are much like a supplement. Real food is always better but when your faced with bad quality food or your trying to reverse illness you will use all the "toys" in your tool bag to get the job done. I believe that is how most of the people on this thread are looking at sex toys. I think sex toys are exercise for the cortisol DHEA oxytocin melatonin axis reconstruction in our brain. When you learn how to slowly re build it then you must exercise it. Not everyone has easy access to a sexual partner when they are trying to heal themselves. It is at this point getting a toy makes a ton of sense. Also several people here are using circadian biology principles to re build their neural circuits so the timing of the oxytocin release is critical. Here a partner may not be available due to job, life, or circumstance. So a toy could help heal.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-15T18:59:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64515>

Inger ... you gotta do what you gotta do. If you're ok going long stretches without anything then good on you.

I'm going to go out on a limb here and reveal something so personal it makes me want to cry just contemplating it. I've been married for (counting fingers) 37 years. for about the last 30 of those our marriage has been sexless. And I'm not talking about that "once every few months bs." I mean No. Sex.

I love my husband dearly. I can't imagine my life without him.

But 30+ years without that close contact? please.

As I said. You gotta do what you gotta do. I didn't sign on to live like a nun. But I did it for all those years. Not any more. My purple buddy is a poor substitute for the real thing but an orgasm is an orgasm no matter how you get there.

(pats self on back for being so damned bold)

I can hardly tear myself away from my new love as the feel of his body against mine is so incredibly delicious. He's 6'6" and I'm 5"3" and we crack up that we're going to make a giant baby bjorn (front baby carrier) so I can hang out with him while he's working. It's silly, but we laugh about it all the time. If we were to introduce toys, it would be in "addition to," and not a "substitute for" him. I believe there is no "right" or "wrong." The only thing that matters is to align with your own intuition and inner guidance and let it lead the way.

> As I said. You gotta do what you gotta do. I didn't sign on to live like a nun. But I did it for all those years. Not any more. My purple buddy is a poor substitute for the real thing but an orgasm is an orgasm no matter how you get there.
>
>
>
> (pats self on back for being so damned bold)

Darleen!!! My heart bleeds when reading your post above..

I have gone through exactly the same. But for 13 years only. It was a tiny bit different.. hubby do wanted sex once in a while, but it was always a 2 minute quickie and mostly when he had a bad hangover after drinking for days.. it felt humiliating at times.. like he used me only as a tool to feel better. He did not cared to touch me eather.. even if I asked for it not only once. He complained my butt is too fat.. I was only good enough when I weighed 20 pounds less then now in this pictures;

(Pic from this morning.. I think I am not skinny in any way and I think I have gained some this fall, but I am not feeling too fat eather and I think I look ok for someone not exercising or calorie-restricting at all. He do not liking my body have hurted me in a way I struggle still today to come over.. I am so confused and it hurts deep inside. Remember I am a former bulemic too with body issues so it certainly did not helped to have a hubby like that. I felt the only thing he liked about me was my personality..really.)

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

...

Darleen.. I had a lover. For one year. That is where I experienced how amazing it felt when someone loves your body.. loves to touch it.. enjoy it.. loves you as a whole person. I thought I was in heaven. I really was. Now, I gave that up too because of commitment. I thought I had to be committed to hubby because I once promised so as we got married (this is something I got from my parents and their religiousity). I married mostly because of fear. I was only 22 yo and had no clue about life. Makes me cry to think about all this.

I cannot believe you have had to go through this Darleen. I so do not wish it for anyone.. it hurts so much so one gets totally crushed.. and that is when I started to see the beauty in even the smallest things.. I had to, to survive. That really saved my life and kept me sane. So I count that as a blessing, somthing I learned through the suffering.

I just want to hug you right now...

### caroline — 2012-11-16T12:46:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64694>

Inger - you are a stunning, beautiful woman. Thank God you had a wonderful lover - it scares me to think where you may have ended up. You have a whole wonderful life to look forward to - it is just around the corner.

Your husband must have been such a mean spirited, horrible person and he impacted on you in so many terrible ways - but you have come out the other end such a strong and loving and confident young woman. He has lost so much in so many ways and you have gained so much more. xoxo

### Inger — 2012-11-16T14:26:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64704>

Caroline... what you write makes me cry..............

I realize I have such a sick brain. What if I am the mean one and I am just not getting it right and that I should manage to repair hubbys damage.. (he was in prison for 20 years in countries like Russia and Hungary and got tortured there too) what if it is all my fault? I am getting so freaking confused at times!!!! He says he loves me.. but he throw me out this spring because of another woman he said it directly in my face.. but what? Now he denies it and says there was nothing? And I do not even care.. I was never that kind of a women that demands total faithfulness.. if there is, I am very happy but if not I am all for talking trough the issues and then forget about it. I am not even really thinking about such stuff. All these lies.. they make me crazy.

Now he wants me to be hes wife again (without sex..) and he says he loves me..(but says my butt is too fat?) but when I try to talk about the issues to gain understanding he just gets irritated and sometimes mad. For sure he offers his help too at times like driving to the shop or twice he washed the dishes too when I worked the whole day but it is not healthy for me I guess. I asked him to sleep in another room because he often plays games on the computer late at night and it helped some because I need good sleep and it was making me sleep not so well.

I just cannot throw him out he has nowhere to go right now but I am waiting for him to find an own place. I thought I was safe here by my parents but it is my own fault letting him in. But he asked me cause he lost his appartement. I told him, ok just as friends. I just feel so cruel writing this. I feel cruel. I have lost my istincts quite a bit in this matter. That is where I fail terribly.. like they have somehow gotten destroyed during all these years. I honestly do not understand. There is never all bad eather.

But I am slowly starting to think my ability to see the beauty in everything maybe served me not well in this particular issue.. I fooled myself to believe it was allright and it was not. Maybe.

I need more understanding. I need patience. I need wisdom. OMG I need it all so much. There is so much; what if it is all my fault... like I am commited to let him into my life.. like.. I just need to love more.. (I do slowly realize love can and sometimes should be tough love too.. )

I am sorry I did not wanted to rant. I am ashamed to share this. Because I have clearly failed so much in my life.

But you know what.

There is a tiny part of me that is so calm. I think it might be the healthy spot..lol It just knows what to do.. I just need to listen and not let my emotional feelings take over. It is such a tiny spot yet but it has crazy powers for sure.. huh!

When I am alone I can feel that. I feel just so happy. Like.. even if it is tough.. I got my wings again... and then I feel just one with the universe and all feels so good inside

What I think is, I need to be alone to heal. It wan't work otherwise, I am too broken. When once healed I can decide what to do because then I will know.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-16T14:46:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64705>

Inger as I read your reply I can see you are on a precipice your instinct is so natural you know what to do yet my perspective of your situation is that you might be allowing Neolithic though about marriage/relationships could the correct decision for YOU. There is nothing mean or wrong w/doing what is good for you. You own your health. Now own your relationship if you don't want what your in close the door & walk away you are the most important thing in your life. No one else should take that from you in any form. Remember fear is an indication your ready to embrace something new. I'm cheering for you you have to be at peace with everything in your life for your soul to truly heal.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-16T15:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64714>

Inger normally I am not paternalistic on the forum.......take it from somebody who's brain is pretty good........get him out of your life and regain your sanity as soon as possible. The people you allow to invade your life can make it.......or ruin it. This is why this forum can be a place where people bloom. It contains the fertilizer, soil, and water for rebirth of health. The people here care about one another and how they do even though they may have never touched one another. Here, we touch with words and our emotions via those words.

These relationships matter deeply to those who are trying to change because the people you spend time with shape who you are and who you become. Behavior and beliefs are contagious: you easily “catch” the emotional state of your friends and family, imitate their actions, and absorb their values as your own. If their ideas are counter productive to your health you must do something about it. If your friends are the types of people who get stuff done and remain healthy with good choices, **chances are you’ll be that way, too**.

The fastest way to change yourself is to hang out with people who are already the way you want to be. I hope this helps Inger. Good luck to you and your family. They are lucky to have a person like you who cares deeply for them, but they will drag you down if you let it happen. You have the control. Gretchen was trying to say just that to you. I want to make it crystal clear to you here in this post. I went through the same thing with my own family. It was harder to get them to adapt than it was for me to do it, so many of them I cut from my life. It was hard to do, but now 7 years later I know I was right. I have changed dramatically while they still wallow in their own despair because they cant get out of their own way.

### Inger — 2012-11-16T15:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64724>

> **Jack Kruse;66716 wrote:** Inger normally I am not paternalistic on the forum.......take it from somebody who's brain is pretty good........

Jack.... I cannot thank you enough for your response. Your whole response above and your advice. I appreciate it more then you can possibly imaging Jack.

I can see the fruits of your life. Your beautiful loving marriage.

I can see the fruits of my moms life.. she says we just need to be loving, no matter what the other does. I tried that 13 years long. It did not changed anything. I see the fruits of her life.. I do not want such a marriage myself ever.

I need to say nothing more...

@ Anna.. thank you too.

Wow.. It is getting crystal clear to me now what to do. You all give me the courage to look under the stones.. what is there? to move them so the water can flow.. the beautiful well.. it needs to flow for sure...

Tank you all so much...!

### vkiernan — 2012-11-16T16:18:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64731>

Inger,

Sorry, I know you have gotten a lot of responses but I felt compelled. This is not to garner sympathy from anyone but merely to hope that you see yourself and can get out of what you are in. I was married to a man who didn't drink but he worked - constantly or was always out working on a project of his in his workshop. Birthdays were never celebrated with him for the most part. He was always working. He also never had much of anything nice to say. Glass was always half empty and still is. It took finding the right man to get me out of that situation which wasn't the right way to go about it. Like you, I never thought I was good enough. How could I? He would never tell me anything nice. I was never overweight except after my third child but he never thought I was thin and that was weighing 120. Sex was also the way you described it. Never had an O with this man and he never really tried even after being married for 20 years. Like you said, 2 min. Very, very sad indeed and also very selfish.

One last story on him, this is how his messed up brain thought: When my third baby was born, I had a varicose vein break in the vaginal area and they had to stitch me up. So after the couple days in the hospital and we go home, that very day my husband helped his godsend move who has 3 brothers of his own that could have helped. Keep in mind, I just got home from the hospital, with stitches, with brand new baby, with 2 other small children at home. What the hell was he thinking? Nothing, that is how it always was!!!!! He always cared how much everyone on the outside thought of him versus his own family and doing right by them.

You need to do yourself the biggest favor and GET HIM OUT OF YOUR LIFE!. You are the good person here and he doesn't deserve you nor do you deserve to be treated this way. Somehow, you have to realize that you are kind and generous and beautiful and deserve someone that treats you like a princess cuz you treat them like a king. It should work both ways and one sided isn't going to bring you to optimal. Look at how much you are stressing over this. Why should you feel sorry for someone who is causing you harm in one way or another. This is what I did my whole 1st marriage. His parents died when he was 18 and I "always" felt sorry for him. I think that is why I married him in the first place.

Have you read my journal? Do you see how messed up I am and HOW LONG it is taking me to get better? I don't blame myself so much as I blame him and the stress and guilt he placed on me and our children. He is still messing with them after all these years too. I only wish that I would have left him when the kids were little. But, didn't work that ways so onward and upward.

You have no ties to this man anymore. If he could say to you to get out and then come back and say it was nothing means he is all about himself and will never be anything different. You gave him a huge chance at having you back and he is blowing it. And the fact that he tells you your butt is too big is just messed up!!! If you get him out of your life you may actually help him. He might have to realize his mistakes and clean up his life. He may or may not do this but it is his choice and he needs to make it. You are enabling him by letting him stay there. What lesson has he learned??? Since he came back and has not really done right by you in terms of making you feel loved and beautiful, then what is the point??? What are YOU gaining by letting him live there? I was a pretty strict catholic too and I tell you I am still dealing with this internally but I know that I did the right thing in the end regardless what my religion said. My kids now see what a relationship is supposed to be like. They now have another side to the story. I am slowly getting better.

Inger, I think you are so loving and kind and truly deserve someone to treat you that way too. Don't settle for less. You are on your way to optimal. I wish you were here so I could give you a great big hug and continue reinforcing what a gentle and kind person you truly are.

Wow.. Vkiernan. That was a strong post. That you shared it with me.. thank you. THANK YOU.

You know.. I did not even managed to leave hubby when I had my lover.. He was the love of my life and I am sure, thinking about it later, he was waiting for me this whole year. He gave lots of hints how much I ment to him. But I was not believeng one second I was good enough for him. He was successful in his career, had a very good job and a balanced life, he was into health too.. he was always eating fish and taking cold showers.. I did not know anything about CT and Epipaleo back then.

But I was this lost girl that had nothing. Now I have no one to cling to.. but I will manage it alone. I know I will. There is just no other choise.

IDK I am just failing words that tells what I feel about you all. What you are giving me.

Huh.. I am feeling a bit overwhelmed to say the truth..

### curves64 — 2012-11-16T19:17:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64759>

> ....
>

>

> When I am alone I can feel that. I feel just so happy. Like.. even if it is tough.. I got my wings again... and then I feel just one with the universe and all feels so good inside
>

Inger, nurture your little calm voice. It is the voice of your internal guidance and it knows precisely what to do. Even if you find yourself in a negative situation, you have the power and ability to create a beautiful life for yourself. In my first marriage, my husband was a cocaine addict. For six long years, I didn't get any of the things I needed in relationship, yet I felt sorry for him!! In hindsight, I can see that he was just reflecting back to me the negative feelings I had about myself. Once I started connecting with my own little voice, it got louder and clearer and the people I started attracting changed too. Today, the people I bring into my life reflect back to me the love I feel for them. It's a 180 from where I had been.

### curves64 — 2012-11-16T19:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64761>

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-11-16T19:47:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64764>

> **Inger;66747 wrote:** Now I have no one to cling to..

You have Inger! Inger, whose intuition (not the voices of parents, grandparents, church, media) seeks to preserve herself. If you need to cling to something, cling to that.

Inger, not only do I have an ex-husband, I also have an ex-family. A few years ago I cut my parents off completely from my life (not on a whim.. due to abusive behavior). At that time the scripts kept running in my head about how children are supposed to view/treat parents. My friends at the time were not on my side and told me I was a bad person for not being forgiving/accepting. Extended family wrote me asking me to apologize for being a bad child and to stop being a toxic family member. I was already having health problems at that time and some of them were telling me that it was punishment for treating my parents so badly. I cut them all off, including the scripts in my head.

My last correspondence with my grandmother before she passed away was telling her that I wouldn't corrrespond with her she kept sending me letters about reconciling with my parents. It was a hard period. I listened to myself though and was determined to move beyond the people and ideas that were hurting me. Sure, there are painful memories now, but I don't live in the past, I live now. I have a wonderful husband, new friends, awesome kids, and most of all..... I have no regrets.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-16T20:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64770>

That is how I live to RN. Be present for now.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-16T21:51:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64789>

"There is no sense clinging to the rock that is falling." Allan Watts

My teachers have told me it is exactly when there is nothing to cling to, and no one to rescue you, that you learn what you need to be free. They are painful lessons, but they have sustained me.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-16T22:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64804>

> **KiwiLauren;66791 wrote:** "There is no sense clinging to the rock that is falling." Allan Watts
>
>
>
> My teachers have told me it is exactly when there is nothing to cling to, and no one to rescue you, that you learn what you need to be free. They are painful lessons, but they have sustained me.

Which has reminded me of a dream I once had (which I later found to be archetypal). I stood on a high peak and could hear someone urging me to jump. Man it was a loooong way down. I sensed the approach of someone from behind me. Don't be afraid -- you'll fly. they kindly gave me an assist and

I flew.

Whenever I have been ready to test my wings, the teacher has appeared. I may not have known I was ready, I may not have THOUGHT I was ready, but that little shove off the edge always proves I can fly.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-16T23:03:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64805>

> **Inger;66691 wrote:** Darleen!!! My heart bleeds when reading your post above..
>
> I have gone through exactly the same. But for 13 years only. It was a tiny bit different.. hubby do wanted sex once in a while, but it was always a 2 minute quickie and mostly when he had a bad hangover after drinking for days.. it felt humiliating at times.. like he used me only as a tool to feel better. He did not cared to touch me eather.. even if I asked for it not only once. He complained my butt is too fat.. I was only good enough when I weighed 20 pounds less then now in this pictures;
>
> (Pic from this morning.. I think I am not skinny in any way and I think I have gained some this fall, but I am not feeling too fat eather and I think I look ok for someone not exercising or calorie-restricting at all. He do not liking my body have hurted me in a way I struggle still today to come over.. I am so confused and it hurts deep inside. Remember I am a former bulemic too with body issues so it certainly did not helped to have a hubby like that. I felt the only thing he liked about me was my personality..really.)
>
>
>
> Darleen.. I had a lover. For one year. That is where I experienced how amazing it felt when someone loves your body.. loves to touch it.. enjoy it.. loves you as a whole person. I thought I was in heaven. I really was. Now, I gave that up too because of commitment. I thought I had to be committed to hubby because I once promised so as we got married (this is something I got from my parents and their religiousity). I married mostly because of fear. I was only 22 yo and had no clue about life. Makes me cry to think about all this.
>
>
>
> I cannot believe you have had to go through this Darleen. I so do not wish it for anyone.. it hurts so much so one gets totally crushed.. and that is when I started to see the beauty in even the smallest things.. I had to, to survive. That really saved my life and kept me sane. So I count that as a blessing, somthing I learned through the suffering.
>
> I just want to hug you right now...

I have had to give this some thought. yes, I was hurt for many many years and, like you, was told it was my fault because I was fat. Well that was about 60 fewer pounds ago back when, looking back, I was a bit overweight but not fat by any stretch of the imagination. My self esteem, which was already way low, simply cratered.

I tried for years to figure out what I had done wrong. Because in my life and my experience if anything went awry it was always "my fault." At least that's what my family told me and I believed them. What else could I do?

I thought that if I could figure out what I had done wrong I could fix things. It never occurred to me the problem was not with me.

There were two occasions when I came within a hair's breadth of leaving. I even told him I was going to go. He promised to change and even went to a couple of counseling sessions with me.

What it has taken is time. and retirement. His job was about 99% of the problem. The other 1% is he is pretty much incapable of intimate communication (and I mean TALKING about personal issues). You think I'm bad? I'm a chatterbox compared to him.

The only thing that has kept me going is that I love him and I know he loves me. He tells me so daily. In all other things he has been supportive to a crazy degree.

I'll tell you something else. I "divorced" my family a very very long time ago as they were incredibly toxic. I made my own family. You can do the same thing. Find friends who are loyal and trustworthy and who will love you for who you are and who you can love in return. Your family is how you got here but there is no law that says you have to stay with them.

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-17T19:26:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64963>

This thread is now 20 pages long, so it's obviously touched off nerves in so many of us. "Finding Optimal" (hey that could be a movie title!) is not only about physical health, but emotional health as well.

Like Dr. K. said, we easily adopt our families and friends attitudes. I've been super lucky in that, while I have many family/friends who have these negative, Eeyore-type attitudes, my own father is the most optimistic, "lemonade out of lemons" guy in the world. Through the years, I've chosen only to "listen" to him. When I'm alone and facing a crisis, I think....WWDD....(What would Dad Do?). I have just had to turn off about 90% of the others in my family/friends circle because going to a negative place always seems to be the easiest thing to do for people.

I'm 50 years old and a triple divorcee. Never had kids. But I'm always smiling, laughing, and generally happy. When people ask me how I can be so happy, even though I'm so alone, I tell them that if I had had a crystal ball at age 20 and someone told me that at age 50, I'd be triple divorced and childless, I'd have put my wrists out then to be slit. But as the years have gone on, I've constantly moved the people out of my life who have been negative influences. Each time I meet someone new, I assess whether they have negative energy or positive energy. It's very simple, really. If I feel any negative energy whatsoever, I just....don't return their calls anymore.

Unfortunately, negative men seem to be attracted to me. They hate their job? Then they love me, because they feel I'll listen all night. Hate their ex-wives? Great, Pam will listen. Hate their families? Awesome, let me call Pam.

So I wonder when or how I'll ever find someone else with positive energy, who has their sh\*t together, who cares about their health. Is that asking so much?

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-17T19:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-64965>

Also, re: my "lemonade out of lemons" father: My father has had quad bypass and multiple stents yet, at 78 years old, still works. It saddens me so: 20 years ago, he had quad bypass surgery, and he immediately (literally, the day after surgery) started following his doctor's advice: LF diet and 5 days of cardio/week. Guess what....he's had multiple stents since then, even recently had another heart attack. Yet his doctor is still G-d to him. He listens to me about Epi-Paleo but can't seem to "get" that whole wheat pasta and LF cheese isn't where it's at. He'll eat extra fish if I say it's good for him, but he won't do anything contra to his doctor's advice. He credits his cardiologist and cardiac surgeon with saving his life, which technically they have, so it's a hard battle for me with him, as he heeds their advice as it's the law.

It so saddens me when I read Dr. K's blog post (can't remember which one) about heart disease eventually affecting the kidneys, as that's what's happening right now with my dad. His kidneys are beginning to fail, and I'm so sick about the fact that he's been given this terrible nutritional advice all these years.

Off topic, but I'm venting as it's T-giving and I'm giving thanks he's still here...

### caroline — 2012-11-17T22:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-65005>

Hi Pam - I so totally agree about the negative energy. For a number of years now - if someone gives off negative energy I run the other way. It has such a horrible effect on me - I just shut down. I always try to be positive and happy and won't allow anyone to do that to me anymore. It is interesting tho - when I use the words negative or positive energy to some people they think it is woo woo.

I wish lots of wonderful things for you and what could be more positive than this forum and JK.

I also wish good things for your Dad - but it sounds like he is going in the wrong direction - unfortunately - I have been there.

You must be such a blessing to your Dad. xo

Â And yes, if you don't know the tune it is exactly what you think it's about. Â Which led me to thinking about how men in rock use their (ahem) instruments to pound out the sex while women (when they aren't being timid, coy little woodland nymphs) just flat out tell you what's on their naughty little minds.

Which then led to me thinking about Redbook magazine. Â Yeah, seriously. Â Because a few years ago I happened to see an article listed on the cover of an issue that caught my attention so I bought it. Â  And there I am perusing the brownie recipes and the house cleaning tips and the the next thing that caught my eye (how could it not) was an article on how to give mind-blowing blowjobs. Â Yes indeedy. Â Today's Redbook is definitely NOT my Mama's version. Â After I picked my jaw up off the floor I promptly sent in my subscription and have made sure not to let it lapse. Â I now get it on my iPad. Â Gotta love those tech toys.

Which today has led me this ["let's talk about sex, baby,"](http://http://www.huffingtonpost.com/dr-logan-levkoff/female-masturbation-sex-for-one_b_2213593.html)Â in-your-face, let's-not-be-shy-about-it post.

Anna, with all due respect for the research efforts you put in, allow me to say .... horsefeathers.

Men are such damned know-it-alls but when it comes to women they are pretty much clueless. Â Hence the debate that has raged for-seemingly-ever as to whether women even HAVE orgasms. Â Kind of goes hand in hand with the debate the Church had for centuries as to whether or not we even had souls.

Men.

I'm halfway through the book "Sex at Dawn" and have to say I love their snarky, juvenile humor because this topic can be mightily uncomfortable for even the most "enlightened" folks. Â I have reached the chapter on "female hysteria" and if it weren't so sad it would be hilarious. Â Did you know the fifith electrical appliance invented was a vibrator? Â Do you know why? Â Seems the medical profession had gotten tired of masturbating their women patients. Â Poor guys.

I do so love this particular paragraph:

> But perhaps even more dumbfounding than their silence on being paid to provoke _nervous paroxysms like so many Chippendale's studs is the fact that these same medical authorities managed to maintain the conviction that female sexuality was a weak and reluctant thing.
>
> _

_
Oh and at the same time these guys were providing extramarital orgasms to their patients they were also telling them that they should not masturbate themselves_ to orgasm because it would cause the demise of Western civilization. Â So pardon me if I take ANYthing a man should try and tell me about my body and how and when I have an orgasm with about a pound and a half of salt.

Having said all that I will also say that I only have my own experience to draw on and it tells me these chuckleheads are full of it. Â First of all if there is a "root" to the clitoris, which to me implies some sort of dendritic nerve system they should have been able to easily find it. Â Secondly if all three "types" of orgasms are part and parcel of the same thing then one would expect they would have elements in common. Â Not in my experience.

Frankly I would think their time would be better served trying to figure out to pleasure a woman rather than how it all works. Â Who CARES how it works as long as it does. Â In addition I find this kind of research suspect and put it on the shelf right along with men who deliver babies and peer up women's nether parts for a living. Â Really? Â You know better than a woman how to give birth, do you. Â 

/derisive laughter

the older I get the less patience I have for this nonsense.

Now that I've unloaded all THAT I will say I have already paid a price for deciding to be so forthright. Â Bad dreams, much weeping and shame in those dreams. Â this is not easy for me as I'm sure it isn't for others. Â But total optimal health requires all parts to be in healthy, working order. Â At least, that's my story for now and I'm stickin' to it.

That means a lot of stimulation is then necessary for women to achieve orgasms compare to a man,Â  while some women haveÂ  much easier time getting an orgasm then others.Â 

Â 

I always wonder why this is so.Â  I kinda of research the topic and realize that the Brain is practically our largest sex organ.Â  You have to be into the act to be in the best possible state to achieve orgasms,Â  more so for women since it takes more than just one switch to be turn on.Â  Weather you love the person you are with or whatever else needs to be there(music, candles, etc.) to get you more tune to the mood.

Healthy diet I think ties to all of this.Â  Our brain controls everything.Â  I keep forgetting that sexual pleasure is a system that can also be broken just like others when not eating healthy.Â Â  All this time many people including myself thought we were eating healthy because of what we are told by our parents growing up, common doctors, advertising, etc, and really we are just damaging ourselves even further.Â  Making it harder to do things since we continue to eat unhealthy and without realizing it.

Â 

It wasnt until I found about Sean Croxton in Underground Wellness and later Jack Kruse along with some other health specialist that open my eyes and how far I was from eating optimal and how BROKEN my body is.Â 

Â 

I feel that a woman's sexual mechanism of getting pleasure is more complex and more sensitive,Â  meaning much easier to damage when not on a healthy diet, exercise.Â Â 

Â 

Sex is supposed to be something that we should enjoy all the way to our senior years,Â  things will decrease as with age but not to the extent that some people are experiencing.Â  I mean I keep hearing that about half the population of women in many anonymous survey say they never had an orgasm.Â  Needing a bunch of mechanical toys but only to some that had no issues using mechanical toys to get sexual pleasure.

Â 

I remember reading a couple of articles while I was in college about some people living with mother nature,Â  had no problems with their sexual drive, even women who were elderly.Â Â 

Â 

I have to keep in my mind that sexual pleasure is what also makes us human and it could become broken.Â  Once broken, healing it takes a bunch of time,Â  as with many other things I am finding as I continue to research on health.Â Â  It is kind of funny that I mentioned this to my last girlfriend since she was having the problems that I mentioned when it comes to achieving orgasm (she never had one).Â  She passed it off as I was reading something from some "quack" doctor.Â  I mean after all she was taught differently just like I was.Â  Most folks I come in contact just dont have an open mind into trying to solve their problems,Â  health and sex being two of them.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-06T21:38:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-66621>

I started reading one of the books I bought on Tantric sex. Â Not too far in yet but it's -- interesting. Â Pretty much finished "Sex at Dawn." Â Seems about 40% is reference. Â I was so looking forward to more.

ANYhoo.

I shove info into my head and let it simmer. Eventually I get an AHA! moment when things clarify or new connections are made. Â It happened this afternoon. Â See ... I told you that "just because I wander doesn't mean I'm lost." Â I wasn't kidding.

anyone here remember that Pointer Sisters hit from eons ago "Slow Hand?" Â It's one the band I sang with covered which is probably why it floated to the top. Â Here's the chorus:

> I want a man with a slow handÂ 
> I want a lover with an easy touchÂ 
> I want somebody who will spend some timeÂ 
> Not come and go in a heated rushÂ 
> I want somebody who will understandÂ 
> When it comes to love I want a slow hand

Everything I've been reading and thinking about coalesced when I thought of those lyrics. Â THAT'S what's missing. Â And I'm not blaming anyone but myself. Â Recall I said a while back that deep down I thought I was afraid of being touched. Â So while the light is dawning I recall a passage from THE GAME OF THRONES (the book not the HBO show) where the young princess (who has been abused horribly in all ways by her older brother) is married off to the "king" of an uncivilized horde. Â They are horse people and rather than try and break her he spends a day and a night gentling her. Â I remember when I read it I thought "well that's how it should be, right?"

It isn't easy facing my fears. Â And really REALLY not easy making my deepest thoughts public like this. Â But doing so seems to be (for some strange reason) not only hastening my healing but actually encouraging it. Â Who'd a thunk it.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-06T11:49:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-69588>

We are all our own biggest enemy..........once you realize it and overcome it life become easy. This community we have built here really is designed to teach you that foundational lesson. The difficulty lies not so much in developing new ideas as in escaping from old ones. Your thinking has to evolve to accomplish this. When you feel badly at the beach or on vacation think of this question? What thinking brought you this body? Then follow it up this one.........
So what do you do when the life you thought you had no longer works for you any longer?

Every mistake we make in our own health creates an opportunity for a healthy recovery. We cant just live our life, we must create it and live that result.

Evaluate the people and things in your life and then demote, or promote or terminate. Ultimately you are the CEO of your life. Sometimes the wrong choices bring us to the right places. I am thankful to all the physicians I saw that said I could not reverse my obesity. It was because of them that I took it upon myself and found a way to do it myself.

Embrace your now, you never know where it will bring you unless you live it. This is what I was driving home above to Darleen.
When YOU know better about you.........regardless of what any of the experts say to you or how the belittle you........YOU will do better.

There is solace in knowing you better than the rest of the world.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T12:06:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-69591>

> **Jack Kruse said**
> When YOU know better about you.........regardless of what any of the experts say to you or how the belittle you........YOU will do better.
> There is solace in knowing you better than the rest of the world.

Well said! That is exactly what has been driving me all these years. On the surface it was despair and anger about my weight Underneath I knew everything the docs were telling me was wrong. Why? Because I am the one living in this body. I knew deep down something was amiss but I didn't know what it was or how to figure it out.

That's when the Universe started throwing things at me. I learned very quickly to sit up and pay attention. When information appears to leap out at you from nowhere it's best to open your eyes, your mind, your heart and listen up. It's how I "found" how messed up my hormones were and how to start fixing them. It's how I ultimately found my way here.

My motto? Question everything and everybody. Don't stop even when you think you have the final answer. Because you don't.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-14T15:38:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lets-talk-about-sex-baby-lets-talk-about-you-and-me-lets-talk-about-sex.4744/post-105629>

>

> My wife gets the same thing - she has a very high libido and gets alot of attention from men half her age as well. I think alot of it has to do with confidence and health. If you recall from the sex webinar Jack talked about movement (quality of movement?) being a huge visual stimulus. Perhaps improved health and confidence translates into attractive movement. She also has a large hip to waist ratio which I think has a lot to do with the attention . . . . also (golden ratios/fibonacci proportions) mentioned in the webinar.

It absolutely does. Movement is tied to the electric potential of collagen. When collagen is in supreme shape movement is top of the line. Remember that collagen is the most common protein in our body and it is hydrophilic. It is also designed to be surrounded by water. When water touches a hydrophilic protein it undergoes phase transition to it fourth phase of water. It arranges itself in hexagonal planes or sheets. This allows it to do some amazing things. The most important one is charge separation to electrify collagen. When is unelectrified it forms a gelatin glob and has no triple helix protein confirmation. When it becomes electrified it form a triple helix with amino groups of hydroxyproline in the center of the collagen. In this collagen nanotube water becomes confined to a tight space below 1.4 nm to form the fourth phase of water. The water closest to the collagen becomes more negative due to the separation of oxygen and proton of hydrogen. When water separates it charges it becomes a de facto battery. The collagen becomes a wire embedded in the charges and conducts the current. The more current it transfers the better movement happens and the pleasing it is to the eye.

> [http://www.port.ac.uk/uopnews/2012/11/07/science-throws-cold-water-over-sports-theory/](http://http://www.port.ac.uk/uopnews/2012/11/07/science-throws-cold-water-over-sports-theory/)

The only thing I know about the matter is that my son, who's into weight lifting, has a drastic decrease in muscle pain on the days that he sits in an ice bath afterwards. It's not directly after, but 10-12 hours after.

### Dali Dula — 2012-11-09T15:57:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-baths-dont-work.4750/post-63114>

I am not a competitive athlete, but as a very active 52 yr old this does not correlate with my personal experience. CT is impressive.

### kathylu — 2012-11-09T16:16:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-baths-dont-work.4750/post-63117>

The article repeatedly mentioned the "possible threat to one's health" and the "potential dangers" of cold water immersion, but none of the threats or dangers were explained. Also, what sketchy details there were indicated the subjects were standing in cold water (up to ankles, knees, thighs, what?) for 10 minutes or sitting (again, how much immersion?) for 2 minutes. What temperature? Not exactly CT as we practice it. Lots of apparent bias in the experimental design....

### [email redacted] — 2012-11-09T16:37:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-baths-dont-work.4750/post-63118>

### shilohman — 2012-11-09T16:39:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-baths-dont-work.4750/post-63119>

Lets put some context to this story. I am 62 yrs old with bad knees (my right knee has torn ACL, been that way for over 30 yrs). Playing basketball has always been a passion of mine. Before I started CT (started in March of 2012) I could play a game of full court basketball. Afterwards, I always would have pain in both knees, but mostly my right (I do wear braces on both knees, couldn't play at all without the braces). When I first started CT there wasn't a whole lot of effect on the pain in my knees. But as I became cold adapted the effect of CT on my pain has been remarkable. Now I can play full court BB with 20 yr olds and I have more energy than they do and the pain in my knees is very negligible. Within my context, I experienced similar results as the cited study while I was warm adapted. But once I became cold adapted, CT had a major effect on pain reduction.

The researchers need to read Dr. K's blogs on CT.

As Martin would say, "that's my story and I'm sticking to it".

### PrimalPam — 2012-11-09T20:43:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ice-baths-dont-work.4750/post-63182>

This article only talks about "cold plunges" immediately after a sporting event. How long a cold plunge? 10 minutes? 15? Hardly enough to make a difference.

Dr. K. has written, time & again, that the effects of cold are cumulative, and that length of time + frequency = results. 10 minutes a couple of times a week hardly comes close to that equation.

My money's on Dr. K.'s protocol, which has worked well for me. I don't think I'm even an N=1 anymore, as so many other people here have said the same thing.

I hate inflammatory articles like this with no data to backup. Sorry.
